title: 12V Battery Maintenance and Warranty for New Vehicle Inventory
raw_content: Hybrid Electric Vehicles equipped with 12V Lithium batteries should refer to TSB 25-EE-007H for inspection and inventory maintenance information relevant to those models. The 12V battery in a stored vehicle is subject to conditions that can reduce its performance and life. Regular monthly battery maintenance must be performed by the dealer to ensure proper operation and optimal battery life for the customer. A 12V battery that is not adequately maintained during dealer storage can result in reduced battery life and customer dissatisfaction. As a matter of warranty policy (2.3.5), Hyundai does not provide warranty coverage for discharged and/or failed batteries due to lack of dealer maintenance. Dealers should inspect batteries within 7 days of arrival from Hyundai. Warranty claims can only be submitted for repairs performed within 7 days of unit delivery at dealers. Claims can only be submitted using the approved testing methods outlined in 25-EE-003H. It is the dealer’s responsibility to maintain the 12V battery in good condition while the vehicle is in dealer stock. Maintaining the battery in good condition will minimize the dealer’s battery replacement cost since dealer stock is not covered by Hyundai warranty. The regular upkeep of the battery will also ensure optimal battery performance for the customer. Section 2 Receipt, Inspection and Storage of New Vehicles 35 The following TSBs contain battery tester information and warranty related procedures. – 24-EE-009H – 12V BATTERY HOLD WTC REQUIREMENT – 24-EE-014H – 12V BATTERY MAINTENANCE FOR INVENTORY & PDI – 24-EE-013H – WEBDCS BLUELINK SOC ALERTS – 25-EE-003H – GUIDE TO BATTERY DIAGNOSTICS TSBS Note: The TSBs listed above are subject to change; for the most up-to-date version(s) please visit www.HyundaiDealer.com under the Service tab/Service Programs/Hyundai Tech Info/TSB. Dealer Battery Maintenance Schedule (per TSB 24-EE-014H) Note : Hyundai highly recommends storing dealer stock vehicles in “Shipping Mode” to help reduce battery current draw and conserve Battery State of Charge (SOC%) while vehicles are in dealer lot or storage location. For more information on Shipping Mode, please review TSB 24-EE-012H-1. Dealer Option - Covered by Warranty: Within 7 days of vehicle delivery to dealer: Inspect the battery condition using Battery Test/Claim App on GDS Smart, referencing TSB 25-EE-003H. Warranty claim submission must use the approved process described in the TSB. Take the appropriate action based on the test results: Battery Condition Action D00 or Good Battery (Cadex) No action required (covered under PDI) D01: Parasitic Draw Detected (non-sleep communications modules) D02: Parasitic Draw Detected (Vehicle Device Short) D03: Vehicle in ACC mode depleting battery D04: Vehicle not operated often or Charge & Retest (Cadex) Notes: • D02 tests for inventory units may be submitted to PCR as this is an edge case condition. This code is normally not warrantable for retailed units. • D03/D04 is not warrantable. Diagnose, repair the specified Battery Condition and utilize either of the following methods to charge the battery: - Run at engine idle with the headlights ON or use battery charger; charge time will be dependent upon battery condition and vehicle model Note: Turning the headlights ON induces maximum charging rate of the battery. - Charging can be conducted by automatic regulated battery charger. Hyundai recommends DCA8000, Associated Intellimatic or Schumacher DSR124 chargers. - Check the battery again after charging. Battery test result must yield, “Good Battery”. Warranty claim submission notes: - Save the GDS Battery Diagnosis test result for your records. The results should be uploaded to the server automatically. Section 2 Receipt, Inspection and Storage of New Vehicles 36 - For QX Venue, the Cadex tool per TSB 25 - EE - 005H, two warranty codes from the battery test result printout below must be submitted with the claim using GDS Smart: o Initial “Charge & Retest” result before charging. This test is sent to servers automatically o “Good Battery” or “Replace Battery” result printout from DCA-8000 after charging must be scanned and submitted to HMA using Battery Test/Claim App. R01/R02/R03/R04 or Replace Battery (Cadex) Replace Battery - Save the GDS Battery Diagnosis test result for your records. The results should be uploaded to the server automatically. - Retain the battery for WTC collections per 24- EE-009H Aged Inventory Maintenance Alerts: Units requiring Aged Inventory Maintenance can be viewed within WebDCS – Aged Inventory – BlueLink Alerts. The 12V batteries in dealer inventory must be maintained in reference to TSB 24-EE-013H WEB DCS BLUELINK SOC ALERTS. All battery warranty claims will be reviewed against Aged Maintenance history for approval. Vehicle delivery to the customer: Set the vehicle to “Customer Mode”. See TSB 24-EE-012H-1 – VEHICLE SHIPPING MODE for more details. Note: Certain functions will be inoperative if the vehicle is driven while in Shipping Mode. Dealer Required - Not Covered by Warranty: Dealer stock unit battery maintenance for every 30 days (monthly), every 90 days (3 months), and in conjunction with WEBDCS Aged inventory - Bluelink SOC Alerts (TSB 24-EE-013H). – Check the battery condition with Hyundai’s required tools: Battery Condition Action Good Battery No action required. Section 2 Receipt, Inspection and Storage of New Vehicles 37 Charge & Retest Use either option “A” or “B” below to charge the battery. A. Engine Idle with Headlights ON B. Automatic Regulated Battery Charger Charging time will depend on the battery state of charge (SOC) and vehicle model. After charging, check the battery again with a Hyundai approved battery tester and utilize the Battery SOC information on TSB 24-EE-013H . Battery test results must yield, “Good Battery” Refer to TSB 24-EE-014H and TSB 24-EE-013H for more details. Replace Battery Replace Battery at Dealer’s expense Please note: The TSBs listed above are subject to change; for the most updated version(s), please visit www.HyundaiDealer.com under the Service Tab/Service Programs/Hyundai Tech Info/TSB. Vehicle delivery to the customer: 1. Turn ON the power fuse switch in the fuse panel if it was turned off. If vehicle is equipped with the Quiescent/Dark Current Control system, switch it to minimize “Customer Mode”. See TSB 24-EE-012H-1 – VEHICLE SHIPPING MODE for more details. Note: There will be a warning on the instrument cluster if the vehicle is driven with the Power Fuse Switch turned off.
coverage_details: Warranty claims for battery repairs or replacements are covered only if performed within 7 days of vehicle delivery to the dealer.
Battery inspection within 7 days of vehicle delivery to the dealer using the Battery Test/Claim App on GDS Smart (referencing TSB 25-EE-003H).
No action required if the battery test result within 7 days is D00 or 'Good Battery' (covered under PDI).
Diagnosis and repair of 'D01: Parasitic Draw Detected (non-sleep communications modules)' conditions identified within 7 days of vehicle delivery.
Submission of D02 parasitic draw test results for inventory units to PCR as an edge case condition.
Battery replacement if the initial test within 7 days yields 'Replace Battery' (R01/R02/R03/R04 Cadex result).
Approved battery charging methods (engine idle with headlights ON or automatic regulated battery charger) for warrantable conditions identified within the 7-day window.
exclusions: Hyundai does not provide warranty coverage for discharged and/or failed batteries due to lack of dealer maintenance.
Dealer stock (inventory) is generally not covered by Hyundai warranty for routine maintenance.
Battery conditions D03 ('Vehicle in ACC mode depleting battery') and D04 ('Vehicle not operated often') are not warrantable.
Periodic dealer stock unit battery maintenance (monthly, quarterly, or in conjunction with BlueLink SOC Alerts) is not covered by warranty.
Battery replacement identified during periodic maintenance after the initial 7-day window is at the dealer's expense.

title: Moisture and Condensation Control
raw_content: Windows, ventilators and heaters should be in a closed position. Should moisture condense in the vehicle, moisture absorbing agents can be installed. This procedure should be especially adhered to in extremely hot or humid locations.
coverage_details: Windows, ventilators, and heaters should be in a closed position.
Installation of moisture absorbing agents if moisture condenses in the vehicle.
Adherence to moisture control procedures, especially in extremely hot or humid locations.

title: New Vehicle Preparation and Delivery - Aged Inventory Maintenance
raw_content: To help ensure engine protection, the vehicle should be started and run at engine idled with the headlights ON for 20 minutes (or directed by the latest Aged Inventory Maintenance TSB), until either normal operating temperature is reached or required 12V battery maintenance time is achieved to preserve battery life. This procedure should be performed once a month at minimum. While performing this procedure, the Dealer should check fluid levels and potential leakage, unusual noises and/or erratic engine operation, operation of the gear shift lever, the brake pedal, the clutch pedal, the parking brake lever, and also briefly activate the A/C system in fresh air mode to confirm normal operation. The vehicle should be moved a minimum of one car length (12 inches if there are capacity issues) to allow lubrication of parts and to prevent tire flat spots. In cold weather climates, the cooling system should be checked for proper anti- freeze protection. Section 3 New Vehicle Preparation and Delivery 38 Section 3: New Vehicle Preparation and Delivery
coverage_details: Engine protection for aged inventory vehicles
Preservation of 12V battery life for aged inventory vehicles

title: Pre-Delivery Inspection (PDI) Requirement
raw_content: A proper and complete Pre-Delivery Inspection (PDI) is a central element in developing owner confidence in both the Dealer and HMA. A proper PDI minimizes shop comebacks for adjustments and builds owner loyalty while having a direct impact on customer satisfaction experienced during the vital first days of ownership. This inspection is required for each new vehicle as provisioned in the Dealer Service and Sales Agreement (Section 11).
dealer_actions: Perform a proper and complete Pre-Delivery Inspection (PDI) for each new vehicle
Ensure PDI is conducted as provisioned in the Dealer Service and Sales Agreement (Section 11)

title: New Vehicle Pre-Delivery Inspection (PDI)
raw_content: The following are the dealer’s responsibilities relative to performance of the Pre-Delivery Inspection (PDI): • The responsibility for performing PDI work must be assigned to qualified Dealership personnel with a valid Hyundai Service ID and password. Every step on the Hyundai Dealer PDI App - Pre-Delivery Inspection (PDI) is to be performed. • New car preparation should be scheduled far enough ahead of customer delivery in order to avoid any delay caused by required repairs. Coordinate delivery time promises with other shop work being scheduled. • The Hyundai Dealer PDI App is to be used during the course of performing PDI on each new vehicle. The App lists the inspections and other work which must be performed during PDI. • Any warrantable repair performed during the Pre-Inspection must be submitted as a separate warranty repair via a warranty claim. • Each new vehicle is to be road-tested at the proper point during the pre-delivery sequence. • The Shop Foreman, Lead Tech, or Service Manager is required to carefully review each new vehicle PDI inspection and certify that the services performed are satisfactory by digitally signing the Pre-Delivery Inspection form at the end of each inspection on the PDI App. • The retail selling dealer must perform the Pre-Delivery Inspection for the first retail purchaser. In the case of a transferred unit, the retail selling dealer may not be the one that performed the PDI. The new owner’s lasting impression may well be determined from the first few miles of driving. • The PDI must be performed prior to vehicle delivery to the first retail owner or placed as a demo. Claims with PDI performed after Retail Delivery Date (RDR) or Demo date will not be accepted by HMA. • The PDI must be performed prior to vehicle accumulating 250 miles. • The PDI for IONIQ branded vehicles (e.g. IONIQ 5, IONIQ 6), must be performed by IONIQ certified dealers. Non-IONIQ certified dealers are not authorized to perform PDIs on IONIQ branded vehicles. Section 3 New Vehicle Preparation and Delivery 39

Any warrantable repair identified during the pre-inspection is considered a separate warranty repair.

title: New Vehicle Preparation and Delivery
raw_content: The Hyundai Dealer PDI Application (the ‘HMA PDI App’) is to be utilized in all Pre-Delivery Inspections. The Hyundai Dealer PDI App automatically uses the latest PDI model-specific checklist. It is critical for all inspectors to properly perform the PDI inspection and record any vehicle quality issues directly on the app with supporting documentation. Failure to properly document these items may result in additional claim review and/or disapproval of claim submission. All PDI submissions must be completed through the HMA PDI Application on the tablet. The PDI ‘paper’ form is not acceptable method for PDI submission. If a technical issue occurs on the PDI Application, contact GITA Technical Support Center for resolution. In addition, check for updated PDI related communications: • Within the PDI Application • Hyundai Tech Info (HTI) Service Communication • TechNet Times Publications (HTI) Section 3 New Vehicle Preparation and Delivery 40

raw_content: Reimbursement for the Pre-Delivery Inspection of the vehicle will be at a labor time in effect as of the date the PDI claim is submitted via WebDCS. Credit will be calculated in the amount of the pre-delivery allowances as specified for each model and year multiplied by your current Dealer approved Warranty Labor Rate. These PDI claims are automatically generated once a user submits the inspection. Failure to complete all sections of the PDI inspection will result in the claim not being automatically generated. If a PDI claim does not automatically generate, there is likely an issue with the inspection and it must be rechecked to ensure all sections are complete and all items are green. For any issues relating to PDI claims, please contact the WCC by using the Warranty Support Portal in WebDCS or by phone at 1-877-446-2922. Section 3 New Vehicle Preparation and Delivery 41

title: Vehicle Delivery Presentation
raw_content: Proper delivery presentation of a vehicle to the customer has a profound impact on the owner’s perception of both the Dealer and the product. A proper delivery presentation will improve customer satisfaction and minimize customer complaints and/or unnecessary inquiries.
coverage_details: Improved owner’s perception of the Dealer and the product
Improved customer satisfaction
Minimized customer complaints
Minimized unnecessary inquiries
dealer_actions: Perform a proper vehicle delivery presentation to the customer

title: Dealer Responsibilities for Vehicle Delivery
raw_content: The following are Dealer’s responsibilities related to the delivery of the vehicle: • The customer must be given an Owner’s Manual, Owner’s Handbook and Owner’s Handbook Supplement at the delivery presentation, as well as any other applicable documents. • A road test should be conducted to demonstrate the operation of the vehicle, as well as to familiarize the customer with the instrumentation and “Key Delivery Features” identified in the Customer Delivery Checklist. • If a road test is not possible, special emphasis needs to be placed during the delivery presentation on at least these “Key Delivery Features”. These features need to be demonstrated to the customer and also shown where within the Quick Reference Guide they can find further information related to these key items. • The Customer should be given an explanation of HMA’s recommended maintenance intervals, as well as the required preventive maintenance with the aid of the maintenance schedule printed on the back cover of the Quick Reference Guide or “QRG”. • Introduce the owner to Service Management and familiarize them with the service procedures and operating hours.
dealer_actions: Provide customer with an Owner’s Manual, Owner’s Handbook, Owner’s Handbook Supplement, and any other applicable documents at delivery.
Conduct a road test to demonstrate vehicle operation and familiarize the customer with instrumentation and “Key Delivery Features” from the Customer Delivery Checklist.

If a road test is not possible, emphasize and demonstrate “Key Delivery Features” during the delivery presentation, showing the customer where to find further information in the Quick Reference Guide.
Explain HMA’s recommended maintenance intervals and required preventive maintenance using the schedule in the Quick Reference Guide (QRG).
Introduce the owner to Service Management and familiarize them with service procedures and operating hours.

title: Warranty Start Date Registration and Corrections
raw_content: Immediately upon delivery of a vehicle to a retail customer or the vehicle being put into Dealer demonstrator service, a record of the retail delivery or demonstrator service must be transmitted to HMA via the Dealer Communications System, so as to properly register the Warranty start date of the vehicle. The retail delivery date is the date the vehicle was delivered to the customer and the demo service date is the date the vehicle was put into demo service, if applicable. Vehicles must be registered as a retail sale or demonstrator before accumulating 500 miles. Any vehicle that has accumulated 500 miles or more is considered to have been placed in service, and therefore the date that the vehicle was placed in service must be accurately reported. A vehicle’s retail delivery date (or if a Dealer Demonstrator, the demo service date) is considered the Warranty start date (i.e. the date recorded as the warranty coverage start date). In order to automatically change or correct an incorrect Retail Delivery Registration (RDR): Section 3 New Vehicle Preparation and Delivery 42 • The incorrect Retail Delivery Registration (RDR) submission must be canceled within 15 days of initial registration with HMA. • The correct resubmitted (subsequent) Retail Delivery Registration requires that the vehicle’s actual mileage be 500 miles or less (if the vehicle’s actual mileage exceeds 500 miles, the original retail delivery date from the previous RDR is retained.) Note: If the vehicle was initially registered as a Dealer demonstrator, the demo service date remains the same as the Warranty start date. Policy Statements 43 Policy Statements

title: Warranty Period and Transferability
raw_content: The warranty period begins on the date of retail sale or date of first use, or if the vehicle is first placed in service as a “Demonstrator” or “Company” car prior to delivery, on the date it is first placed in service, whichever occurs first. If the vehicle is first placed in service as a “Demonstrator” or “Company” car as previously described and subsequently sold at retail, the original selling dealer must inform the owner of the applicable warranty start date (i.e., the date it was first placed in service). The date of first use is recorded per the Dealer’s input of the Retail Delivery Registration (RDR) information via the Dealer Communications System (DCS). The time and mileage limits are detailed in each limited warranty statement. In the event that the vehicle is sold by the original owner while still within the warranty period, the unexpired portion of the Hyundai warranty will be transferred to each subsequent owner. Hyundai Motor America Warranty coverage (excluding 10 year/100,000 mile Powertrain Limited Warranty) applies to the vehicle, regardless of a change in the ownership and is transferable to subsequent owners. title: Magnuson–Moss Warranty Act Compliance and Hyundai's Limited Warranty
raw_content: With the passage of the Magnuson–Moss Warranty Act (Public Law 93–637) (the “Act”) in 1975, Congress granted the Federal Trade Commission the authority to establish rules and regulations aimed at encouraging both manufacturers and Dealers to improve the consumer’s understanding of warranties. Hyundai Motor America encourages all of its Dealers to become thoroughly familiar with the provisions of the Act. The Act does not require that a supplier give a warranty, nor does it stipulate that any specific coverage must be provided, but strictly regulates warranties that are given in writing. Further, the Act stipulates that any written warranty, as defined by the Act, must meet certain disclosure requirements. Provided the warranty meets certain substantive requirements, it may be called a “Full (Statement of Duration) Warranty”. These requirements include: (a) the repairs must be made in a reasonable time and be effective within a reasonable number of repair visits and (b) implied warranties may not be limited, and no charge can be made to the customer for warranty repairs. The language used in these warranties must be simplified so that they can be easily understood. A written warranty which does not meet these requirements must be labeled as a “Limited Warranty”. Hyundai’s vehicle warranty is a limited warranty which complies with the Act. As such, Hyundai’s warranty limits the duration of implied warranties to the duration of the expressed warranty. Under the Act, after December 31, 1976, all retailers of customer goods costing more than $5.00 are required to make available to consumers and prospective customers the written text of the warranty prior to sale. Hyundai Motor America has made available to all Dealers an Owner’s Handbook. The Owner’s Handbook must be displayed prominently in the Dealer’s new vehicle showroom. To fulfill the Dealer’s obligation to advise the customer of the written warranty on the vehicle, Dealers should point out the warranty statement in the Owner’s Handbook to the customer at the time of purchase (i.e., just before the customer signs the buyer’s order). In addition, all purchasers of Hyundai vehicles will be provided a statement of the warranty Policy Statements 45 coverage through the Owner’s Handbook supplied with each new vehicle. Dealers must provide this Owner’s Handbook to the customer at the time of vehicle delivery. Note: This warranty coverage information must also be available for review by prospective consumers. The provisions of the Act also apply to parts and accessories for cars and light trucks. Hyundai Motor America provides a warranty to retail customers on Hyundai Motor America supplied parts and accessories sold to them. Dealers are obligated under the Act to convey this warranty to the purchaser and prospective purchasers. All purchasers or prospective purchasers of Hyundai parts and/or accessories must be provided with a copy of the Hyundai Service Parts and Accessories Limited Warranty.
coverage_details: Hyundai’s vehicle warranty is a limited warranty which complies with the Magnuson–Moss Warranty Act.
Hyundai’s warranty limits the duration of implied warranties to the duration of the expressed warranty.
A statement of the warranty coverage is provided through the Owner’s Handbook supplied with each new vehicle.
Hyundai Motor America provides a warranty to retail customers on Hyundai Motor America supplied parts and accessories sold to them.

title: Warranty Applicability and Exclusions
raw_content: The New Vehicle Limited, Powertrain Limited, and Anti-Perforation Limited warranties apply to vehicles manufactured to United States specifications which are distributed by Hyundai Motor America, and registered and normally operated in the 50 United States and Washington, D.C. The Federal Emissions Defect and Emissions Performance Warranties also apply to vehicles registered in the 50 United States and Washington, D.C., the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands, Guam, and American Samoa, including the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands. Contact the Hyundai Customer Care Center at 1-800-633–5151 prior to performing such a warrantable repair. Force Majeure (“Acts of God”) Exclusions HMA will not be liable for any breach of this warranty caused by or resulting from prohibition or restriction by law or regulation of any government, fire, flood, storms, earthquake, epidemic, war, strike, lockout or other labor troubles, accident, riot, Acts of God or other events beyond a party’s control. Sublet Paint Claim Submission Limits: • The sublet type on all paint claims is “P1” - Paint. • Paint sublet amount over 100% of the LTS time allowance, even if under $500.00 - is not to exceed 150% of the LTS. To calculate the allowance, multiply your labor rate by the total number of paint labor op hours listed on your claim. Then multiply that number by 1.5. This gives you the maximum sublet reimbursable amount. • All paint labor operations performed on the same day must be listed on one claim. – Include paint labor operations for panels that require blending • Material Allowance is calculated into the Paint Labor Op.

are warranted in normal service, only when the replacement is the result of a defect in material or factory workmanship, for 12 months from the date of original retail delivery, or date of first use, or 12,000 miles, whichever occurs first, or up to the first scheduled maintenance replacement interval. • Normal deterioration or wear of any part such as: – Spark plugs – Worn brake pads/linings – Worn clutch linings – Filters – Worn wiper blades – Bulbs and fuses – Other wear and consumable items • Any vehicle where the odometer mileage has been altered. • Any vehicle that has been scrapped and a certificate of salvage has been issued for the vehicle. • Damage or failure resulting from: – Negligence of proper maintenance as required in the Owner’s Manual. – Misuse, abuse, accident, theft or fire. – Use of improper or insufficient fuel, fluids or lubricants. – Use of parts other than Hyundai Genuine Parts or parts of equivalent quality and design. – Any devices and/or accessories not supplied by HMA. – Modifications, alterations, tampering, or improper repair. – Parts or accessories used in applications for which they were not designed or not approved by HMA. – Slight irregularities not recognized as affecting quality or function of the vehicle or parts, such as slight noise or vibration, or items considered characteristic of the vehicle. – Tires (See separate tire manufacturer’s warranty). Some states do not allow limitations on how long an implied warranty lasts, or the exclusions or limitation of incidental or consequential damage, so the limitation or exclusions set forth regarding this limited warranty may not apply to the owner. The owner may also have other rights which vary from state to state. Slight irregularities not recognized as affecting quality or function, such as slight noise or vibration, or items considered characteristic of the vehicle.
Tires (covered by separate tire manufacturer’s warranty).
Damage resulting from airborne “fallout”, salt, hail, windstorms, or other “Acts of God.”
Damage from action of road elements (sand, gravel, dust, or road debris) resulting in stone chipping of paint or glass.
Paint scratches, dents, or similar paint or body damage.
Parts or glass broken during removal as part of a repair.
The charge for diagnostic labor which does not lead to the determination that a warrantable condition exists.
Incidental or consequential damages, including loss of time, inconvenience, loss of vehicle use, or commercial loss.
Duration of any implied warranties (including merchantability and fitness for a particular purpose) is limited to the duration of this limited warranty.

title: Speedometer/Odometer Replacement and Recalibration Policy
raw_content: If a speedometer fails within the Hyundai Basic Warranty period of 60 months/60,000 miles, HMA’s policy is to recalibrate the new speedometer with the vehicle mileage at the time of failure replacement. Many states now require that only preset speedometer/odometers be installed. A copy of the repair order and purchase order must be sent with the new speedometer or I/P cluster to the following HMA recommended vendor: Palo Alto Speedometer, Inc. Model Electronics, Inc. 718 Emerson Street 615 East Crescent Avenue Palo Alto, CA 94301 Ramsey, NJ 07446 Phone: (650) 323-0243 Phone: (800) 433-9657 Email: info@paspeedo.com Email: cmcgraw@modelelectronics.com www.paspeedo.com www.modelelectronics.com Dealers may use their own qualified vendors to recalibrate the new speedometer or I/P cluster. The recalibration final invoice must be attached to the warranty claim. Turn around time is between one and three days, depending upon the speedometer. Warranty Claim Submission Information • Submit as a normal warranty claim • Use the recalibrated mileage on the claim • Use Op 94210R00 Speedometer Assy or Op 94002R00 Instrumental Cluster Assy • Charge out the replacement part and list the speedometer/instrument cluster as the causal part • Input the calibration charge in sublet using the C2 Instrument Cluster Calibration • Include shipping charges and attach final invoices to the claim in WebDCS • Submit any applicable rental charges on a separate rental claim BF Goodrich Tire: 800-521-9796 Bridgestone Tire: 866-775-6480 Continental Tire: 800-847-3349 Dunlop Tire: 800-321-2136 Hankook Tire: 800-426-8252 (West) 800-426-5665 (East) Kumho Tire: 800-445-8646 Michelin Tire: 800-847-3435 Policy Statements 52 Vehicles equipped with Instrument Cluster Module / GDS tablet odometer mileage writing: For vehicles equipped with separate Instrument Cluster Module and odometer mileage capability through GDS tablet, the dealer must perform the odometer mileage writing process with the GDS tablet. Software verification is required prior to the odometer mileage writing. Please refer to TSB 25-BE-008H or latest version for additional information. Policy Statements 53
coverage_details: Speedometer failure within the Hyundai Basic Warranty period (60 months/60,000 miles).
Recalibration of a new speedometer/I/P cluster with the vehicle mileage at the time of failure replacement.
Use of HMA recommended vendors (Palo Alto Speedometer, Inc. or Model Electronics, Inc.) or dealer's own qualified vendors for recalibration.
Inclusion of shipping charges in the warranty claim.
Submission of applicable rental charges on a separate rental claim.
For vehicles with Instrument Cluster Module / GDS tablet, odometer mileage writing capability through GDS tablet is covered.
dealer_actions: Recalibrate the new speedometer with the vehicle mileage at the time of failure replacement.
If using HMA recommended vendors, send a copy of the repair order and purchase order with the new speedometer or I/P cluster to Palo Alto Speedometer, Inc. or Model Electronics, Inc.
If using dealer's own qualified vendors, attach the recalibration final invoice to the warranty claim.
Submit recalibration as a normal warranty claim.
Use the recalibrated mileage on the claim.
Use Op Code 94210R00 for Speedometer Assy or Op Code 94002R00 for Instrumental Cluster Assy.
Charge out the replacement part and list the speedometer/instrument cluster as the causal part.
Input the calibration charge in sublet using the C2 Instrument Cluster Calibration.
Include shipping charges and attach final invoices to the claim in WebDCS.
Submit any applicable rental charges on a separate rental claim.
For vehicles equipped with Instrument Cluster Module / GDS tablet odometer mileage writing, perform the odometer mileage writing process with the GDS tablet.
Perform software verification prior to the odometer mileage writing for GDS tablet procedures.
Refer to TSB 25-BE-008H or the latest version for additional information on GDS tablet procedures.

title: 10 Years/100,000 Miles Powertrain Limited Warranty - Original Owner Verification
raw_content: Original Owner is defined as the first retail purchaser of the vehicle who took delivery of the vehicle on its date of first use. If the vehicle was first placed in service as a lease vehicle, and the lessee purchases the vehicle at the end of the lease, the 10 years/100,000 miles Powertrain Limited Warranty remains in effect. Policy Statements 54 If the vehicle was first placed in service as a Hyundai Motor America (HMA) Manager Demonstrator Vehicle or Hyundai Motor America Service Rental Car (SRC) under HMA’s Service Rental Car (SRC) Program, the original (first) retail purchaser of the vehicle is considered the original owner, and the 10 years/100,000 miles Powertrain Limited Warranty remains in effect. If the first retail purchaser of the vehicle is a commercial business, organization or government agency and not placed in commercial use (i.e. taxi, route delivery, livery service, car rental service, etc.), the 10/100,000 Powertrain Limited Warranty remains in effect. The 10 year/100,000 mile Powertrain Limited Warranty coverage is excluded for vehicles placed in commercial use (i.e. taxi, route delivery, livery service, car rental service, etc.). (2024-2026 Model Year Vehicles - Powertrain Limited Warranty) For 2024-2026 model year vehicles, this Powertrain Limited Warranty is only transferable to the spouse or registered domestic partner of the Original Owner. All subsequent owners have powertrain components covered under the 60 months/60,000 miles Hyundai New Vehicle Limited Warranty. 10 Years/100,000 Miles Powertrain Limited Warranty - Original Owner Verification Affidavit Form The 10 years/100,000 miles Powertrain Limited Warranty Original Owner Verification Affidavit Form was developed for the customer and dealer to certify their eligibility for the 10 years/100,000 mile Powertrain Limited Warranty. The affidavit is required on all powertrain claims associated with the 10 years/100,000 miles Powertrain Limited Warranty. A copy of the customer’s current and up-to-date (not expired) owner registration from Department of Motor Vehicles (DMV) to validate powertrain warranty is required with the affidavit. This affidavit form is not required on Powertrain warranty repairs during the 5 years/60,000 miles coverage period.
coverage_details: The 10 years/100,000 miles Powertrain Limited Warranty applies to the original owner, defined as the first retail purchaser who took delivery on the date of first use.
If the vehicle was first placed in service as a lease vehicle and the lessee purchases it at the end of the lease, the 10 years/100,000 miles Powertrain Limited Warranty remains in effect.

If the vehicle was first placed in service as an HMA Manager Demonstrator Vehicle or HMA Service Rental Car (SRC), the first retail purchaser is considered the original owner, and the 10 years/100,000 miles Powertrain Limited Warranty remains in effect.

If the first retail purchaser is a commercial business, organization, or government agency and the vehicle is NOT placed in commercial use, the 10 years/100,000 miles Powertrain Limited Warranty remains in effect.
For 2024-2026 model year vehicles, this Powertrain Limited Warranty is transferable only to the spouse or registered domestic partner of the Original Owner.
All subsequent owners (other than qualifying spouse/partner transfers) have powertrain components covered under the 60 months/60,000 miles Hyundai New Vehicle Limited Warranty.
exclusions: The 10 years/100,000 mile Powertrain Limited Warranty coverage is excluded for vehicles placed in commercial use (e.g., taxi, route delivery, livery service, car rental service).
For 2024-2026 model year vehicles, transfers to subsequent owners beyond the spouse or registered domestic partner of the Original Owner are excluded from the 10 years/100,000 mile coverage.
dealer_actions: The 10 years/100,000 miles Powertrain Limited Warranty Original Owner Verification Affidavit Form is required on all powertrain claims associated with the 10 years/100,000 miles Powertrain Limited Warranty.
A copy of the customer’s current and up-to-date (not expired) owner registration from the Department of Motor Vehicles (DMV) is required with the affidavit to validate powertrain warranty.
The Original Owner Verification Affidavit Form is not required on Powertrain warranty repairs during the 5 years/60,000 miles coverage period.

title: Hybrid/Electric High Voltage Battery Warranty
raw_content: Vehicles) Original Owner Only - non-transferable. The Lifetime Battery Warranty covers the Hybrid/Electric High Voltage Battery Assembly throughout the ownership period for the original owner of the vehicle. Policy Statements 57 Second and subsequent owners - The warranty period for the Hybrid/Electric High Voltage Battery Assembly for second and subsequent owners is limited to 10 years from the date of original retail delivery or date of first use, or 100,000 miles, whichever occurs first. Please Note: The Hybrid High Voltage Battery is covered for 10 years from the date of original retail delivery or date of first use, or 150,000 miles, whichever occurs first under the California Emissions coverage for subsequent owners. For applicable 2012MY - 2015MY Sonata Hybrid certified as a SULEV, 2016MY - 2019MY Sonata Plug-in Hybrid certified as a PZEV; 2018MY - 2022MY Ioniq Plug-in Hybrid certified as a PZEV or TZEV; 2022MY - 2026MY Tucson Plug-in Hybrid, and 2022MY - 2023MY Santa Fe Plug-in Hybrid certified as a TZEV, the Hybrid High Voltage Battery is covered 10 years/150,000 miles for vehicles sold, registered and operated in California, Colorado, Connecticut, Delaware, Maine, Maryland, Massachusetts, New Jersey, New York, Oregon, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island, Vermont, and Washington. Vehicles certified as a SULEV / PZEV / TZEV* must be sold, registered and operated in California or any state (with the exceptions noted above) that adopts the California Emissions warranty provisions. Certified vehicles are VIN-specific, be sure to check the Hyundai WebDCS Warranty Vehicle Information screen to identify the emission type. *“Super Ultra Low Emissions Vehicle Standard (SULEV)” / “Partial Zero-Emission Vehicles (PZEV)”/”Transitional Zero Emissions Vehicle (TZEV)”
coverage_details: The Hybrid/Electric High Voltage Battery Assembly is covered for the original owner throughout their ownership period (Lifetime Battery Warranty).

For second and subsequent owners, the Hybrid/Electric High Voltage Battery Assembly is covered for 10 years from the date of original retail delivery or first use, or 100,000 miles, whichever occurs first.

Under California Emissions coverage, for subsequent owners, the Hybrid High Voltage Battery is covered for 10 years from the date of original retail delivery or first use, or 150,000 miles, whichever occurs first.

For specific SULEV, PZEV, or TZEV certified models (2012MY-2015MY Sonata Hybrid, 2016MY-2019MY Sonata Plug-in Hybrid, 2018MY-2022MY Ioniq Plug-in Hybrid, 2022MY-2026MY Tucson Plug-in Hybrid, 2022MY-2023MY Santa Fe Plug-in Hybrid), the Hybrid High Voltage Battery is covered for 10 years or 150,000 miles.

The 10 year/150,000 mile coverage for SULEV/PZEV/TZEV certified vehicles applies if sold, registered, and operated in California, Colorado, Connecticut, Delaware, Maine, Maryland, Massachusetts, New Jersey, New York, Oregon, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island, Vermont, and Washington.
Vehicles certified as SULEV/PZEV/TZEV must be sold, registered, and operated in California or any state (with the exceptions noted above) that adopts the California Emissions warranty provisions.
exclusions: The Lifetime Battery Warranty is non-transferable and applies only to the original owner.
The extended 10 year/150,000 mile coverage for SULEV/PZEV/TZEV certified vehicles is not applicable if the vehicle is not sold, registered, and operated in the specified California Emissions states.
dealer_actions: Check the Hyundai WebDCS Warranty Vehicle Information screen to identify the emission type for VIN-specific certified vehicles.

title: 2013 Model Year and Greater Federal Emission Control Warranty Statement
raw_content: 2013 Model Year Vehicles and greater Federal Emission Control Warranty Statement In accordance with Federal Clean Air Act, as amended, Hyundai Motor America (HMA) warrants to the original and each subsequent owner of each new model Hyundai vehicle that the vehicle: (1) was designed, built and equipped to conform at the time of sale to applicable federal regulations and (2) is free from defects in material and workmanship at the time of sale which would cause the vehicle to fail to conform with such regulations for a period of five (5) years from the date of first use or 60,000 miles, whichever occurs first, except for certain specified major emissions control components for which the coverage is 8 years from the date of original retail delivery, or date of first use, or 80,000 miles, whichever occurs first. Failures, other than those resulting from defects in material or workmanship, which arise solely as a result of owner abuse and/or lack of proper maintenance are not covered by the warranty.
coverage_details: Vehicle was designed, built and equipped to conform at the time of sale to applicable federal regulations
Vehicle is free from defects in material and workmanship at the time of sale which would cause the vehicle to fail to conform with federal regulations
Standard coverage period: 5 years from the date of first use or 60,000 miles, whichever occurs first
Coverage for certain specified major emissions control components: 8 years from the date of original retail delivery or date of first use, or 80,000 miles, whichever occurs first
exclusions: Failures resulting solely from owner abuse
Failures resulting solely from lack of proper maintenance
Failures other than those resulting from defects in material or workmanship

title: Emission Performance Warranty
raw_content: Some states and local jurisdictions have established periodic vehicle inspection and maintenance (I/M) programs to encourage proper maintenance of the vehicle. If an I/M program in the customer’s area has U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) approval, the customer may be eligible for HMA’s Emission Performance Warranty coverage under the following conditions: 1. The vehicle has been maintained and operated in accordance with the scheduled maintenance instructions described in the Owner’s Manual provided with the vehicle; 2. The vehicle fails to conform during the warranty period to the applicable emission standards as determined by an emissions test approved by the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), and 3. The failure to conform results, or will result, in the owner of the vehicle having to bear a penalty or other sanctions, such as denial of the right to operate or use the vehicle or a fine under local, state or federal law. If all of the above conditions are met, HMA warrants that any authorized Hyundai Dealer will replace, repair or adjust to Hyundai’s specifications, at “NO CHARGE”, any of the components listed under Parts List”, which may be necessary to cause the vehicle to conform to the applicable federal emission standards.
coverage_details: Coverage for vehicles in states/jurisdictions with U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) approved Inspection and Maintenance (I/M) programs.
Vehicle fails to conform to applicable emission standards during the warranty period, as determined by an EPA-approved emissions test.
Failure to conform results in the owner having to bear a penalty or other sanctions (e.g., denial of right to operate, fine).
Vehicle has been maintained and operated in accordance with scheduled maintenance instructions in the Owner’s Manual.
Replacement, repair, or adjustment of necessary components (listed in 'Parts List') to cause the vehicle to conform to applicable federal emission standards.
dealer_actions: Verify the vehicle has been maintained and operated in accordance with the scheduled maintenance instructions in the Owner’s Manual.
Confirm the vehicle failed to conform to applicable emission standards during the warranty period via an EPA-approved emissions test.
Verify that the failure resulted in the owner incurring a penalty or sanctions.
Replace, repair, or adjust any necessary components to Hyundai’s specifications at “NO CHARGE” to the customer, to ensure the vehicle conforms to federal emission standards.

This warranty coverage applies to the vehicle regardless of a change in ownership and is transferable to subsequent owners.

title: Emission Warranty Exclusions and Limitations
raw_content: The Emission Defect Warranty and the Emission Performance Warranty shall not apply to: • Normal maintenance service (such as filters). • Malfunctions in any part directly caused by misuse, modification, improper adjustments, alterations, tampering, disconnections, improper or inadequate maintenance, or use of leaded gasoline or contaminated fuel. • Damage resulting from an accident or an “Act of God”. • Any vehicle on which the odometer reading has been altered so that mileage cannot be readily determined. • Failures that are a direct result of a lack of performance of required emission control maintenance as outlined in the Owner’s Manual. • Parts or accessories used in applications for which they were not designed or not approved by HMA. • Parts not supplied by HMA or damage to other parts caused directly by using non- Hyundai parts, non-EPA certified replacement parts in the maintenance or repair of the vehicle which ultimately proves to be defective in material, or workmanship, or not equivalent from an emission control standpoint to the original equipment part. • The charge for diagnostic labor which does not lead to the determination that a warrantable condition exists. • INCIDENTAL OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES INCLUDING WITHOUT LIMITATION, LOSS OF TIME, INCONVENIENCE, LOSS OF USE OF THE VEHICLE, OR COMMERCIAL LOSS. The duration of any implied warranties, including those OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE regarding emissions systems defects are limited to the duration of these emission system warranties. Some states do not allow limitations on how long an implied warranty lasts, or the exclusion or limitation of incidental or consequential damages, so the limitations or exclusions set forth regarding these warranties may not apply to the owner. Note : Vehicles which contain a non-genuine Hyundai part, or which have been misused, or improperly maintained, will not have the coverage denied under the Emissions Control Systems Defect Limited Warranty, Emissions Defect Warranty, or Emissions Performance Warranty, unless the non-genuine Hyundai part, the misuse, or the improper maintenance directly caused or contributed to the subsequent problem.

title: Maintenance Requirements and Exclusions
raw_content: It is the owner’s obligation to properly maintain and use the vehicle in accordance with HMA’s written instructions in the Owner’s Manual. Failures caused by inadequate or improper maintenance are not covered by the warranty. To help resolve questions as to whether the Policy Statements 63 proper vehicle maintenance has been performed, HMA urges that the owner retain all maintenance service records and receipts indicating that service has been performed on the vehicle.
coverage_details: Failures occurring on vehicles properly maintained and used in accordance with HMA’s Owner’s Manual instructions.
exclusions: Failures caused by inadequate or improper maintenance.

title: Replacement Parts and Emission Control System Warranty
raw_content: It is recommended that any replacement parts used for maintenance or for the repair of emission control systems be new or remanufactured Hyundai Genuine Parts. The warranty obligations are not dependent upon the use of any particular brand of replacement parts. The owner may elect to use non-genuine Hyundai parts for replacement purposes. The use of replacement parts which are not equivalent may impair the effectiveness of the emission control system. If other than Hyundai Genuine Parts are used for maintenance replacements or for the repair of components affecting emission control, the owner should determine that such parts are warranted by their manufacturer to be equivalent to Hyundai Genuine Parts in performance and durability. New or remanufactured Hyundai Genuine Parts when used in connection with Hyundai vehicles means parts manufactured by or approved by Hyundai, designed for use on Hyundai Vehicles and distributed by HMA.
coverage_details: Warranty obligations are not dependent upon the use of any particular brand of replacement parts.
Owners may elect to use non-genuine Hyundai parts for replacement purposes.
New or remanufactured Hyundai Genuine Parts are defined as parts manufactured by or approved by Hyundai, designed for use on Hyundai Vehicles, and distributed by HMA.
exclusions: Warranty may not cover issues or impairment to the emission control system if replacement parts used are not equivalent to Hyundai Genuine Parts.

title: Emission Warranty Scope and Service Requirements
raw_content: Maintenance replacement or repair of the emission control devices and systems may be performed by any qualified automotive repair establishment or individual using any part certified pursuant to applicable emission regulations, however, warranty service must be performed by an authorized HMA dealer. Claims under the Emission Performance Warranty may not be denied due to the failure of a properly installed certified non-Hyundai Genuine Part. Claims may be denied on the basis of an uncertified replacement part used in the maintenance or repair of a vehicle if Hyundai presents evidence that the uncertified replacement part is either defective in materials or workmanship or not equivalent from an emission standpoint to the original equipment part and the vehicle owner is unable to rebut the evidence. Receipts covering the performance of maintenance services should be retained in the event questions arise concerning maintenance. These receipts should be transferred to each subsequent owner of this vehicle. HMA reserves the right to deny warranty coverage if the vehicle has not been properly maintained. However, denial will not be based solely on the absence of maintenance records. These warranties apply only to vehicles manufactured to United States specifications which are distributed by Hyundai Motor America and registered and normally operated in the 50 United States and Washington, D.C., the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands, Guam, Policy Statements 64 American Samoa, including the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands. Vehicles manufactured to other than United States specifications, distributed by other than HMA and registered and normally outside the 50 United States and Washington, D.C., the Commonwealth or Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands, Guam, and American Samoa or the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands are entitled to service of the emission control systems on the basis of the warranty applicable to such other distributing country.

title: Warranty Service Procedures and Emission Performance Claims
raw_content: To obtain warranty service under these warranties, the owner should return the vehicle to an authorized HMA Dealer’s place of business during its normal service business hours, where such service will be performed at no charge for parts, labor, diagnosis, and tax. If an owner’s claim under the Emission Performance Warranty is denied, HMA will provide the owner with a written explanation of why the claim was denied (1) within 30 days from the time the owner brings the car to a HMA Dealer or (2) within the time required by local, state, or federal law for the vehicle to be repaired without incurring further penalty, whichever is shorter. Failure to provide an explanation within a 30 day period shall obligate HMA to remedy the nonconformity under the Emission Performance Warranty except: • When delay is requested by the vehicle owner. • When delay is caused by factors beyond the control of HMA or authorized HMA Dealers. If the HMA Dealer cannot repair the vehicle or process the owner’s claim within a reasonable time, the customer is to contact Hyundai’s Customer Care Center at 1-800-633-5151 for assistance. The Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) is authorized to investigate the failure of manufacturers to comply with the terms of emission warranties. If the vehicle owner has followed Hyundai’s procedures (including those for appeals) for making a warranty claim as set out in the Owner’s Handbook, has received a written denial and is not satisfied with Hyundai’s determination, the vehicle owner may submit a letter to EPA at the following address. It should provide details of the situation including the basis for the claim, a copy of the written denial, copies of the vehicle owner’s letters to Hyundai, and copies of any receipts for emission control parts and repairs that the vehicle owner has paid for: U.S. Environmental Protection Agency Office of Transportation and Air Quality Compliance Division, Light-Duty Vehicle Group Attn: Warranty Complaints 2000 Traverwood Drive Ann Arbor, MI 48105 Email: complianceinfo@epa.gov Policy Statements 65
coverage_details: Warranty service performed at no charge for parts, labor, diagnosis, and tax.
exclusions: HMA's obligation to remedy a nonconformity under the Emission Performance Warranty is waived if the delay in providing a denial explanation is requested by the vehicle owner.

HMA's obligation to remedy a nonconformity under the Emission Performance Warranty is waived if the delay in providing a denial explanation is caused by factors beyond the control of HMA or authorized HMA Dealers.
dealer_actions: Perform warranty service at no charge for parts, labor, diagnosis, and tax when the owner returns the vehicle during normal business hours.
If an Emission Performance Warranty claim is denied, ensure a written explanation for the denial is provided to the owner within 30 days or the legally required shorter time.
If the vehicle cannot be repaired or the owner's claim cannot be processed within a reasonable time, advise the customer to contact Hyundai’s Customer Care Center at 1-800-633-5151 for assistance.

title: Owner and Dealer Responsibilities
raw_content: The Hyundai vehicle owner is responsible for the performance of the required maintenance listed in the Owner’s Manual. HMA recommends that the owner retain all receipts covering maintenance on their Hyundai vehicle, but HMA will not deny emission warranty coverage solely for the lack of receipts or for failure to ensure the performance of all scheduled maintenance. • The customer is responsible for presenting their Hyundai vehicle to a HMA Dealer as soon as a problem exists. The warranty repairs should be completed in a reasonable amount of time, not to exceed 30 days. • The Hyundai vehicle owner should also be aware that HMA may deny their warranty coverage if their Hyundai vehicle or a part has failed due to abuse, neglect, improper maintenance or unapproved modifications. If the owner has any questions regarding their warranty rights and responsibilities, the owner should contact Hyundai’s Customer Care Center at 1-800-633-5151, or the Chief, Mobile Source Operations Division, California Air Resources Board at 9528 Telstar Avenue, El Monte, CA 91731. Note: According to Federal Regulations, the vehicle may also be eligible for additional warranty coverage under the Federal Emission Warranty.
coverage_details: HMA will not deny emission warranty coverage solely for the lack of maintenance receipts.

exclusions: Warranty coverage may be denied if the vehicle or a part has failed due to abuse.
Warranty coverage may be denied if the vehicle or a part has failed due to neglect.
Warranty coverage may be denied if the vehicle or a part has failed due to improper maintenance.
Warranty coverage may be denied if the vehicle or a part has failed due to unapproved modifications.

title: California and Federal Emission Warranty Coverage
raw_content: California requires minimum emission warranty coverage for the first 7 years or 70,000 miles for certain “high cost” emission related parts, determined by a California government specified formula. Policy Statements 68 Hyundai provides greater warranty coverage of 5 years or 60,000 miles for all emission related components and 8 years or 80,000 miles coverage for catalytic converters, the electronic engine control module, and the onboard emissions diagnostic device. Hyundai also provides coverage for the first 7 years or 70,000 miles for other “high cost” emission related parts, determined by a California government specified formula. The 5 year/60,000 miles, 7 year/70,000 miles, or 15 year/150,000 miles (For 2023MY, select Elantra, Sonata, Kona, Santa Fe and Tucson certified as SULEV; and Santa Fe Plug-In Hybrid and Tucson Plug-In Hybrid models certified as TZEV) Emission Control System Defect Warranty covers any part that affects emissions. Check the Owner’s Handbook and Warranty Information Booklet for specific coverages on California Spec & Federal Spec VINs. Some items are scheduled for replacement as part of the required maintenance for the vehicle. Replacement of these items is warranted up to the first replacement interval or up to the limits of the applicable Emission Warranty coverage period, whichever occurs first (refer to the Scheduled Maintenance chart in the Owner’s Manual). Any such part repaired or replaced under warranty shall be warranted for the remainder of the warranty period, for that part. If the failure of a warranted component causes damage to any other vehicle part, both parts will be covered by the Emission Warranties.
coverage_details: California minimum emission warranty for 7 years or 70,000 miles for certain “high cost” emission related parts (determined by California formula)
Hyundai provides 5 years or 60,000 miles coverage for all emission related components

For 2023MY, select Elantra, Sonata, Kona, Santa Fe and Tucson certified as SULEV, and Santa Fe Plug-In Hybrid and Tucson Plug-In Hybrid models certified as TZEV, are covered by the Emission Control System Defect Warranty for 15 years or 150,000 miles (covers any part that affects emissions)
