title: Emission Control System Warranty Exclusions
raw_content: The Emission Control System Defects Warranty and the Emission Control System Performance Warranty shall not apply to: • Malfunctions in any part directly caused by abuse, misuse, modification, improper adjustment (except those done by a dealership during warranty service work), alterations, tampering, disconnections, improper or inadequate maintenance, neglect or use of leaded gasoline or other fuels not recommended in the Owner’s Manual. • Damage resulting from an accident or an “Act of God”. • Any vehicle on which the odometer reading has been altered so that mileage cannot be readily determined. • Failures that are a direct result of a lack of performance of required emission control maintenance as outlined in the Owner’s Manual. • Parts or accessories used in applications for which they were not designed or not approved for use by HMA. Policy Statements 69 • Parts not supplied by HMA or damage to other parts caused directly by non-Hyundai parts or non-equivalent parts. • The charge for diagnostic labor which does not lead to the determination that a warrantable condition exists. • INCIDENTAL OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES, SUCH AS, LOSS OF TIME, INCONVENIENCE, LOSS OF USE OF THE VEHICLE, OR COMMERCIAL LOSS. • The duration of any implied warranties, including those of MERCHANTABILITY and FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, regarding emissions systems defects, are limited to the duration of this limited warranty. Some States do not allow limitations on how long an implied warranty lasts or the exclusion or limitation of incidental or consequential damage, so the limitations or exclusions set forth regarding this limited warranty may not apply to the vehicle owner. Note : Vehicles which contain a non-genuine Hyundai genuine part, or which have been misused, or improperly maintained, will not have the coverage under the California Emissions Control Systems Warranty denied, unless the non-genuine Hyundai part, the misuse, or the improper maintenance directly caused or contributed to the subsequent problem.
coverage_details: Emission Control System Defects
Emission Control System Performance issues
exclusions: Malfunctions directly caused by abuse, misuse, modification, improper adjustment (unless done by a dealership during warranty service work), alterations, tampering, disconnections, improper or inadequate maintenance, neglect or use of leaded gasoline or other fuels not recommended in the Owner’s Manual
Damage resulting from an accident or an "Act of God"
Any vehicle with an altered odometer reading where mileage cannot be readily determined
Failures directly resulting from a lack of performance of required emission control maintenance as outlined in the Owner’s Manual
Parts or accessories used in applications for which they were not designed or not approved for use by HMA
Parts not supplied by HMA or damage to other parts caused directly by non-Hyundai parts or non-equivalent parts
Diagnostic labor charges that do not lead to the determination of a warrantable condition
INCIDENTAL OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES, such as loss of time, inconvenience, loss of use of the vehicle, or commercial loss

Implied warranties, including MERCHANTABILITY and FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, regarding emissions systems defects are limited to the duration of this limited warranty (where allowed by state law)

title: Emission Control System - Non-Genuine Parts and Emergency Repairs
raw_content: It is recommended that any replacement parts used for maintenance, repair or replacement of the emission control systems be parts supplied by HMA. Maintenance, repair or replacement of the emission control system may be performed by any qualified automotive repair facility or individual, using parts other than Hyundai Genuine Parts without invalidating this warranty. However, the cost of such maintenance and parts will not be covered under this warranty unless it is an “emergency repair” situation. Refer to “Emergency Repair” for definition of emergency repair situation. Use of replacement parts which are not equivalent in quality to Hyundai Genuine Parts may impair the effectiveness of the emission control system. The owner should make sure such parts are warranted by their manufacturer to be equivalent in design and quality to Hyundai supplied parts. HMA assumes no liability under this warranty for any vehicle damage caused by a defect in any such parts.
coverage_details: Warranty for the emission control system remains valid even if maintenance, repair, or replacement is performed using parts other than Hyundai Genuine Parts.
The cost of maintenance and parts (other than Hyundai Genuine Parts) is covered if the situation qualifies as an "emergency repair".
exclusions: The cost of maintenance and parts (other than Hyundai Genuine Parts) will not be covered, unless it is an "emergency repair" situation.
HMA assumes no liability under this warranty for any vehicle damage caused by a defect in non-Hyundai Genuine Parts.
dealer_actions: Evaluate claims for emission control system repairs involving non-Hyundai Genuine Parts to determine if the situation qualifies as an "emergency repair" for cost coverage (refer to "Emergency Repair" definition).

title: Emission Control Systems Warranty Service Procedures
raw_content: To obtain warranty service under these warranties, the owner must return the Hyundai vehicle to any authorized HMA Dealer in California, Connecticut, Delaware, Maine, Maryland, Massachusetts, New Jersey, New York, Oregon, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island, Vermont, or Washington during its normal service business hours, where such service will be performed. Policy Statements 70 When failing a Smog Check Test, an authorized HMA Dealer may ask the owner to provide proof that the required maintenance was performed when the Dealer has reason to believe that the required maintenance: • Was not performed, and • If not performed, may be the cause for the particular vehicle exceeding applicable emission standards. For a Smog Check Test failure, if your warranty claim under the Emission Control Systems Limited Warranties is denied, HMA or its Dealer will provide the owner with a written explanation of why the claim was denied within 30 days from the time the owner brings the car to a HMA Dealer. If the authorized Hyundai Dealer does not notify the vehicle owner within 30 days that a warrantable condition does not exist, then HMA will repair the vehicle free of charge, unless: • The delay is requested by the Hyundai vehicle owner. • The delay is caused by factors beyond the control of HMA or an authorized HMA Dealer. The lack of availability of warranted parts within 30 days shall constitute an emergency situation (see Section 4.7.9–Emergency Repair).
coverage_details: Warranty service will be performed at any authorized HMA Dealer in California, Connecticut, Delaware, Maine, Maryland, Massachusetts, New Jersey, New York, Oregon, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island, Vermont, or Washington during normal business hours.
If an authorized Hyundai Dealer does not notify the vehicle owner within 30 days that a warrantable condition does not exist, HMA will repair the vehicle free of charge.
exclusions: If the delay in notifying the owner about a warrantable condition (beyond 30 days) is requested by the Hyundai vehicle owner.
If the delay in notifying the owner about a warrantable condition (beyond 30 days) is caused by factors beyond the control of HMA or an authorized HMA Dealer.

Warranty claims under Emission Control Systems Limited Warranties may be denied if required maintenance was not performed and is believed to be the cause for the vehicle exceeding applicable emission standards.
dealer_actions: Perform warranty service on Hyundai vehicles returned to them for service.

May ask the owner to provide proof that required maintenance was performed if the dealer has reason to believe it was not performed and may be the cause for the vehicle exceeding applicable emission standards (Smog Check Test failure).

For a Smog Check Test failure, if an Emission Control Systems Limited Warranty claim is denied, provide the owner with a written explanation of why the claim was denied within 30 days from the time the owner brings the car to the HMA Dealer.
Notify the vehicle owner within 30 days if a warrantable condition does not exist.
Consider the lack of availability of warranted parts within 30 days as an emergency situation (refer to Section 4.7.9–Emergency Repair).

title: Emergency Warranty Repairs
raw_content: In an emergency, where the vehicle is inoperable or unsafe and an authorized HMA Dealer is not reasonably available, warranty repairs may be made at any available service facility or by the owner using any replacement part. A repair not being completed within 30 days also constitutes an emergency. HMA will reimburse the owner for expenses including diagnostic charges, for repairs covered by this warranty, for parts at HMA’s suggested retail price and for labor at a geographically appropriate labor rate per HMA’s recommended time allowance. For reimbursement, the owner must contact the Hyundai Customer Care Center at 1-800-633- 5151. HMA assumes no liability for the workmanship of the repair facility.
coverage_details: Warranty repairs may be made at any available service facility or by the owner using any replacement part if the vehicle is inoperable or unsafe and an authorized HMA Dealer is not reasonably available.
A repair not being completed within 30 days also constitutes an emergency for warranty repair purposes.
HMA will reimburse the owner for expenses, including diagnostic charges, for repairs covered by this warranty.
Reimbursement for parts will be at HMA’s suggested retail price.
Reimbursement for labor will be at a geographically appropriate labor rate per HMA’s recommended time allowance.
exclusions: HMA assumes no liability for the workmanship of the repair facility used for emergency repairs.

title: Smog Check Warranty Coverage (2013 Model Year and Greater)
raw_content: 2010 Model Year Vehicles and prior - See Applicable Owner’s Handbook 2013 Model Year Vehicles and greater If a Hyundai 2013 Model Year Vehicle or greater fails to pass a Smog Check test after a period of use of 5 years or 60,000 miles, but before a period of use of 8 years or 80,000 miles, whichever occurs first, the owner may choose to have their vehicle repaired at an authorized HMA Dealer or another service facility. Policy Statements 71 In the event that the HMA Dealer determines that the smog check failure was caused by the failure or malfunction of a part listed in this Owner’s Handbook specially noted with coverage for 8 years or 80,000 miles, then the Hyundai vehicle will be repaired under this limited warranty at no expense to the owner, unless the part failure or malfunction was caused by abuse, neglect, or improper maintenance. However, if the authorized HMA Dealer determines that the smog check failure was directly caused by abuse, neglect, or improper maintenance, then the owner will be responsible for all diagnostic and repair costs related to detecting and repairing the Hyundai vehicle so it will pass the Smog Check Test. If the owner chooses to have their Hyundai vehicle repaired at a service facility other than an authorized HMA Dealer, HMA will not be liable for any diagnostic and repair expenses, except where such repair is necessary by reason of an emergency. If the Hyundai vehicle is determined to fail the Smog Check Test by such service facility, the owner may deliver their Hyundai vehicle to an authorized HMA Dealer and have it repaired free of charge in accordance with this warranty. Policy Statements 72
coverage_details: Smog Check test failure for Hyundai 2013 Model Year vehicles and greater, occurring after 5 years or 60,000 miles but before 8 years or 80,000 miles, whichever occurs first.
Repair of parts listed in the Owner's Handbook with 8 years or 80,000 miles coverage, if their failure or malfunction caused the smog check failure.
Covered repairs performed at an authorized HMA Dealer will be at no expense to the owner.
If a vehicle determined to fail the Smog Check Test by an independent service facility is brought to an authorized HMA Dealer, it will be repaired free of charge under this warranty.
exclusions: Part failure or malfunction caused by abuse, neglect, or improper maintenance.
Diagnostic and repair costs are the owner's responsibility if an authorized HMA Dealer determines the smog check failure was directly caused by abuse, neglect, or improper maintenance.

HMA is not liable for diagnostic and repair expenses if the owner chooses to have their vehicle repaired at a service facility other than an authorized HMA Dealer, except where such repair is necessary by reason of an emergency.
dealer_actions: Diagnose the cause of the Smog Check failure for 2013 Model Year and greater vehicles.

Determine if the smog check failure was caused by the failure or malfunction of a part listed in the Owner
as Handbook specially noted with coverage for 8 years or 80,000 miles.
Repair the Hyundai vehicle under this limited warranty at no expense to the owner if the failure is covered.
Inform the owner if the smog check failure was caused by abuse, neglect, or improper maintenance, making the owner responsible for all related diagnostic and repair costs.
Perform repairs free of charge in accordance with this warranty if an owner brings in a vehicle determined to fail a Smog Check Test by another service facility.

title: Replacement Parts and Accessories Warranty Policy
raw_content: Warranty coverage is for repair or replacement of any Hyundai Genuine Replacement Part or Accessory supplied from Hyundai Motor America and installed on Hyundai vehicles which is found to be defective in material or workmanship under normal use and maintenance, except any item specifically referred to in the Section 4.8.6 “What is Not Covered”. Repairs are to be performed using New Hyundai Genuine Parts or Hyundai authorized remanufactured parts. Parts/Accessories that were sold and installed by an Authorized Hyundai Dealer will be repaired or replaced without charge for diagnosis, parts/accessories and labor. If the parts/accessories were not originally installed by an Authorized Hyundai Dealer, they will be repaired or replaced without charge for parts/accessories, but labor charges will not be covered. Exception to policy: Engine assembly, transmission assembly, or electric high voltage battery that fails during the replacement part warranty period will need to be diagnosed by an Authorized Hyundai Dealer. If the engine, transmission, or high voltage battery is determined to be defective in material or workmanship, HMA will reimburse parts and labor to an Authorized Hyundai Dealer to replace the defective component(s). Prior Approval from the PA Center is required for engine assembly, transmission assembly, and electric high voltage battery. Initial diagnosis will be customer pay until the dealer diagnostic is complete and verified as a manufacturing defect. Not warrantable: If replacement part(s) failure was due to incorrect installation by a third-party repair facility, the third-party repair facility will be responsible for repair costs (parts & labor) associated with the incorrect installation. Failure to follow HMA published repair procedures (e.g., TSBs) will result in disapproval of claim submission.
coverage_details: Repair or replacement of any Hyundai Genuine Replacement Part or Accessory supplied from Hyundai Motor America and installed on Hyundai vehicles, found to be defective in material or workmanship under normal use and maintenance.
Repairs are to be performed using New Hyundai Genuine Parts or Hyundai authorized remanufactured parts.
Parts/Accessories sold and installed by an Authorized Hyundai Dealer will be repaired or replaced without charge for diagnosis, parts/accessories, and labor.
If parts/accessories were not originally installed by an Authorized Hyundai Dealer, they will be repaired or replaced without charge for parts/accessories (labor charges not covered).

If an engine assembly, transmission assembly, or electric high voltage battery fails during the replacement part warranty period and is determined defective in material or workmanship by an Authorized Hyundai Dealer, HMA will reimburse parts and labor to the dealer for replacement.
exclusions: Any item specifically referred to in Section 4.8.6 “What is Not Covered”.
Labor charges if parts/accessories were not originally installed by an Authorized Hyundai Dealer.
Initial diagnosis for engine assembly, transmission assembly, or electric high voltage battery until the dealer diagnostic is complete and verified as a manufacturing defect (customer pay).
Replacement part(s) failure due to incorrect installation by a third-party repair facility.
Claims resulting from failure to follow HMA published repair procedures (e.g., TSBs).
dealer_actions: Diagnose engine assembly, transmission assembly, or electric high voltage battery that fails during the replacement part warranty period to determine if defective in material or workmanship.
Obtain Prior Approval from the PA Center for engine assembly, transmission assembly, and electric high voltage battery replacements.
Perform repairs using New Hyundai Genuine Parts or Hyundai authorized remanufactured parts.
Adhere to HMA published repair procedures (e.g., TSBs).

title: Replacement Parts Limited Warranty
raw_content: The Replacement Parts Limited Warranty Period is limited to twelve (12) months from the date of installation or twelve thousand (12,000) miles of service, whichever occurs first, for Hyundai Genuine replacement parts supplied by HMA and purchased from and installed by an authorized Hyundai Motor America Dealer, which were installed beyond the time and mileage limits of the New Vehicle Limited, Powertrain, Emissions Defect, Emissions Performance, California Emissions Control System, and Anti-Perforation Warranties. Parts replaced free of charge to the customer under the terms of the New Vehicle Limited Warranty, Powertrain Warranty, Emission Defect Warranty, Emission Performance Warranty, or California Emission Control System Warranty are warranted for the remainder of the time/mileage coverage applicable to the warranty coverage under which the part/accessory was previously replaced. Policy Statements 73 The twelve (12) month/twelve thousand (12,000) miles Hyundai Parts and Accessories Limited Warranty only applies to genuine Hyundai parts supplied by HMA and that were purchased from an authorized HMA dealer. Those parts replaced under one of the above noted Hyundai Warranties will be warranted for the remainder of the warranty coverage that the part was replaced under. Example: A heater control switch on a 2017 Sonata is replaced under the terms of the Hyundai New Vehicle Limited Warranty at fifty-nine (59) months/fifty-nine thousand (59,000) miles. The heater control switch is covered under the sixty (60) months/sixty thousand (60,000) miles Hyundai New Vehicle Limited Warranty. The heater control switch that was installed at fifty-nine (59) months/fifty-nine thousand (59,000) miles is covered for the remainder of the 60 month/60,000 miles, or an additional 1 month/1,000 miles and not for an additional 12 months/12,000 miles. Certain Emission Control System items require scheduled replacement as part of the scheduled maintenance of the vehicle (i.e. spark plugs). Replacement of these items is warranted in normal service up to the first replacement interval (time or mileage), or the emissions warranty period (Refer to the Owner’s Manual and its scheduled maintenance section). Genuine Hyundai Parts and Accessories supplied by HMA and purchased from an authorized Hyundai Motor America Dealer, but not installed by an authorized Hyundai Dealer are covered twelve (12) months/unlimited mileage from the date of purchase from the authorized Hyundai Motor America Dealer.
coverage_details: Hyundai Genuine replacement parts supplied by HMA and purchased from and installed by an authorized Hyundai Motor America Dealer, installed beyond the time and mileage limits of the New Vehicle Limited, Powertrain, Emissions Defect, Emissions Performance, California Emissions Control System, and Anti-Perforation Warranties, are covered for twelve (12) months or twelve thousand (12,000) miles, whichever occurs first.

Parts replaced free of charge under the New Vehicle Limited Warranty, Powertrain Warranty, Emission Defect Warranty, Emission Performance Warranty, or California Emission Control System Warranty are warranted for the remainder of the time/mileage coverage applicable to the warranty under which the part was previously replaced.

Genuine Hyundai Parts and Accessories supplied by HMA and purchased from an authorized Hyundai Motor America Dealer, but not installed by an authorized Hyundai Dealer, are covered for twelve (12) months/unlimited mileage from the date of purchase.

Certain Emission Control System items requiring scheduled replacement are warranted in normal service up to the first replacement interval (time or mileage) or the emissions warranty period (refer to Owner's Manual).
exclusions: The 12-month/12,000-mile warranty does not apply to parts replaced under the New Vehicle Limited Warranty, Powertrain Warranty, Emission Defect Warranty, Emission Performance Warranty, or California Emission Control System Warranty; these parts are covered only for the remainder of the original applicable warranty.
Parts not supplied by HMA or not genuine Hyundai parts.
Parts not purchased from an authorized HMA dealer (for the 12-month/12,000-mile installed warranty).
Parts purchased from an authorized dealer but not installed by an authorized dealer are subject to different coverage (12 months/unlimited mileage from purchase date).
dealer_actions: Ensure installation of genuine Hyundai replacement parts for warranty validity.
Refer to Owner's Manual for scheduled maintenance and emission control system item replacement intervals.

title: Replacement Battery Warranty
raw_content: Hyundai replacement batteries installed after 36 months from the date of first use are covered for twelve (12) months/unlimited mileage from date of installation or retail sale. If the replacement battery was originally installed by a Hyundai Dealer, the labor to remove and replace the battery is also covered. After this twelve (12) month period, dealer warranty reimbursement will be made on a prorated basis of the current dealer net cost of the battery to a maximum of forty-eight (48) months/unlimited mileage from the date of installation. Refer to the Replacement Battery Proration Chart located in the 9.0 Warranty Resources section of this manual. The labor to remove and replace the battery is not covered after this twelve (12) month period and is to be charged to the customer.
coverage_details: Hyundai replacement batteries installed after 36 months from the date of first use are covered for 12 months/unlimited mileage from date of installation or retail sale.
If the replacement battery was originally installed by a Hyundai Dealer, the labor to remove and replace the battery is also covered for the initial 12-month period.

After the 12-month period, dealer warranty reimbursement for the battery itself will be made on a prorated basis of the current dealer net cost, up to a maximum of 48 months/unlimited mileage from the date of installation.
exclusions: Labor to remove and replace the battery is not covered after the initial 12-month period.
dealer_actions: Refer to the Replacement Battery Proration Chart located in the 9.0 Warranty Resources section of this manual.
Labor to remove and replace the battery after the initial 12-month period is to be charged to the customer.

title: Hyundai Genuine Accessory Warranty
raw_content: Accessories installed by authorized Hyundai dealer Model Year 2018 - 2026 vehicles Policy Statements 74 A Hyundai Genuine Accessory installed by an Authorized Hyundai Dealer on a new Hyundai vehicle at the time of, prior to, or after the vehicle’s date of delivery to the original retail purchaser, or the date the vehicle is first put into service, is covered for 1 year / unlimited mileage, whichever occurs first from the date of original date of original retail delivery or date for first use. The accessories may also be covered by a separate manufacturer warranty. Please refer to the specific accessory for full details. Certain accessories, such as Thule, are covered by its own manufacture warranty and do not carry any warranty provided by Hyundai. Note: Port Installed Accessories prior to RDR is covered under the New Vehicle Limited Warranty and is limited to 60 months from the date of original retail delivery, or date of first use, or 60,000 miles, whichever occurs first. Model Year 2016 - 2017 vehicles An accessory installed on a new Hyundai vehicle at the time of, or prior to the vehicle’s date of delivery to the original retail purchaser, or the date the vehicle is first put into service, whichever is earlier, is covered under the New Vehicle Limited Warranty and is limited to 60 months from the date of original retail delivery, or date of first use, or 60,000 miles, whichever occurs first. This would include all Port Installed and Dealer installed Accessories prior to RDR. An accessory installed after the vehicle has been retailed or placed into service shall be warranted for the remainder of the New Vehicle Limited Warranty, up to 60 months from the date of original retail delivery, or date of first use, or 60,000 miles, whichever occurs first. An accessory installed after the vehicle has been retailed or placed into service shall be warranted for twelve (12) months or twelve thousand (12,000) miles, whichever occurs first, from the date of purchase or installation. Accessories sold over the counter An accessory purchased over-the-counter is warranted for twelve (12) months/unlimited mileage. An air conditioning system installed by an authorized Hyundai Motor America Dealer is covered for twelve (12) months or twelve thousand (12,000) miles, whichever occurs first, or the remainder of the New Vehicle Limited Warranty, whichever is greater, from the date of installation. An AM/ FM radio, cassette player, CD/DVD player or air conditioning system purchased over-the-counter is warranted for twelve (12) months/unlimited mileage. An AM/FM radio, cassette player and CD/DVD player are covered for 12 months, unlimited mileage, if purchased and installed after the vehicle has been retailed or placed into service. Policy Statements 75 A Bluetooth system, if installed after the vehicle has been retailed or placed into service, is covered for 12 months, or 12,000 miles.
coverage_details: Hyundai Genuine Accessory installed by an Authorized Hyundai Dealer on a new Hyundai vehicle (Model Year 2018-2026) at the time of, prior to, or after the vehicle’s date of delivery or first use: 1 year / unlimited mileage from date of original retail delivery or first use.

Port Installed Accessories (Model Year 2018-2026) prior to Retail Delivery Report (RDR): Covered under the New Vehicle Limited Warranty, limited to 60 months from the date of original retail delivery or first use, or 60,000 miles, whichever occurs first.

Accessory installed on a new Hyundai vehicle (Model Year 2016-2017) at the time of, or prior to the vehicle’s date of delivery or first use: Covered under the New Vehicle Limited Warranty, limited to 60 months from the date of original retail delivery or first use, or 60,000 miles, whichever occurs first. This includes Port Installed and Dealer installed Accessories prior to RDR.

Accessory installed on Model Year 2016-2017 vehicles after the vehicle has been retailed or placed into service: Covered for the remainder of the New Vehicle Limited Warranty, up to 60 months from the date of original retail delivery or first use, or 60,000 miles, whichever occurs first.

Accessory installed on Model Year 2016-2017 vehicles after the vehicle has been retailed or placed into service: Warranted for twelve (12) months or twelve thousand (12,000) miles, whichever occurs first, from the date of purchase or installation.
Accessory purchased over-the-counter: Warranted for twelve (12) months / unlimited mileage.

Air conditioning system installed by an authorized Hyundai Motor America Dealer: Covered for twelve (12) months or twelve thousand (12,000) miles, whichever occurs first, or the remainder of the New Vehicle Limited Warranty, whichever is greater, from the date of installation.
AM/FM radio, cassette player, CD/DVD player or air conditioning system purchased over-the-counter: Warranted for twelve (12) months / unlimited mileage.
AM/FM radio, cassette player and CD/DVD player purchased and installed after the vehicle has been retailed or placed into service: Covered for 12 months / unlimited mileage.
Bluetooth system installed after the vehicle has been retailed or placed into service: Covered for 12 months or 12,000 miles.
exclusions: Certain accessories (e.g., Thule) are covered by their own manufacturer warranty and do not carry any warranty provided by Hyundai.
dealer_actions: Refer to the specific accessory for full details regarding any separate manufacturer warranty.

title: Customer Responsibilities for Warranty Claims
raw_content: • Proper use, maintenance, and care of the vehicle (part) in accordance with the instructions contained in the Owner’s Manual. • Retention of maintenance service records. It may be necessary for the customer to demonstrate and prove that the required maintenance has been performed as specified in the Owner’s Manual. • Retention of the Customer’s copy of the original repair order or original part counter sales receipts. The repair order or sales slip receipt will state the date of the parts sale. For parts originally installed by an authorized Hyundai Dealer, the repair order must also state the mileage of the vehicle at the time of original replacement. • Delivery of the part or accessory and supporting documentation indicating the date and if applicable, mileage at time of purchase and installation during regular parts and/or service hours to any authorized Hyundai Dealer to obtain warranty service.

Customer must retain and be able to demonstrate proof of required maintenance service records.
Customer must retain their copy of the original repair order or original part counter sales receipts.

Customer must deliver the part or accessory and supporting documentation indicating the date and, if applicable, mileage at time of purchase and installation to an authorized Hyundai Dealer during regular parts and/or service hours to obtain warranty service.

title: Over the Air (OTA) Software Updates
raw_content: Some Hyundai vehicles with an active Bluelink account have control units that can perform “Over the Air (OTA)” software updates. These updates are performed in the background and are typically imperceptible to the dealer or customer. Performance of Over the Air updates are considered a feature of operation of the vehicle and are not a warrantable concern. In the event of an Over the Air update fails to program properly or if the vehicle is presented with a concern that is rectified by performing an Over the Air update; any associated diagnosis and repairs are covered under the New Vehicle Limited Warranty (NVLW). Note: If an Over the Air causes failure to a component and the vehicle is out of warranty, please submit a Prior Approval Request for goodwill consideration prior to performing the work. See section 5.4.1 Prior Approval Requirements.
coverage_details: Diagnosis and repairs associated with an Over the Air (OTA) update that fails to program properly are covered under the New Vehicle Limited Warranty (NVLW)
Diagnosis and repairs for a vehicle concern rectified by performing an Over the Air (OTA) update are covered under the New Vehicle Limited Warranty (NVLW)
exclusions: The performance of Over the Air (OTA) updates themselves are considered a feature of operation and are not a warrantable concern
dealer_actions: If an Over the Air (OTA) update causes a component failure and the vehicle is out of warranty, submit a Prior Approval Request for goodwill consideration prior to performing any work
Refer to section 5.4.1 Prior Approval Requirements

title: Reimbursement Policies
raw_content: Annual map updates performed by the Hyundai dealer are not covered under warranty. Reimbursement Policies 78 Reimbursement Policies

title: Warranty Claim Submission and Powertrain Warranty Requirements
raw_content: The majority of the claims submitted to HMA will be entered on the Warranty Claim Screen. This screen is used to obtain reimbursement for most repairs performed during the warranty period. The only time this screen would not be used is if the claim falls within the criteria of one of the following other screens: • Replacement Parts and Accessories (Retail Part) Claim Screen • Campaign Claim Screen • Pre-Delivery Inspection (PDI) Claim Screen • Rental Claim Screen Before a dealer submits each 10 Year/100,000 Mile Powertrain Limited Warranty related repair claim, a Dealer must input original owner validated information in the screen listed below. • Original Owner Verification Screen If a warranty claim is submitted for a 10 Years/100,000 Miles Powertrain Limited Warranty repair, and there is no corresponding Original Owner Verification record on file with HMA, the Powertrain claim will be returned. The first retailed owner of a Class II vehicle “sold as used” which is sold at auction is eligible for 10/100. Note : Check your Warranty Claims Processing Manual on HyundaiDealer.com under the Service tab / Dealer Resources / Documents Library / Warranty / Warranty Claims Processing Manual (SVC-1241) for the necessary instructions on Original Owner Verification Record submission.

title: Replacement Parts and Accessories Limited Warranty Claims
raw_content: The Replacement Parts and Accessories Claim Screen is to be used for submission of claims related to parts previously replaced as part of a customer pay repair or an over-the-counter sale found to be defective. For detailed information on the Replacement Parts and Accessories Limited Warranty, see Policy Statements) in this manual. Reimbursement Policies 79 The Replacement Parts and Accessories Claim Screen should only be used when the defective part was previously replaced and originally purchased by the customer. In all cases, the replacement part must be determined to be defective in material or workmanship. The following situations will indicate the use of the Replacement Parts and Accessories Claim Screen: 1. The part was previously replaced on a “customer pay” repair within the past 12 months or 12,000 miles, whichever occurs first. 2. The part was previously purchased over-the-counter and is returned due to defects in material or workmanship within 12 months of purchase, regardless of mileage.

The defective part was previously replaced and originally purchased by the customer.

Ensure the defective part was previously replaced and originally purchased by the customer.
Determine that the replacement part is defective in material or workmanship.

title: Rideshare and Service Rental Car Reimbursement
raw_content: Rideshare Hyundai Motor America encourages its dealers to participate and actively utilize Service Rental Cars (SRC), 3rd Party Rentals and Rideshares. These programs are intended to help drive and maintain customer satisfaction by providing service customers with transportation while their vehicle is in service. Reimbursement Policies 81 The expectation for the program is that dealers will present SRC and alternative transportation options to all retail service customers regardless of warranty status or where the vehicle was purchased. The SRC program and alternative transportation options are among the strongest customer retention and satisfaction tools available. Note: The SRC, 3rd Party Rental and Rideshare options are not considered part of the New Vehicle Limited Warranty (NVLW). For further details, including reimbursement rates, best practices and requirements, please refer to the SRC Program Rules available on www.Hyundaidealer.com > Service > Document Library > Service Rental Car. Criteria for Reimbursement For those repairs being performed under the New Vehicle Limited Warranty (NVLW) that meet the specific requirements outlined below, Hyundai Motor America will reimburse the dealer based on a three-tier daily reimbursement rate. For 3rd Party Rentals or Rideshares (i.e., Uber, Lyft) a specific defined daily rate of reimbursement is provided as well. No Prior approval is required for SRCs, 3rd Party Rentals or Rideshares so long as it meets the criteria for reimbursement outlined below. Hyundai Motor America may provide a Service Rental Car (SRC), 3rd Party Rental or Rideshare reimbursement to dealers for customer satisfaction purposes for one or more of the following reasons: • Vehicle is within any warrantable and certain goodwill conditions and the repair cannot be completed in the same day and: – Vehicle is unsafe, inoperative, or requires emergency repairs; or – Non-emergency repairs require the vehicle to be kept overnight to complete. • Parts are on HMA backorder (warrantable concern or customer pay) - Both parts on backorder (B/O) screen printout(s) and part arrival record(s) (for example, WebDCS Parts Order Inquiry screen) be attached to Repair Order, and meets the requirements of: – Vehicle is unsafe, inoperative, or requires emergency repairs; or – Non-emergency repairs require the vehicle to be kept overnight to complete. • Vehicle has multiple warranty issues where total time to complete repairs requires more than one day. • Vehicle has extensive diagnostic time where total time to complete diagnosis and repairs require more than one day. • At the request of HMA’s AMM, FSE, or CA department; alternative transportation can be provided for technical inspection of the subject vehicle. If a dealer cannot duplicate a customer complaint and consequently submits a No Trouble Found (NTF) claim, HMA can reimburse a maximum of two days rental (2 business days, Monday to Friday) . Reimbursement Policies 82 If one or more of the aforementioned bulleted requirements are met, the dealer is eligible to receive reimbursement. The Hyundai SRC will only pay out max of one daily reimbursement rate per day. IE: If a vehicle is used multiple times in one day for warranty related concerns, HMA will only reimburse for one occurrence that day. However, a vehicle used multiple times a day will benefit SRC utilization. Please refer to SRC Program rules for complete details. Dealers are required to complete warranty repairs in a timely manner. Dealers may be denied or charged back reimbursement if it is the result of inadequate dealer scheduling or if the dealer is found not to have accessed available TSB, HTSS, or other HMA technical resources leading to delays in the repair. Backordered parts should be placed on a daily parts order or SOP order. If a dealer is found not to have placed an expedited parts order, the rental is subject to chargeback. Customers are responsible for meeting the requirements of the 3rd party rental agency. Note: Allowance for ordered parts is up to 2 rental days (2 business days, Monday to Friday) after the part arrives for dealer to complete the repair. If allowance is exceeded, dealer must attach supporting documentation to the claim for review. In the event a customer is not offered or initially declines an SRC, 3rd Party Rental, or Rideshare but meets the requirements and later requests one of these services; the SRC, 3rd Party Rental, or Rideshare can be offered and is eligible for reimbursement. Rideshare The dealer is eligible for reimbursement for the use of rideshare services provided on behalf of the customer such as Uber or Lyft up to the published daily rate per day while the customer’s vehicle is in service and meets the criteria for reimbursement outlined in this section. The trips can be to and from the dealership or for any ridesharing trips requested by the customer for the duration of their repairs up to the maximum daily reimbursement amount. Dealer Provided Shuttle Service Dealer provided shuttle service is not eligible for dealer reimbursement. Active SRC units cannot be used as a Shuttle. Reimbursement Criteria Not Met Costs associated with SRC vehicles, 3rd Party Rentals, or Rideshares provided for customers that are outside of any warrantable, certain goodwill, certain customer pay conditions, complimentary maintenance, or for those that do not meet the program criteria for reimbursement are the responsibility of the dealership or customer and will not be reimbursed. Source of Rental Vehicles Hyundai encourages dealers to participate in the SRC program, and when an SRC is not available, then an independent 3rd Party Rental may be provided. Dealers are encouraged to Reimbursement Policies 83 sign up for and utilize the Enterprise ARMS© Dealership platform for 3rd Party Rental services and, if available, request a Hyundai vehicle for the warranty customer. Submission of Service Rental / 3rd Party Rental / Rideshare Claims Documentation & Retention All SRC, 3rd Party Rental and Rideshare claims must be submitted to Hyundai Motor America only if there is a corresponding paid warranty claim, campaign claim or certain customer pay conditions. All program claims are subject to review at time of claim submission. Refer to the Hyundai Warranty Claims Processing Manual for details. For warranty claims or campaign claims, please check the Claim Status Report (answerback) or the Claim Inquiry screen in WebDCS to obtain the causal part number. For SRCs, a copy of the TSD (SRC fleet management tool) Vehicle Use Agreement signed by the customer must be attached to the claim. Please also attach any corresponding documents to justify the rental days submitted. For 3 rd Party Rentals, a copy of the rental agreement or final invoice must be attached to the claim. This must show RO number, customer name, days, rental model, rental VIN, daily rate and total amount. Please also attach any corresponding documents to justify the rental days submitted. A rental justification form may be required for explanation and supporting documents on rentals over 3 days. For Rideshares, a copy of the final invoice/receipt must be attached to the claim. Please also attach any corresponding documents to justify the rental days submitted. Receipts for each ride must be attached to the repair order and maintained with the file copy. Unless otherwise required by applicable state law, claims for SRC, 3 rd Party Rentals, and/or Ride Shares should be submitted at the same time as the corresponding repair claim. Claim submission or resubmission over 30 days is subject to denial. See section 6.0. Claims submitted with incorrect/invalid Repair Claim information (RO number and Causal Part) may go into “hold status” for up to 7 days. If after 7 days the system does not find the corresponding repair claim, the claim will be returned. If dealer notices incorrect information was entered or there is no corresponding claim, such as a buyback situation, please contact the WCC through the Warranty Support Portal or by phone at 877-446-2922 for immediate processing. Note: Chargeback of warranty claim will result in subsequent chargeback of associated SRC / Rental / Rideshare Claim(s). What Is Not Covered Under no circumstances should a claim be submitted to Hyundai for the following reasons: Reimbursement Policies 84 • The vehicle is outside of any warrantable and certain goodwill conditions or does not meet the criteria for claim reimbursement. • The rental vehicle is kept beyond the completion of warranty repairs to the eligible vehicle. When the warranty repairs are completed and the customer is notified, continued use of a rental vehicle is at the expense of the customer or dealership. Any warranty claim submitted to Hyundai should not include charges for continued use. • Over-the-counter and retail parts purchases are not eligible for rental coverage due to parts issues or delay. • Dealer scheduling/time issues or delays in servicing the vehicle are not valid reasons for submitting extended rental coverage. • Extended Service Contracts with rental coverage must be used and documented first before considering HMA goodwill rental. Reimbursement Policies 85 RENTAL REASON CODE RENTAL REASON DESCRIPTION REQUIREMENT FOR USAGE DOCUMENTATION NEEDED R4 Parts on HMA Backorder Parts on Backorder due to Hyundai Motor America backorder status. Additional comments must be entered on claim, including part number and parts order number. Repair issue must affect drivability/safety. Requires copy of parts availability screen attached to R.O. R5 Overnight repair / VOR Part Order / Weekend / Holiday Issue must affect drivability/safety for more than one day of rental coverage, or repair falls over a weekend or Holiday Additional comments must be entered on claim and include which part(s) were SOP. Requires copy of parts order attached to R.O., or detail why customer was provided rental over holiday/ weekend, rather than set up for appointment during normal work week. R6 Extensive Diagnosis / FSE / Fast involved Repair requires extensive diagnosis to determine applicable repair Additional comments must be entered on claim including Techline case #, FSE or FAST involvement. R8 Remedy Not Available Rental was provided due to an open recall without a remedy Additional comments must be entered on claim including remedy not available recall information and related part order information, if applicable. R9 Buyback Vehicle has been in dealership for multiple concerns and rental given as part of customer satisfaction. Additional comments must be entered. If CA case exists, Case # must be included in tech notes with claim. Service Manager must sign R.O. and notate authorizing rental in this case. Note: Allowance for ordered parts is up to two (2) rental days (2 business days, Monday to Friday) after the part arrives for dealer to complete the repair. If allowance is exceeded, dealer must attach supporting documentation to the claim for review. Reimbursement Policies 86

title: Parts Handling Allowance Reimbursement
raw_content: Unless otherwise required by applicable state law, HMA will reimburse the Dealer at the Dealer net price plus an additional parts handling allowance based on the Dealer net price. The Dealer net price is the current purchase price applicable to the Dealer on the repair order (RO) open date. The parts handling allowance is to cover the dealer’s administrative costs associated with the storage and handling of the parts. The parts handling allowance is: • 40% for 2016 - 2026 model year vehicles • 40% for all over-the-counter claims • For new Hyundai dealers as a result of a Buy-Sell: the parts handling allowance shall be the lower of: (1) the parts allowance that the selling dealer received at the time of sale or (2) the median dealer parts allowance in the state in which the dealer operates. For example: Selling dealer is 42% and the median dealer parts allowance in the state is 41%, the buying dealer will receive a 41% dealer parts allowance. • For new Hyundai dealers by appointment of HMA: the parts handling allowance shall be 40% • For all other situations: the parts handling allowance shall be 40%
coverage_details: HMA will reimburse the Dealer at the Dealer net price plus an additional parts handling allowance, unless otherwise required by applicable state law.
The Dealer net price is defined as the current purchase price applicable to the Dealer on the repair order (RO) open date.
The parts handling allowance is provided to cover the dealer’s administrative costs associated with the storage and handling of parts.
The parts handling allowance is 40% for 2016 - 2026 model year vehicles.
The parts handling allowance is 40% for all over-the-counter claims.

For new Hyundai dealers resulting from a Buy-Sell: the parts handling allowance shall be the lower of (1) the allowance the selling dealer received at the time of sale, or (2) the median dealer parts allowance in the state where the dealer operates.
For new Hyundai dealers by appointment of HMA: the parts handling allowance shall be 40%.
For all other situations: the parts handling allowance shall be 40%.

title: Reimbursement Policies for Core Charges and No Part Number (NPN) Purchases
raw_content: Specific parts (such as a remanufactured alternator assembly), will have a core charge that is a separate charge from the dealer net part price. Warranty reimbursement will be based upon the current dealer net price for the assembly, not the core value, plus the parts handling allowance applied to the dealer net price. Your parts department can give you information on core reimbursement amounts. Please contact the Dealer Parts Support Call Center at 1-800-545-4515 for status relating to these core exchanges. Your PDC will issue credit for cores on the monthly Dealer Parts Statement. 5.1.2 “No Part Number” (NPN) Purchase Reimbursement The Dealer is to maintain an inventory of new Hyundai Genuine supplied parts in sufficient quantity to enable necessary parts replacements in vehicles serviced by the Dealer. The Dealer is required to use new Hyundai Genuine parts in all warranty and customer satisfaction repairs and in the case of sublet repairs, to furnish the outside sublet repair shop with such new Hyundai Genuine parts as may be required for repairs. There are some supplies which Hyundai does not sell (i.e. battery acid, etc.) and others that Hyundai sells that are also available from other sources, which the Dealer or sublet shop must purchase from a source other than HMA. Reimbursement for such items will be based on the allowances shown in the Warranty Claim Processing Manual, with no additional parts handling allowance. title: Reimbursement Policies
raw_content: Parts returned to the WTC must be completed online at www.HyundaiDealer.com. Click WTC Part Return on either the PARTS or SERVICE page. Dealers will not be responsible for paying freight charges, account or online label fees. For more information please refer to Section 7.5 and 7.6 of this manual. Reimbursement Policies 89
coverage_details: Dealers are not responsible for paying freight charges for WTC part returns
Dealers are not responsible for paying account fees for WTC part returns
Dealers are not responsible for paying online label fees for WTC part returns

Click 'WTC Part Return' on either the PARTS or SERVICE page

title: 12V Battery Warranty and Maintenance for Dealer Aged Inventory
raw_content: A battery in a stored vehicle is subject to conditions that can reduce its performance and life. Regular monthly battery maintenance must be performed by the dealer to ensure proper operation and optimal battery life for the customer. A vehicle battery that is not adequately maintained during dealer storage can result in reduced battery life and customer dissatisfaction. As a matter of warranty policy (2.3.5), Hyundai does not provide warranty coverage for discharged and/or failed 12V batteries due to lapses in dealer aged inventory maintenance. For more information, please refer to section (2.3.2) Dealer Stock Inspection every 30 days (Monthly) – Not Covered by Warranty. Dealers should inspect batteries within 7 days of arrival from Hyundai. Warranty claims can only be submitted for repairs performed within 7 days of unit delivery at dealers. Claims can only be submitted using approved testing methods. For more information and relevant TSBs, please refer to section (2.3.3) 12V Lead-acid/AGM Battery Inspection and Maintenance. It is the dealer’s responsibility to maintain the battery in good condition while the vehicle is in dealer stock. Maintaining the battery in good condition will minimize the dealer’s battery replacement cost since dealer stock is not covered by Hyundai warranty. The regular upkeep of the battery will also ensure optimal battery performance for the customer. The following TSBs contains battery tester information and warranty related procedures. - 24-EE-014H – 12V BATTERY MAINTENANCE FOR INVENTORY & PDI - 24-EE-013H – WEBDCS AGED INVENTORY – BLUELINK SOC ALERTS - 25-EE-003H – GUIDE TO BATTERY DIAGNOSTIC TSBS Note: The TSBs listed above are subject to change; for the most up-to-date version(s) please visit www.HyundaiDealer.com under the Service tab/Service Programs/Hyundai Tech Info/TSB. For unusual battery situations, such as a leaking battery, please contact the WCC using the Warranty Support Portal or by phone at 877-446-2922 for assistance. Dealer Battery Maintenance Schedule (per TSB 24-EE-014H): Note: Hyundai highly recommends storing dealer stock vehicles in “Shipping Mode” to help reduce battery current draw and conserve Battery State of Charge (SOC%) while vehicles are in dealer lot or storage location. For more information on Shipping Mode, please review TSB 24-EE-012H-1. Dealer Option - Covered by Warranty: Within 7 days of vehicle delivery to dealer: Reimbursement Policies 90 Inspect the battery condition using Battery Test/Claim App on GDS Smart, referencing TSB 25-EE-003H. Warranty claim submissions must use the approved process described in the TSB. Take the appropriate action based on the test results: Battery Condition Action D00 or Good Battery (Cadex) No action required (covered under PDI) D01: Parasitic Draw Detected (non-sleep communications modules) D02: Parasitic Draw Detected (Vehicle Device Short) D03: Vehicle in ACC mode depleting battery D04: Vehicle not operated often or Charge & Retest (Cadex) Notes: • D02 tests for inventory units may be submitted to PCR as this is an edge case condition. This code is normally not warrantable for retailed units. • D03/D04 is not warrantable. Diagnose, repair the specified Battery Condition and utilize either of the following methods to charge the battery: - Run at engine idle with the headlights ON or use battery charger; charge time will be dependent upon battery condition and vehicle model Note: Turning the headlights ON induces maximum charging rate of the battery. - Charging can be conducted by automatic regulated battery charger. Hyundai recommends DCA8000, Associated Intellimatic or Schumacher DSR124 chargers. - Check the battery again after charging. Battery test result must yield, “Good Battery”. Warranty claim submission notes: - Save the GDS Battery Diagnosis test result for your records. The results should be uploaded to the server automatically. - For QX Venue, the Cadex tool per TSB 25-EE- 005H, two warranty codes from the battery test result printout below must be submitted with the claim using GDS Smart: o Initial “Charge & Retest” result before charging. This test is sent to servers automatically o “Good Battery” or “Replace Battery” result printout from DCA-8000 after charging must be scanned and submitted to HMA using Battery Test/Claim App. R01/R02/R03/R04 or Replace Battery (Cadex) Replace Battery - Save the GDS Battery Diagnosis test result for your records. The results should be uploaded to the server automatically. - Retain the battery for WTC collections per 24- EE-009H Dealer Required - Not Covered by Warranty: Dealer stock unit battery maintenance for every 30 days (monthly), every 90 days (3 months), and in conjunction with WEBDCS Aged inventory - Bluelink SOC Alerts (TSB 24-EE-013H). – Check the battery condition with Hyundai’s required diagnostic tools: Reimbursement Policies 91 Battery Condition Action Good Battery No action required. Charge & Retest Use either option “A” or “B” below to charge the battery. A. Engine Idle with Headlights ON B. Automatic Regulated Battery Charger Charging time will depend on the battery state of charge (SOC) and vehicle model. After charging, check the battery again with a Hyundai approved battery tester and utilize the Battery SOC information on TSB 24-EE-013H . Battery test results must yield, “Good Battery” Refer to TSB 24-EE-014H and TSB 24-EE-013H for more details. Replace Battery Replace Battery at Dealer’s expense Please note: The TSBs listed above are subject to change; for the most updated version(s), please visit www.HyundaiDealer.com under the Service Tab/Service Programs/Hyundai Tech Info/TSB. Note: There will be a warning on the instrument cluster if the vehicle is driven with the Power Fuse Switch turned off.
coverage_details: Battery inspection and necessary repairs/replacements within 7 days of vehicle delivery to the dealer.
Diagnosis and repair for parasitic draw (D01, D02) or vehicle device short (D02) identified within 7 days of delivery to the dealer (D02 may be submitted to PCR for inventory units).
Replacement of batteries (R01/R02/R03/R04) if identified within 7 days of vehicle delivery to the dealer.
Battery conditions noted as D00 or 'Good Battery' (Cadex) are covered under PDI.
Unusual battery situations, such as a leaking battery, upon contact with the Warranty Contact Center (WCC).
exclusions: Discharged and/or failed 12V batteries due to lapses in dealer aged inventory maintenance.
Dealer stock inspection every 30 days (monthly) is not covered by warranty.
Battery conditions D03 (Vehicle in ACC mode depleting battery) and D04 (Vehicle not operated often) are not warrantable.
Dealer stock unit battery maintenance (monthly or every 90 days) is not covered by Hyundai warranty.
Battery replacements required outside the initial 7-day period due to dealer stock maintenance are at the dealer’s expense.
dealer_actions: Perform regular monthly battery maintenance for vehicles in storage.
Inspect batteries within 7 days of vehicle arrival from Hyundai.
Submit warranty claims only for repairs performed within 7 days of unit delivery to the dealer, using approved testing methods.
Refer to section (2.3.2) Dealer Stock Inspection every 30 days (Monthly) and section (2.3.3) 12V Lead-acid/AGM Battery Inspection and Maintenance.
Maintain the battery in good condition while the vehicle is in dealer stock.
Contact the WCC for unusual battery situations (e.g., leaking battery) using the Warranty Support Portal or by phone (877-446-2922).
Perform Dealer Battery Maintenance Schedule per TSB 24-EE-014H.
Store dealer stock vehicles in 'Shipping Mode' to reduce battery current draw and conserve Battery State of Charge (SOC%) (refer to TSB 24-EE-012H-1).
Within 7 days of vehicle delivery to dealer: Inspect battery condition using Battery Test/Claim App on GDS Smart (referencing TSB 25-EE-003H).

For conditions D01/D02/D03/D04 identified within 7 days: Diagnose, repair the specified Battery Condition, and charge the battery using either engine idle with headlights ON or an automatic regulated battery charger.
After charging, recheck the battery; the test result must yield 'Good Battery'.
For R01/R02/R03/R04 conditions identified within 7 days: Replace the battery.
Save GDS Battery Diagnosis test results for your records (results should upload automatically to the server).

For QX Venue: Submit two warranty codes from the Cadex tool per TSB 25-EE-005H with the claim (initial 'Charge & Retest' result and 'Good Battery' or 'Replace Battery' result printout from DCA-8000 after charging).
Retain replaced batteries for WTC collections per TSB 24-EE-009H.
Perform dealer stock unit battery maintenance every 30 days (monthly), every 90 days (3 months), and in conjunction with WEBDCS Aged inventory - Bluelink SOC Alerts (TSB 24-EE-013H).
For non-covered maintenance (monthly/quarterly): Check battery condition with Hyundai’s required diagnostic tools.
If 'Charge & Retest' condition for non-covered maintenance: Charge the battery (engine idle with headlights ON or automatic regulated battery charger) and recheck for a 'Good Battery' result.
If 'Replace Battery' condition for non-covered maintenance: Replace the battery at the dealer’s expense.
Visit www.HyundaiDealer.com under the Service tab/Service Programs/Hyundai Tech Info/TSB for the most up-to-date TSBs.

title: Original Equipment Battery Warranty
raw_content: An original equipment battery found to be defective in material or workmanship will be warranted for the first 3 years from the date of original retail delivery or date of first use, or 36,000 miles, whichever occurs first.
coverage_details: Original equipment battery found to be defective in material or workmanship
Coverage for the first 3 years from the date of original retail delivery or date of first use
Coverage for 36,000 miles, whichever occurs first

title: 12V Lead-Acid/AGM Battery Warranty Diagnosis and Claim Procedures
raw_content: (2016-2025 models year vehicles NOT capable of GDS battery diagnosis) 0–36 Months/36,000 Miles 2016-2025 model year vehicles and the Venue model not capable of GDS battery diagnosis must be tested using Cadex Spectro Modular via GDS Smart Battery Test/Claim Application. For more information, please refer to section (2.3.3) 12V Lead-acid/AGM Battery Inspection and Maintenance. The fields on the Warranty screen must be correctly filled in following regular submission procedures, including the following: • Causal part is the part number of the Interstate battery. • Labor operation code as applicable: – 37110R00 for Battery Replacement – 37110A00 for Battery Diagnostic Inspection and Charging Operation – 37110A00 + 37110R00 for Charge AND Battery Replacement – Refer to TSB 25-EE-003H and 25-EE-005H • Labor operation code quantity of 1. Reimbursement Policies 92 • Part number of the replacement battery, quantity 1. (2025-2026 models year vehicles capable of GDS battery diagnosis ) 0-36 Months/36,000 Miles 2025-2026 model year vehicles capable of GDS battery diagnosis must be tested using the GDS Smart Battery Test/Claim Application. For more information, please refer to section (2.3.3) 12V Lead-acid/AGM Battery Inspection and Maintenance. The fields on the Warranty screen must be correctly filled in following regular submission procedures, including the following: • Causal part is the part number of the Interstate battery. • Labor operation code as applicable: – 37110R00 + 37110A1A for Battery Diagnostic Codes R01 and R02 – 37110R00 + 37110A00 for Battery Diagnostic Codes R03 and R04 – 37110A1A for Battery Diagnostic Inspection – 37110A00 for Battery Diagnostic Inspection and Charging Performance Test • Diagnostic Code D01 • Labor operation code quantity of 1. • Part number of the replacement battery, quantity 1. Note: The TSBs listed above are subject to change; for the most up-to-date version(s) please visit www.HyundaiDealer.com under the Service tab/Service Programs/Hyundai Tech Info/TSB.

title: 12V Lead-acid/AGM Batteries (Over-the-Counter)
raw_content: 12V Lead-acid/AGM batteries purchased over-the-counter are warranted for 48 months/unlimited mileage from the date of purchase. For the first 12 months HMA will cover the full dealer net cost of the battery, plus the applicable parts handling allowance. During the 13th through 48th month, the battery is prorated. The Warranty Claim System will prorate the battery based on the submitted detail part. Labor is not covered on an over-the-counter sale.
coverage_details: Warranty period of 48 months/unlimited mileage from the date of purchase
Full dealer net cost of the battery plus applicable parts handling allowance covered for the first 12 months
Battery prorated from 13th through 48th month
exclusions: Labor costs on an over-the-counter sale
dealer_actions: Submit detail part to the Warranty Claim System for proration

title: Dealer Installed Replacement 12V Lead-acid/AGM Batteries
raw_content: Dealer installed replacement 12V Lead-acid/AGM batteries are warranted for 48 months/unlimited mileage from the date of purchase. For the first 12 months HMA will cover the full dealer net cost of the battery, plus the applicable parts handling allowance, and the labor to remove and replace the battery. During the 13th through 48th month the battery will be reimbursed at a prorated amount and the labor is not covered under warranty.
coverage_details: Dealer installed replacement 12V Lead-acid/AGM batteries
Warranted for 48 months/unlimited mileage from the date of purchase
For the first 12 months: Full dealer net cost of the battery covered
For the first 12 months: Applicable parts handling allowance covered
For the first 12 months: Labor to remove and replace the battery covered
During the 13th through 48th month: Battery reimbursed at a prorated amount
exclusions: Labor for battery replacement during the 13th through 48th month is not covered under warranty

title: Prior Approval Requirements
raw_content: Hyundai’s Prior Approval (PA) Center provides a centralized location that authorizes Warranty and Goodwill Repairs. Prior Approval is required for certain repairs (5.4.1) prior to performing the repair. As a reminder, the required claim preparation procedures, as outlined in other sections of the P&P including other HMA publications (i.e. Campaign Literature, Technical Service Bulletin, etc.) must also be adhered to; a Prior Approval will not serve as a “blanket” approval and the claim may still be subject to denial or chargeback for failure to comply with the claim preparation procedures. Dealer shall adhere to the following: • Submit PA request within 2 business days (Monday to Friday) of R.O. open date. • Respond to PA return within 1 business day (Monday to Friday). • Order part(s) within 1 business day (Monday to Friday) of PA approval. Note: Refer to SRC allowance guidelines for max days allowed for repair completion.
coverage_details: Hyundai's Prior Approval (PA) Center authorizes Warranty and Goodwill Repairs.
Prior Approval is required for certain repairs (5.4.1) before performing the repair.
exclusions: Prior Approval will not serve as a "blanket" approval.
Claims may still be subject to denial or chargeback for failure to comply with required claim preparation procedures (e.g., outlined in Campaign Literature, Technical Service Bulletins).
dealer_actions: Submit PA request within 2 business days (Monday to Friday) of R.O. open date.
Respond to PA return within 1 business day (Monday to Friday).
Order part(s) within 1 business day (Monday to Friday) of PA approval.
Refer to SRC allowance guidelines for maximum days allowed for repair completion.

title: Prior Approval and Goodwill Repair Policies
raw_content: Dealers are required to submit a Prior Approval for authorization, prior to performing the following repairs: – Engine Assembly (sub engine assembly and short engine assembly) – Transmission/Transaxle Assembly (manual, automatic, IVT and DCT) – Electric High Voltage Battery – Engine Assembly, Transmission Assembly or Electric High Voltage Battery sold to a third-party repair facility – Combustion Chamber Cleaning (Oil Consumption) – All Goodwill related repairs including: o Campaign related repairs (consequential and previously completed recall repairs) o Goodwill over HSA limits (5.4.6) o Concerns reported while vehicle was in warranty o For latest updates, check the WebDCS Messages and Alerts When replacing Engines or Transmissions, dealers must adhere to the Transmission and Engine Programs outlined in sections 5.6.3, 5.6.4 & 5.6.5. HMA reserves the right to furnish the final decisions in all Goodwill matters. HMA encourages Goodwill participation from both Dealerships and/or Customers in the cost of the repairs. HMA may participate only in a portion of the cost of the repairs (i.e. Only Parts or Labor, a Limited Dollar Amount, or a Percentage of the Total Amount). HMA participation in Goodwill repairs will Reimbursement Policies 96 always be Parts at Warranty pricing and Labor times as per HMA LTS, unless otherwise stated or required under applicable state law. Note : If a previously completed Recall needs to be performed again and the vehicle is out of warranty, in order to ensure that the Recall is performed at no expense to the customer, please submit a Prior Approval Request for goodwill consideration prior to performing the work. Check the WebDCS Messages and Alerts for the latest updates.
coverage_details: Goodwill related repairs
Campaign related repairs (consequential and previously completed recall repairs) under goodwill
Goodwill over HSA limits (5.4.6)
Concerns reported while vehicle was in warranty (for goodwill consideration)
Previously completed Recalls needing re-performance when the vehicle is out of warranty (for goodwill consideration)
HMA may participate in a portion of the cost of goodwill repairs (e.g., Only Parts or Labor, a Limited Dollar Amount, or a Percentage of the Total Amount)

title: Prior Approval Requests
raw_content: Prior to starting any repairs which require Prior Approval (5.4.1), dealers must submit a PA request from WebDCS. To locate the PA Request under WebDCS, find the Service tab, access the PA Request link and then create and submit a new request. Status updates of other PA requests in Approved, Saved, In Progress, Returned or Declined, can also be found under the PA Request link. Comments for the PA Request Reason: • Engine PA Requests Include: – Repairs Needed & Cause of Malfunction – Condition/Level of the Oil/Signs of Sludge/Metal Contamination – New or Reman; include Part Number – Breakdown of Parts and Labor • Transmission PA Requests Include: – Repairs Needed & Cause of Malfunction – DTC Codes – New or Reman; include Part Number – Breakdown of Parts and Labor • Electric High Voltage Battery PA Requests Include: – Techline advised replacement – DTC Codes – Breakdown of Parts and Labor • Goodwill PA Requests Include: – Repairs Needed – Why warranty is not applicable – Reason for the Goodwill Note: The list above is not all-inclusive and is subject to change. Required Attachments: All PA requests require repair order (RO), front & back, including technician comments. Additional required attachments may include photos and documents as requested by PA Center. Submitted photos must be digital, colored, high-resolution, original and unedited. Reimbursement Policies 97 Photocopies of photos are not acceptable. Common photo file formats should be used: e.g. JPEG, PNG, BMP, TIFF. • Engine PA Requests Must Include: – Completed digital Engine Diagnosis Worksheet, including the engine serial number from the removed engine and the Service Manager’s signature. – Copy of customer’s current and up-to-date (not expired) owner registration from Department of Motor Vehicles (DMV) to validate 10/100K Powertrain Warranty. Note: Dealers should check that the removed engine core’s serial number matches the OE engine serial number in WebDCS. If it does not match, dealers should notify PA of the removed engine core serial number as part of the PA review/approval process. Failure to notify PA if the engine’s core serial number does not match can result in denial of claim approval or subject to claim chargeback. • Transmission PA Requests Must Include: – Transmission Diagnosis sheet, completely filled out (Use the latest version of the Diagnosis sheet posted in Documents library on www.HyundaiDealer.com) – Copy of customer’s current and up-to-date (not expired) owner registration from Department of Motor Vehicles (DMV) to validate 10/100K Powertrain Warranty. ) Note: The list above is not all-inclusive and is subject to change. Please review IMPORTANT NOTES below regarding possible reasons for denial of reimbursement claim: • Failure to confirm vehicle and/or customer eligibility • Failure to submit the required PA Request and obtain Prior Approval can result in denial of the dealership’s reimbursement claim. • Reusing photos from another vehicle will result in denial of the dealer’s reimbursement claim. • Failure to disclose information related to warranty eligibility • Obscene, offensive, lewd or derogatory media submissions (including audio submissions) • Falsified documentation • Using Techline / Field Service Engineering repair recommendation as approval in lieu of approved PA request for warranty coverage or goodwill assistance. Note: The list above is not all-inclusive and is subject to change.

title: Reimbursement Policies
raw_content: Reimbursement Policies 98 In instance of dealer buy/sell, submit a new PA request utilizing the new dealer code for any vehicle where the claim will be submitted after the previous Dealer’s ‘end’ date and a PA number is required on the claim submission (i.e., campaign engine claims). If a new PA is needed, the only required documentation for the new PA submission is a copy of the repair order from the roll-up (new) Dealer. The related supporting documentation will be referenced from the previous Dealer approval. Any pending or new PA requests needed after dealer’s termination date must be submitted as new PA requests utilizing the new dealer code.

title: HSA Dealer Self-Authorization Program
raw_content: HMA’s dealer self-authorization program is referred to as HSA (Hyundai Dealer Self- Authorization). This dealer self-authorization semi-annual program enables qualifying and enrolled dealerships to perform specific out-of-warranty and/or selected warranty repairs. In addition, the associated claims for these repairs are processed directly upon claim submission. HSA Dealers may self-authorize Goodwill repairs within the following HSA limits: • Repairs beyond coverage but within 12 months/12,000 miles of New Vehicle Limited Warranty (60 months/60,000 miles) & Powertrain Warranty (120 months/100,000 miles) components* • Total repair amount per visit is $1,000.00 or less *Replacement of engine and transmission assemblies require Prior Approval. If any HSA limits are exceeded, dealers must submit for Prior Approval (5.4.1 & 5.4.2). Goodwill claims exceeding any HSA limits, total repair amount per visit/months/mileage, and submitted without obtaining Prior Approval are subject to denial or chargeback. Examples when HSA Dealers can self-authorize Goodwill repairs: • New Vehicle Limited Warranty Repairs beyond 60 months/60,000 miles, but within 72 months/72,000 miles from date of first use • Powertrain Warranty Repairs beyond 72 months/72,000 miles, but within 72 months/72,000 miles from date of first use, for second and/or subsequent owner * • Powertrain Warranty Repairs beyond 120 months/100,000 miles, but within 132 months/112,000 miles from date of first use, for original owner * • Federal emission repairs beyond 96 months/80,000 miles, but within 108 months/92,000 miles from date of first use Reimbursement Policies 99 • California emission repairs beyond 84 months/70,000 miles, but within 96 months/82,000 miles from date of first use • Parts & Accessories Warranty repairs beyond 12 months/12,000 miles, but within 24 months/24,000 miles from date of original customer purchase • Bluetooth, Navigation, Radio and Sound System repairs: - Beginning with 2016MY: Repairs beyond 60 months/60,000 miles, but within 72 months/72,000 miles from date of first use - Prior to 2016MY: Repairs beyond 36 months/36,000 miles, but within 48 months/48,000 miles from date of first use (excluding 2013-2015MY Equus which is covered for 60 months/60,000 miles) • Maintenance and miscellaneous repairs such as belts/brake pads/bulbs beyond 12 months/12,000 miles, but within 24 months/24,000 miles from date of first use ** Note: The list above is not all-inclusive and is subject to change. *Replacement of engine and transmission assemblies require Prior Approval. **Maintenance repairs for Equus not performed as part of a scheduled maintenance as outlined in the Equus Service Advocate QRG is not warranty, see 4.1.15. Any part being replaced due to wear is not eligible for HSA, see 5.4.8. Note: Eligible HSA self-authorized claims submitted by HSA actively enrolled dealers will automatically be identified and processed as self-authorized claims.
