title: HSA Program Eligibility Criteria and Reimbursement Policy
raw_content: *HSA Eligibility Criteria are reviewed on a frequent basis throughout the year and are subject to change. Dealership eligibility for enrollment in the semi-annual HSA program is based on the following criteria: 1. Average Warranty Operational Performance Review (WOPR) ranking is in the top 550 of Hyundai Dealers in most recent 6-month period. AND 2. Average Hyundai Service Experience (HVX) greater than or equal to 835 in most recent 6-month period. HSA Dealer eligibility and participation will be reviewed and determined semi-annually by HMA. A Dealer that has been previously removed from participation in HSA is eligible for reinstatement (participation) in HSA upon the Dealership meeting or exceeding the HMA Service Objectives, indicated above, for the period under review. Reimbursement Policies 100 Region Management can deny the participation in HSA of any “eligible” Dealership, at any time, provided that Region Management has “just cause” for such denial, such as: • Excessive number of vehicles bought back involving Dealership • New Service Management at Dealership HSA participation start date(s) and removal date(s) are based upon date of repair. Claims meeting HSA parameters with repair completion dates prior to the effective date of active participation will not be processed/accepted as HSA claims. Claims meeting HSA parameters with repair completion dates after the effective date of removal from HSA participation will not be processed/accepted as HSA claims. Claims submitted by participating HSA Dealers that exceed the HSA program self-authorization parameters, will automatically be reviewed by the PA Center for authorization/approval or denial.
coverage_details: Dealerships with an average Warranty Operational Performance Review (WOPR) ranking in the top 550 of Hyundai Dealers in the most recent 6-month period.
Dealerships with an average Hyundai Service Experience (HVX) greater than or equal to 835 in the most recent 6-month period.
Dealers previously removed from HSA participation are eligible for reinstatement upon meeting or exceeding HMA Service Objectives for the review period.
exclusions: HSA eligibility criteria are reviewed frequently and are subject to change.
Denial of participation by Region Management for "just cause" (e.g., excessive vehicle buybacks involving the Dealership, new Service Management).
Claims with repair completion dates prior to the effective date of active HSA participation will not be processed/accepted.
Claims with repair completion dates after the effective date of removal from HSA participation will not be processed/accepted.
dealer_actions: Ensure the dealership maintains an average Warranty Operational Performance Review (WOPR) ranking in the top 550 of Hyundai Dealers.
Maintain an average Hyundai Service Experience (HVX) score of 835 or greater.
Submit claims only for repairs completed within the dealership's active HSA participation period.
Be aware that claims exceeding HSA program self-authorization parameters will automatically be reviewed by the PA Center for authorization/approval or denial.

title: Dealer Self-Authorization (HSA) Policy
raw_content: The following types of repairs are to be considered under HSA (Dealer Self-authorization): • Goodwill repairs to be considered only on a case-by-case basis • Warranty repairs must be the direct result of a defect in Hyundai supplied material or Hyundai Factory workmanship • Replacement of a complete assembly with a new assembly when a remanufactured assembly is either not available or the applicable State disclosure laws for a “new” vehicle requires replacement using a new, rather than a used part • Goodwill cost splits/sharing (Customer and/or Dealer contributions to repair cost) • Warranty extension repairs (e.g. clock spring, MDPS Coupling) The following types of repairs/services are NOT to be considered under HSA (Dealer Self- Authorization): • Reconditioning of a used vehicle or repairs to a vehicle in Dealer stock • Repairs for excess expense due to Dealership Technician or Sublet Shop error or inefficiency • Goodwill repairs for a relative, or friend, when such a relationship is the sole basis for the goodwill repair • Repairs to a vehicle for which the actual mileage cannot be determined • Repairs to a vehicle that has a canceled warranty • Repairs to a vehicle that has been abused or used in a manner that is inconsistent with “normal” driving conditions or use • Repairs or components which are not covered under HMA warranty (e.g. Tires) • Repairs that clearly are outside of the warranty period that are due to vehicle: – Abuse – Commercial use – Modification from original factory specification Reimbursement Policies 101 – Normal wear or maintenance related (e.g. fluid changes, belts, spark plugs, brake pads and oil changes-including additional services not covered under the Equus Maintenance Program and Genesis Maintenance Program) – Not being maintained – Covered under an existing Extended Service Contract or similar plan – Deductible for repairs (e.g. repairs covered by Extended Service Contract or Insurance plans) – Gifts, Gift cards or Coupons Note: The list above is not all-inclusive and is subject to change.
coverage_details: Goodwill repairs (considered on a case-by-case basis)

Replacement of a complete assembly with a new assembly when a remanufactured assembly is not available
Replacement of a complete assembly with a new assembly when applicable State disclosure laws for a 'new' vehicle require replacement using a new part

exclusions: Reconditioning of a used vehicle or repairs to a vehicle in Dealer stock
Repairs for excess expense due to Dealership Technician or Sublet Shop error or inefficiency

Repairs to a vehicle for which the actual mileage cannot be determined
Repairs to a vehicle that has a canceled warranty
Repairs to a vehicle that has been abused or used in a manner inconsistent with 'normal' driving conditions or use
Repairs or components not covered under HMA warranty (e.g. Tires)
Repairs clearly outside of the warranty period due to vehicle abuse

title: Anti-Perforation Limited Warranty
raw_content: If a vehicle has a rust hole (from the inside out), through a body panel from corrosion due to defects in material or workmanship, the following procedures must be followed: • The Dealer is to inspect the vehicle to determine if the rust is due to a perforation condition. If there is a perforation, the Dealer must complete the Anti-Perforation Limited Warranty Vehicle Inspection Report (NP430-00000_10-04), available on www.HyundaiDealer.com under the Service tab / Dealer Resources / Documents Library /Warranty / Anti-Perf Inspection Report (NP430-00000_10-04). The Anti-Perforation Limited Warranty Vehicle Inspection Report should be attached to the corresponding repair order. If the Inspection Report is not available upon request, the claim may be subject to chargeback. Note: Surface corrosion or surface rust is covered for the first 12 months from the date of original retail delivery or date of first use or 12,000 miles, whichever occurs first. • The Dealer should submit the anti-perforation claim to HMA following normal WebDCS claim submission procedures. – Causal Part The causal part is the part number of the panel that caused the owner’s complaint and the need for the repair. If more than one panel is affected, the panel that has the majority of the perforation evident should be listed as the causal part. – Nature and Cause Code Use the applicable nature and cause codes, such as A12 and ZZ8. – Sublet Code Use B2 - body repair. Reimbursement Policies 102 • The Anti-Perforation Limited Warranty Vehicle Inspection Report, repair estimate and repair final invoice should be attached to the hard copy of the repair order and retained by the Dealer following normal warranty document retention policy and procedures. Reimbursement Policies 103
coverage_details: Rust hole (from the inside out) through a body panel from corrosion due to defects in material or workmanship.
dealer_actions: Inspect the vehicle to determine if the rust is due to a perforation condition.

If perforation is present, complete the Anti-Perforation Limited Warranty Vehicle Inspection Report (NP430-00000_10-04) from www.HyundaiDealer.com (Service tab / Dealer Resources / Documents Library /Warranty / Anti-Perf Inspection Report).
Attach the completed Inspection Report to the corresponding repair order.
Submit the anti-perforation claim to HMA following normal WebDCS claim submission procedures.
For claim submission, list the panel with the majority of perforation evident as the causal part.
For claim submission, use applicable nature and cause codes (e.g., A12 and ZZ8).
For claim submission, use sublet code B2 (body repair).
Attach the Anti-Perforation Limited Warranty Vehicle Inspection Report, repair estimate, and repair final invoice to the hard copy of the repair order.
Retain all warranty documents following normal retention policy and procedures.

title: Dealer Direct Exchange (DDE) Audio Video Navigation Unit Program
raw_content: 1. The dealer direct exchange manufacturer will stock and ship all remanufactured audio video and navigation units. Ordering of these units will take place using the DCS system. Audio Video Navigation Resources are available at Hyundaidealer.com>Parts>Document Library > Reference Materials > Audio Video Navigation Return and Replacement Program To Exchange Unit (in warranty and out of warranty units) – At time of shipment, HMA will invoice the Dealer at zero ($0.00) cost for the DDE unit. If the order is for an out of warranty unit, a Repair Fee will be charged at time of shipment. • If the inoperative (core) unit is returned to the manufacturer within 45 calendar days of DDE unit ship date, no subsequent charges will be filed. • If the core is not returned to the manufacturer by the 45th calendar day of DDE unit ship date, the DDE unit’s Dealer Net Price will be charged on the 46th day. • If the core is returned to the manufacturer between 46 – 90 calendar days of DDE unit ship date, the DDE unit’s Dealer Net Price will be credited, and a Late Return Fee will be charged. • If the core is returned to the manufacturer after 90 calendar days of DDE unit ship date, the core will not be accepted, and a credit will not be issued. – When returning the core, include a copy of the dealer Repair Order showing the VIN, mileage, and installed part number. 2. Package the inoperative core unit in the carton in which the remanufactured replacement unit was shipped. Then immediately ship, using the provided prepaid FedEx Ground shipping label, the inoperative core, along with a copy of the dealer Repair Order to the manufacturer. The unit will be processed upon receipt and confirmation that the repair falls within the warranty guidelines of the Dealer Direct Exchange AVN Program. – A Repair Fee will be charged for units found to be unwarrantable (out of warranty, modified, damaged due to customer abuse or misuse). 3. Do not attempt any type of repair on these units. If a CD/DVD or any other object becomes stuck or trapped in the unit, do not attempt to remove it . If the customer requests that their CD or other object be returned, fill out the diagnostic form and check the appropriate box in the customer complaint analysis section, requesting the CD/DVD or other object be returned to your dealership, so you may return the item(s) to the customer ( Damage from forcible removal will negate the warranty ). Otherwise, the CD or other object will be disposed of. If the dealership believes that the damage is the customer’s responsibility, the dealership should discuss the matter with the customer. In Reimbursement Policies 104 many cases of customer caused damages, the customer’s auto insurance policy may provide the customer with coverage. HMA recommends that the Dealer communicate this to the customer. Customer caused damage is not warrantable. 4. Submit a warranty claim through the DCS system for the replacement labor. A. Use the labor operation code from HMA LTS that is applicable to the VIN and DDE unit part number, description: radio assembly, qty: 1. Do not use any other labor operation in addition, or substitution, to the one prescribed above. B. Do not charge out the DDE unit as a detail part. If you return the core with 45 calendar days from ship date, you will not be charged for the part. If the core is not returned by the 45th day from ship date, you will be charged for the Dealer Net cost of the replacement radio on your parts statement separately. Audio video and navigation core units are to be returned via prepaid shipping label (FedEx Ground) provided by the manufacturer. For all audio video and navigation replacements, the following must be adhered to: – DDE units must be used unless any of the following are true: • Vehicle has less than 500 miles. • Specific Legal requirement by State Governing Agency. • DDE unit is unavailable or on backorder (parts availability screen print must be attached to the subject repair order to document this condition). • Repeat repair or customer satisfaction - Regional approval is required. – Please note: If dealer substitutes a float unit for an OE unit, DDE billing rules and core return procedures will apply. 5. DDE Repair Facilities • LG/Specmo - Phone: 800-545-7910 or customerservice@specmo.com • MPA/MRC - Phone: 909-456-3798 or RC1_MRC@mobisusa.com • Harman/United Radio - Phone: 800-448-0944 or contact@unitedradio.com
coverage_details: Manufacturer stocks and ships all remanufactured audio, video, and navigation units.
Exchange of in-warranty and out-of-warranty units is supported.

No subsequent charges will be filed if the inoperative (core) unit is returned to the manufacturer within 45 calendar days of the DDE unit ship date.

Replacement labor can be submitted as a warranty claim through the DCS system using the applicable labor operation code from HMA LTS.

title: Transmission Replacement and Core Return Program Guidelines
raw_content: For all Transmission replacements, the following must be adhered to: • Remanufactured transmission must be used unless any of the following are true: – Vehicle has less than 500 miles. – Specific Legal requirement by a state governing agency. – Reman part is unavailable or on backorder (parts availability screen print from WebDCS must be attached to the subject repair order to document this condition). - Reman part ordered but system converted to new part order due to no reman inventory available at the facing PDC (the order status inquiry screen in WebDCS that shows that the OE part has supersession with an Advice Code “A” must be attached to the subject repair order to document this condition.) The process to submitting a screenshot is as follows: 1. Go to HyundaiDealer.com/WebDCS/Orders/Order Status Inquiry 2. Select the Original Order Number submitted 3. Take a screenshot and attach it to the RO By utilizing Hyundai’s DCS Online Website at www.HyundaiDealer.com , click on WebDCS, Parts tab, Core Returns to enter core returns and request pickups. MOBIS Parts America’s Dedicated Delivery Service carriers (MPA DDS) will arrange for pick-up and delivery to a MPA DDS core return center where the cores will be inspected, processed, credits authorized, and electronically transmitted to HMA. Do not submit a Warranty Freight Claim for these charges. As HMA is paying all Freight Charges under this program, it is important to note the following Program Guideline Updates: • Go to www.HyundaiDealer.com , then click on WebDCS, Parts tab, Core Returns to initiate the core pickups through MPA DDS. • The Reman Vendor will not accept any direct shipments of transmissions from dealers. Use the MPA DDS program for all transmissions, including WTC returns. • If you have any problems using the website, call your Hyundai Parts Dealer Support Group at 1-800-545-4515 or MOBIS Parts Portal • Dealers should remember to print and include with their pickups, the Bill of Lading (BOL) and Packing List from the Website. This will assist in timely processing. Reimbursement Policies 106 • A returned transmission core should be fully assembled, including the torque converter, motors, actuator, etc. and all plastic/rubber plugs, and the core should be completely drained of all fluids. • Include the following completed forms with the returned core. Failure to adhere to these requirements will result in the chargeback of the core credit and possibly the warranty claim. – Transmission Diagnosis Worksheet. Worksheets are available on Hyundaidealer.com/WebDCS/Parts/Core Returns Diagnosis Worksheets. It is also available on www. HyundaiDealer.com/Service/Documents Library/Service/Reman ATM and Engine Core Return and Diagnosis Forms – Core Return Form – Warranty Parts Tag (part number NP445-00011). Sample in section 7.3. – Dealer’s Repair Order must include detailed condition, cause and correction information. – Final Invoice (if over the counter sale). • Additionally, if a warranty claim is submitted and the transmission core was not returned to a MPA DDS core return center, the warranty claim will be charged back to the dealer. • Dealers will be billed the freight pickup & recovery costs for all invalid returned cores. Before and after readings/measurements must be recorded on the Transmission Diagnosis Worksheet whenever the GDS is used. Second and subsequent remanufactured transmission replacements on the same VIN will require a case to be opened with Tech Line 1-800-325-6604 prior to repair and Prior Approval Center approval/authorization for the claim payment. Your adherence to program guidelines will ensure the program continues to run effectively. If you have any questions concerning the Reman Core Return Program, contact your HMA Parts Dealer Support Center at 1-800-545-4515.
coverage_details: Remanufactured transmission must be used for replacements
New transmission can be used if vehicle has less than 500 miles

New transmission can be used if remanufactured part is unavailable or on backorder

exclusions: Direct shipments of transmissions from dealers to the Reman Vendor are not accepted

Warranty claims will be charged back if the transmission core was not returned to an MPA DDS core return center
Dealers will be billed freight pickup & recovery costs for all invalid returned cores

title: Engine Replacement and Core Return Policy
raw_content: For all engine replacements, the following must be adhered to: • Remanufactured engine must be used unless any of the following are true: – Vehicle is covered under the 5 year 60,000 miles New Vehicle Limited Warranty (NVLW)-use new engine, see section 5.6.5. – Specific Legal requirement by a state governing agency. – Reman part is unavailable or on backorder (parts availability screen print from WebDCS must be attached to the subject repair order to document this condition). Reimbursement Policies 107 - Reman part ordered but system converted to new part order due to no reman inventory available at the facing PDC (order status inquiry screen in WebDCS to show that the OE part has supersession with an Advice Code “A” must be attached to the subject repair order to document this condition.) 1. Go to HyundaiDealer.com/WebDCS/Orders/Order Status Inquiry 2. Select the Original Order Number submitted 3. Take a screenshot and attach it to the RO By utilizing Hyundai’s DCS Online Website at www.HyundaiDealer.com, click on WebDCS, Parts tab, Core Returns to enter core returns and request pickups. MPA DDS will arrange for pick-up and delivery to a MPA DDS core return center where the cores will be inspected, processed, credits authorized, and electronically transmitted to HMA. As HMA is paying all Freight Charges under this program, it is important to note the following Program Guideline Updates: • Go to www.HyundaiDealer.com , then click on WebDCS, Parts tab, Core Returns to initiate the core pickups through MPA DDS. • The reman vendor will not accept any direct shipments of Engines from dealers. Use the MPA DDS program for all engines, including WTC returns. • If you have any problems using the website, call your Hyundai Parts Dealer Support Group at 1-800-545-4515 or MOBIS Parts Portal • Dealers should remember to print and include with their pickups, the Bill of Lading (BOL) and Packing List from the Website. This will assist in timely processing. • Damage to the return core due to improper removal process or insufficient packaging may lead to chargeback of the core charge (if applicable) and warranty claim. Refer to section 7.5.1 for details on Engine Shipping Procedure. • If a warranty claim is submitted and the Engine Core was not returned to a MPA DDS core return center, the warranty claim will be charged back to the dealer. • Dealers will be billed the freight pickup & recovery costs for all invalid returned cores. Second and subsequent remanufactured engine replacements on the same VIN will require a case to be opened with Tech Line 1-800-325-6604 prior to repair and Prior Approval Center approval/authorization for the claim payment. Your adherence to program guidelines will ensure the program continues to run effectively. If you have any questions concerning the Reman Core Return Program, contact your HMA Parts Dealer Support Center at 1-800-545-4515.
coverage_details: Remanufactured engines must be used for engine replacements as a default.
New engines can be used if the vehicle is covered under the 5 year 60,000 miles New Vehicle Limited Warranty (NVLW).
New engines can be used if required by a specific Legal requirement from a state governing agency.
New engines can be used if the remanufactured part is unavailable or on backorder.
New engines can be used if a remanufactured part was ordered but the system converted to a new part order due to no reman inventory at the facing PDC.
Engine core returns are managed through the MPA DDS program.
HMA pays all freight charges for valid engine core returns under this program.
exclusions: Damage to a returned core due to improper removal or insufficient packaging may lead to chargeback of core charge and warranty claim.
Warranty claims for engine replacement will be charged back if the engine core was not returned to an MPA DDS core return center.
Dealers will be billed freight pickup and recovery costs for all invalid returned cores.
The remanufactured vendor will not accept direct shipments of engines from dealers.
dealer_actions: For new engine usage due to reman unavailability or backorder, attach a parts availability screen print from WebDCS to the repair order.

For new engine usage due to system conversion from reman to new part order, attach an order status inquiry screen print from WebDCS showing OE part supersession with an Advice Code 'A' to the repair order.

To obtain the WebDCS order status inquiry screen print: Go to HyundaiDealer.com/WebDCS/Orders/Order Status Inquiry, select the Original Order Number, and take a screenshot to attach to the repair order.
Utilize HyundaiDealer.com (WebDCS, Parts tab, Core Returns) to enter core returns and request pickups through MPA DDS for all engines, including WTC returns.
Print and include the Bill of Lading (BOL) and Packing List from the Website with core pickups.
Refer to section 7.5.1 for details on the Engine Shipping Procedure.

For second and subsequent remanufactured engine replacements on the same VIN, open a case with Tech Line (1-800-325-6604) prior to repair and obtain Prior Approval Center approval/authorization for claim payment.
For problems using the core return website, call the Hyundai Parts Dealer Support Group at 1-800-545-4515 or MOBIS Parts Portal.
For questions concerning the Reman Core Return Program, contact the HMA Parts Dealer Support Center at 1-800-545-4515.

title: Engine and Transmission Replacement Policy
raw_content: Replacement of an engine while the vehicle is covered under the 5 year 60,000 miles New Vehicle Limited Warranty (NVLW) will be done with a new engine. If a new engine is not available, a reman engine may be used with customer’s written consent. Reimbursement Policies 108 Second and subsequent engine and transmission replacements on the same VIN will require a case to be opened with Tech Line 1-800-325-6604 prior to repair and Prior Approval Center approval/authorization for the claim payment. Replacement of an engine for a retailed vehicle outside of the 5 year 60,000 miles NVLW will be done with a reman engine except in the case of any specific legal requirement by a state government agency. Refer to section 5.6.4 for the complete engine reman program policy. Replacement of a transmission for a retailed vehicle will be done with a reman transmission except in the case of any specific legal requirement by a state government agency. Refer to section 5.6.3 for the complete transmission reman program policy.
coverage_details: Engine replacement for vehicles under 5-year/60,000-mile New Vehicle Limited Warranty (NVLW) will be done with a new engine.
If a new engine is not available, a remanufactured engine may be used with customer's written consent.
Engine replacement for retailed vehicles outside of the 5-year/60,000-mile NVLW will be done with a remanufactured engine, except in cases of specific legal requirements by a state government agency.
Transmission replacement for retailed vehicles will be done with a remanufactured transmission, except in cases of specific legal requirements by a state government agency.
dealer_actions: If a new engine is unavailable for an NVLW vehicle, obtain the customer's written consent for using a remanufactured engine.
For second and subsequent engine and transmission replacements on the same VIN, open a case with Tech Line (1-800-325-6604) prior to repair.
For second and subsequent engine and transmission replacements on the same VIN, obtain Prior Approval Center approval/authorization for claim payment.
Refer to section 5.6.4 for the complete engine remanufactured program policy.
Refer to section 5.6.3 for the complete transmission remanufactured program policy.

title: Remanufactured Parts Program for ALT, PSC, RAC, STR
raw_content: Starters (STR) Reman Program For all ALT, PSC, RAC and STR replacements, the following must be adhered to: • Remanufactured ALT, PSG, RAC and STR must be used unless any of the following are true: – Vehicle has less than 500 miles – Specific Legal requirement by a state governing agency. – Reman part is unavailable or on backorder (parts availability screen print from WebDCS must be attached to the subject repair order to document this condition). – Repeat repair or customer satisfaction - Regional approval is required. By utilizing Hyundai’s DCS Online Website at www.HyundaiDealer.com, click on WebDCS, Parts tab, Core Returns to enter core returns and request pickups. YRC will arrange for pick-up and delivery to a YRC Core Return Center where the cores will be inspected, processed, credits authorized, and electronically transmitted to HMA. As HMA is paying all Freight Charges under this program, it is important to note the following Program Guideline Updates: • Go to www.HyundaiDealer.com, then click on WebDCS, Parts tab, Core Returns to initiate the core pickups through YRC • If you have any problems using the website, call your Hyundai Parts Dealer Support Group at 1-800-545-4515 or yrcreverselogistics@yrcw.com Do not use the YRC Service Center to initiate pickups, unless it is an extreme urgency. • Dealers should remember to print and include with their pickups, the Bill of Lading (BOL) and Packing List from the Website. This will assist in timely processing. • Include the following completed forms (listed below) with the returned core. Failure to adhere to these requirements will result in the chargeback of the core credit and possibly the warranty claim. – Core Return Form Reimbursement Policies 109 – Dealer’s Repair Order / Final Invoice (if sold over the counter) • Dealers will be billed the freight pickup & recovery costs for all invalid returned cores. Your adherence to program guidelines will ensure the program continues to run effectively. If you have any questions concerning the Reman Core Return Program, contact your HMA Parts Dealer Support Center at 1-800-545-4515.
coverage_details: Remanufactured ALT, PSG, RAC, and STR parts must be used for replacements.
New parts may be used if the vehicle has less than 500 miles.
New parts may be used if there is a specific legal requirement by a state governing agency.
New parts may be used if the remanufactured part is unavailable or on backorder.
New parts may be used for repeat repairs or customer satisfaction issues, with regional approval.
HMA pays all freight charges for core returns under this program.
exclusions: Do not use the YRC Service Center to initiate pickups, unless it is an extreme urgency.
Dealers will be billed freight pickup and recovery costs for all invalid returned cores.
Failure to adhere to core return requirements will result in the chargeback of the core credit and possibly the warranty claim.

For repeat repair or customer satisfaction exceptions, regional approval is required.
Print and include the Bill of Lading (BOL) and Packing List from the Website with core pickups.
Include the completed Core Return Form with the returned core.

title: Hybrid and Electric High Voltage Battery Replacement Policy
raw_content: For all hybrid and electric high voltage battery replacements, the following must be adhered to: • Refurbished high voltage battery must be used unless any of the following are true: - Vehicle has less than 500 miles. - Specific Legal requirement by a state governing agency. - Refurbished high voltage battery is unavailable or on backorder (parts availability documentation must be attached to the subject repair order to document this condition). A returned high voltage battery core should be fully assembled including all plastic and rubber plugs. Include all completed forms with the returned core. Failure to adhere to these requirements will result in the chargeback of any core credit and possibly the warranty claim. Reimbursement Policies 110
coverage_details: Refurbished high voltage battery must be used for replacement.
A new high voltage battery may be used if the vehicle has less than 500 miles.
A new high voltage battery may be used if there is a specific legal requirement by a state governing agency.
A new high voltage battery may be used if a refurbished high voltage battery is unavailable or on backorder.
exclusions: Core credit chargeback for high voltage battery cores that are not fully assembled.
Core credit chargeback for high voltage battery cores missing plastic and rubber plugs.
Core credit chargeback for high voltage battery cores returned without all completed forms.
Warranty claim denial if core return requirements are not met.
dealer_actions: Attach parts availability documentation to the repair order if a refurbished high voltage battery is unavailable or on backorder.
Return the high voltage battery core fully assembled, including all plastic and rubber plugs.
Include all completed forms with the returned high voltage battery core.

title: Dealer Responsibility and Non-Covered Items
raw_content: The following repair items are considered to be Dealer responsibility and are not covered under warranty: • Inspections included and covered under the Pre-Delivery Inspection Service. • Minor paint and cosmetic concerns which were not noted at time of delivery of the vehicle to the dealership. • Disposal fees incurred. • Defects due to long storage or poor storage conditions caused by lapses in aged inventory maintenance. • Repairs or replacements made necessary by faulty diagnosis or poor workmanship by dealer personnel, or duplicate, repeat, or comeback/subsequent repairs of the same item if previously performed by the same Dealer, unless the replaced part proves to be defective in material or workmanship. • Repairs related to the replacement of parts which are not held for inspection by HMA, or are not returned to HMA when requested, or otherwise not handled in accordance with current HMA policies and procedures. Failure of the following components and assemblies are considered the Dealer’s responsibility and are not reimbursable under warranty if the vehicle involved has been in Dealer inventory for an extended period of time (over 90 days): – Batteries – Paint repairs – Bright metal repairs (chrome, etc.) – Wiper blades – Weatherstrips – Soft trim – Lamp bulbs – Failure of powertrain components due to corrosion – Faded or corroded mirrors, lamps, reflectors and lenses – Fuel system corrosion or defects caused by contamination

title: Items Not Covered Under Warranty (Customer Responsibility)
raw_content: The following items are considered to be the customer’s responsibility and are not covered under warranty, unless the items are defective in material or workmanship: • Normal maintenance service: oil & filter changes, engine tune-ups, anti-freeze/coolant, refrigerant, head light aiming, tire rotation, wheel alignment, fuel system cleaning beyond 500 miles of service on a new vehicle, wheel balancing, and any maintenance or inspection service recommended by HMA for the vehicle involved. • Defects judged by HMA to be attributable to failure to properly perform the periodic inspections and services as specified in the Owner’s Manual. Reimbursement Policies 111 • Normal Replacement of Service Items – Replacements suggested by Dealer personnel as a part of preventive maintenance – Replacements required because of abuse, misuse, or improper driving habits of the customer – Worn clutch linings, brake linings, and wiper blades – Filter replacements (fuel, oil, and air) – Spark plugs – Condensers – Drive belts • Upholstery and Trim – Worn – Soiled – Torn or cut by a foreign object – Normal deterioration due to use and exposure • Paint – Dents, nicks, scratches or other damage – Normal deterioration due to exposure – Industrial fallout which occurs after delivery of the vehicle to the customer – Chipping due to road conditions or hazards (e.g. sand) • Bright Metal Trim – Dents, nicks, scratches, or other damage – Normal deterioration due to exposure • Damage or Failure Resulting from – Negligence or improper maintenance as specified in the Owner’s Manual – Misuse, accident, theft or fire – Use of improper or insufficient fuel, fluids, or lubricants – Modification, alteration, tampering, or improper repair – Racing or a competitive event (competitive events are defined as formal or informal time trials, competition with another vehicle, or any abnormal application of stress to the vehicle or the components thereof) – Any device and/or accessory not approved and/or supplied by Hyundai and not installed by Hyundai or an authorized Hyundai Motor America dealer – Slight irregularities not recognized as affecting the quality or function of the vehicle, such as slight noise or vibration, or items considered characteristic of the vehicle • Adjustments Required through Normal Use – Pre-Delivery type adjustments required after a reasonable period of normal use Reimbursement Policies 112 – Adjustment or repair to improve the vehicle’s appearance or performance beyond what is considered normal standards – Adjustment performed for a second time by the same Dealer

title: Warranty Exclusions and Coverage Conditions
raw_content: The following additional repair items are NOT considered to be HMA responsibility and are NOT covered under warranty: • Tires are warranted by the tire manufacturer and the warranty is administered by the tire distributor and their retail store representatives. • Any HMA product which has been damaged from accident, fire, or other casualty, or which fails due to misuse, negligence, alteration, or overloading beyond the specified vehicle weight rating, or which has been used for purposes other than those generally accepted applications for which it was designed. • Any labor operation performed by other than an authorized Hyundai Motor America dealer, except repairs sublet by the authorized Hyundai Motor America Dealer to a specialty shop or performed in an emergency by an independent repair facility. Any claim to HMA for sublet work must be filed by the Dealer who sublets the work, with the sublet final invoice attached to the dealer accounting copy of the repair order and a copy of the sublet final invoice attached to the shop hard copy of the repair order retained in the customer service file. • Parts not acknowledged by HMA to be defective in original workmanship and/or material. • Glass breakage or scratches, unless conclusive evidence is presented to substantiate the claim that breakage was unquestionably due to a defect in material and/or workmanship. • Repairs required as a result of using any component or parts not recommended or not approved by HMA. • Defects in equipment, devices, and accessories, other than those supplied by HMA and mounted on vehicles by authorized Hyundai Motor America dealers or HMA, and defects resulting from the use of such unspecified devices and accessories. • Concerns and/or difficulties which are generally not accepted as caused by a defect in material or workmanship, which happen in very special operations such as: – Slight sound or vibration which is not recognized as affecting the quality or function of the vehicle – Slight misting of oil or the like around packing and/or oil seals which causes no appreciable consumption or dripping of oil – Gaps between panels which are within the limits set by HMA – External appearance defects such as paint, chrome, and trim, which are not visible unless magnified by special means • Loss of use of the vehicle, loss of time, loss or damage to personal property or revenues, inconvenience and other consequential damages such as expenses for fuel, telephone, travel, lodging, and towing, (except where such exclusions or limitations are not permitted by law). Reimbursement Policies 113 – Certain expenses may be covered by the Hyundai Roadside Assistance Program. Customers should refer to their Hyundai Roadside Assistance Program Owner’s Guide or the Hyundai Owner’s Handbook for these coverages • Key fob reprogramming should only be necessary when a new key is required, thus any labor-only key fob claim will be denied. • Shop materials which are considered Dealer responsibility such as rags, shop cleaner, etc. • Disposal fees incurred are considered the cost of doing business and are therefore considered Dealer responsibility. • HMA reserves the right to return any claim for a VIN with an open campaign. Reimbursement Policies 114
coverage_details: Labor operations performed by authorized Hyundai Motor America dealers
Repairs sublet by authorized Hyundai Motor America dealers to a specialty shop

Glass breakage or scratches conclusively proven to be due to a defect in material and/or workmanship
