title: Labor Reimbursement Policy and Actual Time Repair Procedures
raw_content: HMA will reimburse the Dealer for labor based upon the Dealer’s approved warranty labor rate in effect on the date of repair, multiplied by the time allowed for the operation, as indicated on your claim and shown in the current Labor Time Standard in effect on the date of the repair. If the labor operation performed is not listed in the Labor Time Standard or in a separate HMA service communication (i.e. Technical Service Bulletin), or if the labor operation is an Actual Time (TT) operation, the Dealer will be reimbursed on the basis of: • Actual labor operation performance time within reasonable time limits, multiplied by the Dealer’s approved warranty labor rate. Service Manager approval must be obtained prior to performing all actual time repairs and must sign his or her name, print his or her name and note the time authorized prior to performing all actual time diagnosis or repairs. In addition, the Technician must provide an explanation of why the actual time is needed on the hard copy or the repair order. The time must be realistic and consistent with accepted industry practices. Actual time added to a standard labor operation to supplement the standard time may only be claimed for highly unusual repair situations where additional time is required to properly complete the repair procedure. The same documentation and authorization requirements apply. ACTUAL TIME REPAIR OPERATION CODE: (NTT VS ATT) Actual Time Repair Operation means any specific repair performed where no applicable standard labor code exists in the manual or additional time is needed for a specific repair: The operation code for Actual Time must be created as follows: 1. “NTT” – NO APPLICABLE STANDARD LABOR CODE EXISTS IN LTS OR LABOR OPERATION IS NOT APPLICABLE TO MODEL Applicable “NTT” labor operation must always be in the first position when submitting a claim. The first five (5) digits of the causal part number or replaced part is used for creating your applicable “NTT” Labor Operation. Example of Causal Part Number: 91837-XXXXX To create the applicable labor operation, use the first 5 digits of the causal part number or replaced part number followed by “NTT”. Example of Causal Part Number: 91837-XXXXX Labor Operation: 91837-NTT 2. “ATT” – ADDITIONAL TIME REQUIRED TO PERFORM REPAIR Reimbursement Policies 115 Applicable “ATT” labor operation must always be in the second or subsequent position when submitting a claim. The first five (5) digits of the specific labor operation requiring additional time is used for creating your applicable “ATT” Labor Operation. Example of labor operation: 56910R00 To create the applicable labor operation, use the first 5 digits of the specific labor operation followed by “ATT”. Example of Causal Part Number: 56900-29750 Labor Operation: 56910R00 ATT Labor Operation: 56910ATT 3. ALL CLAIMS WILL REQUIRE COMPLAINT, CAUSE, AND CORRECTION (“3 C’S”) OR WILL BE RETURNED. Note: Labor operations performed that are not listed in the Labor Time Standard are considered actual time labor operations (TT time). Technicians must record their actual beginning and ending times for all actual time repairs by punching on and off of a Daily Time Ticket (flag sheet). A separate flag is required to document the actual time spent by the technician to make the needed repairs. The time flag strip must be adhered to the back of the repair order hard copy. For dealers with an ERO system, the technician must open a new line and log on and off to document the actual time spent repairing the vehicle.
coverage_details: Reimbursement for labor based on the Dealer u2019s approved warranty labor rate multiplied by the time allowed for the operation, as indicated in the current Labor Time Standard.

Reimbursement for labor operations not listed in the Labor Time Standard or HMA service communication, or for Actual Time (TT) operations, based on actual labor operation performance time (within reasonable limits) multiplied by the Dealer u2019s approved warranty labor rate.
Actual time added to a standard labor operation for highly unusual repair situations where additional time is required to properly complete the repair procedure.
exclusions: Actual time repairs performed without prior Service Manager approval.
Actual time diagnosis or repairs performed without the Service Manager u2019s signed name, printed name, and noted authorized time.
Actual time claims lacking a Technician u2019s explanation on the hard copy or repair order for why the actual time is needed.
Actual time claimed that is not realistic or inconsistent with accepted industry practices.
Supplemental actual time claims not for highly unusual repair situations where additional time is truly required.
Claims submitted without Complaint, Cause, and Correction ( u201c3 C u2019s u201d).
Actual time repairs performed without technicians recording their actual beginning and ending times by punching on and off of a Daily Time Ticket (flag sheet) or logging on and off in an ERO system.
Actual time repairs lacking a separate flag strip adhered to the back of the repair order hard copy (for non-ERO systems).
dealer_actions: Obtain Service Manager approval prior to performing all actual time repairs.
Service Manager must sign their name, print their name, and note the time authorized prior to performing all actual time diagnosis or repairs.
Technician must provide an explanation on the hard copy or the repair order for why the actual time is needed.
Ensure that actual time claimed is realistic and consistent with accepted industry practices.

For  u201cNTT u201d (No Applicable Standard Labor Code) operations: create the labor operation code using the first five digits of the causal part number or replaced part followed by  u201cNTT u201d (e.g., 91837-NTT).
The  u201cNTT u201d labor operation must always be in the first position when submitting a claim.

For  u201cATT u201d (Additional Time Required to Perform Repair) operations: create the labor operation code using the first five digits of the specific labor operation requiring additional time followed by  u201cATT u201d (e.g., 56910ATT).
The  u201cATT u201d labor operation must always be in the second or subsequent position when submitting a claim.
All claims will require Complaint, Cause, and Correction ( u201c3 C u2019s u201d).
Technicians must record their actual beginning and ending times for all actual time repairs by punching on and off of a Daily Time Ticket (flag sheet).
A separate flag is required to document the actual time spent by the technician to make the needed repairs.
The time flag strip must be adhered to the back of the repair order hard copy.
For dealers with an ERO system, the technician must open a new line and log on and off to document the actual time spent repairing the vehicle.

title: No Trouble Found Diagnosis Reimbursement Policy
raw_content: HMA will reimburse the Dealer for specific diagnosis time for “No Trouble Found”, when no repair (claim has no parts, sublet, towing, freight, or any additional repair labor operation) is performed. Repairs that qualify for diagnosis labor time reimbursement, when no problem/trouble is found and no repair is performed, must adhere to the following requirements: • Repair attempts eligible for no trouble found diagnosis must be related to the following repair categories: – Automatic Transmission – Engine Electrical – Electrical • The customer’s complaints must be fully documented/explained on the repair order. • Normal Hyundai documentation/paperwork requirements apply, with the hard copy of the repair order retained for future review. • Diagnosis performed must directly relate to the Customer’s complaint. • The Technician must provide an explanation of diagnostic procedures and results on the hard copy of the repair order. • Approved diagnosis labor time applies to use of only the following diagnosis equipment: – Global Diagnostic System (GDS) Reimbursement Policies 116 – Hyundai Automatic Transaxle Tester • Reimbursement time is equal to the applicable labor time listed in current Labor Time Standard (LTS) for the labor time operation codes for the Diagnosis operations performed. • Before and after readings/measurements obtained from the above diagnosis equipment must be documented and appear on the Technician’s shop copy of the repair order. All GDS diagnosis usage must have a printout of the before and after readings attached to the Technician’s shop copy of the repair order. • HMA considers fuse failure to be caused not by the fuse itself, but because of an inherently larger issue. Technical support from Techline may assist in finding an actual failure or defective part. Fuses must be claimed under No Trouble Found using NTF0000000 as the causal part. Excessive and/or above average requests for “No Trouble Found” diagnosis time will be continually reviewed by HMA. Reimbursement Policies 117
coverage_details: Reimbursement for specific diagnosis time when no repair is performed and no trouble is found.
Eligible repair categories include Automatic Transmission, Engine Electrical, and Electrical.
Diagnosis performed must directly relate to the customer’s complaint.
Approved diagnosis labor time applies to the use of Global Diagnostic System (GDS) and Hyundai Automatic Transaxle Tester.
Reimbursement time is equal to the applicable labor time listed in the current Labor Time Standard (LTS) for the diagnosis operations performed.
Fuse failures are considered under No Trouble Found using NTF0000000 as the causal part.
dealer_actions: Customer’s complaints must be fully documented/explained on the repair order.
Normal Hyundai documentation/paperwork requirements apply, with the hard copy of the repair order retained for future review.
The Technician must provide an explanation of diagnostic procedures and results on the hard copy of the repair order.
Before and after readings/measurements obtained from GDS and Hyundai Automatic Transaxle Tester must be documented and appear on the Technician’s shop copy of the repair order.
All GDS diagnosis usage must have a printout of the before and after readings attached to the Technician’s shop copy of the repair order.
Fuses must be claimed under No Trouble Found using NTF0000000 as the causal part.

title: Repairs on Used Hyundai Vehicles in Dealer Inventory
raw_content: The phrase, “Repairs on used Hyundai Vehicles in Dealer Inventory” refers to repairs performed on any used Hyundai taken into a Hyundai Dealer’s inventory and has the remainder of the 60 months/60,000 miles New Vehicle Limited Warranty (NVLW). For such repairs, • Engine and transmission repairs will require Prior Approval. • Any used vehicle reconditioning, dents, scratches, or damage to a vehicle is not covered under any Hyundai Warranty. Note 1: HMA will monitor all in-stock vehicle warranty repairs. Note 2: It is the dealer’s responsibility to perform any open Service Campaigns or Recalls on used vehicles in dealer stock prior to selling or trading the vehicle. Service Campaigns or Recalls on used vehicles do not require Prior Approval Center authorization, unless otherwise stated. Reimbursement Policies 118
coverage_details: Repairs performed on any used Hyundai taken into a Hyundai Dealer’s inventory with remaining 60 months/60,000 miles New Vehicle Limited Warranty (NVLW)
Service Campaigns or Recalls on used vehicles
exclusions: Used vehicle reconditioning
Dents
Scratches
Damage to a vehicle
dealer_actions: Engine and transmission repairs require Prior Approval
Perform any open Service Campaigns or Recalls on used vehicles in dealer stock prior to selling or trading the vehicle
Service Campaigns or Recalls on used vehicles do not require Prior Approval Center authorization, unless otherwise stated

title: Dealer Warranty Labor Rate
raw_content: The Dealer’s Warranty Labor Rate approved and reimbursed by HMA is intended to compensate the Dealer adequately, fairly and most importantly, competitively for warranty repairs performed. In determining fair, reasonable, and competitive warranty labor rates under this Section, areas of consideration include: • The Dealer’s current actual retail hourly labor rate. • The reasonableness of the requested labor rate in relation to local competition and market area. • The Dealer’s year-over-year labor rate increases.
coverage_details: Compensation for warranty repairs performed via the HMA-approved and reimbursed Dealer’s Warranty Labor Rate
Consideration of the Dealer’s current actual retail hourly labor rate in determining the warranty labor rate
Consideration of the reasonableness of the requested labor rate in relation to local competition and market area
Consideration of the Dealer’s year-over-year labor rate increases

title: Warranty Labor Rate Request Procedures
raw_content: A warranty labor rate must be requested prior to the operation or start-up date. A change to an existing warranty labor rate may be requested under Subsection 5.10.3 after 12 months from the date of the most recent approved warranty labor rate. The following procedures must be adhered to when requesting a warranty labor rate under this Section. Please Note: The Dealer Principal or General Manager are the only individuals authorized to sign the Labor Rate Request Form under this Section. If the procedure is not adhered to, the Labor Rate Request form will be returned for corrections.
coverage_details: New warranty labor rates may be requested.
Changes to an existing warranty labor rate may be requested under Subsection 5.10.3 after 12 months from the date of the most recent approved warranty labor rate.
exclusions: Labor Rate Request Forms not signed by the Dealer Principal or General Manager.
Labor rate requests that do not adhere to the specified procedures, leading to the form being returned for corrections.
dealer_actions: Request a warranty labor rate prior to the operation or start-up date.
Adhere to all specified procedures when requesting a warranty labor rate.
Ensure only the Dealer Principal or General Manager sign the Labor Rate Request Form.

title: New Dealer Warranty Labor Rate Establishment
raw_content: The procedures stated below must be followed for a new Dealer requesting the establishment of a Warranty Labor Rate under this Subsection: • Dealer obtains and DPSM/Region and/or Business Management Representative assists Dealer in completing a Warranty Labor Rate Request/Competitive Labor Rate Evaluation Form and in establishing a Warranty Labor Rate under this Subsection. This form must be submitted to the HMA Regional Office at least 30 days prior to dealership operation date. The form must include the Retail Labor Rate dealer is planning on charging the customer. This rate should be entered in the area called “Projected Posted Retail Customer Labor Rate” on the Labor Rate Evaluation Form. The Warranty Labor Rate Request form is available on www. HyundaiDealer.com under the Service Tab / Dealer Resources / Documents Library / Warranty. • Warranty Labor Rate Request /Competitive Labor Rate Evaluation form is submitted to the Regional Office for review/approval. Do not send the form directly to the National Warranty Department. *Competitor labor rates from dealers under the same ownership group as the requesting Hyundai dealer is not allowed. • Regional office will forward the reviewed form and proposed recommended rate to the National Warranty Department for approval and processing. National Warranty will not approve a region recommended rate that is greater than the Dealer’s “Projected Posted Retail Customer Labor Rate”. Reimbursement Policies 119 • Dealer receives notification of approval/disapproval of Warranty Labor Rate request from Regional office. Note: Allow at least 30 days for approval/disapproval of the requested Warranty Labor Rate from when the packet is received at National. Please note that the new dealer’s labor rate will be made effective (if approved by National) when the dealer becomes activated by Hyundai National’s Dealer Development. • DPSM/Regional Office may perform a review of at least 25 of the most current sequentially numbered Hyundai customer pay retail repair orders within 60 days after Dealer’s operation date to validate that the approved Warranty Labor Rate is not actually greater than the Dealer’s actual customer pay retail repair labor rate. If the DPSM/Regional Office verifies that the Dealer’s approved Warranty Labor Rate is greater than the Dealer’s actual customer pay retail repair labor rate during this review, HMA shall have the right to adjust the Dealer’s approved Warranty Labor Rate to reflect the Dealer’s actual customer pay retail repair labor rate.
coverage_details: Procedure for a new Dealer to request and establish a Warranty Labor Rate under this Subsection.
exclusions: Competitor labor rates from dealers under the same ownership group as the requesting Hyundai dealer are not allowed.
National Warranty will not approve a region recommended rate that is greater than the Dealer’s “Projected Posted Retail Customer Labor Rate”.
The Warranty Labor Rate Request/Competitive Labor Rate Evaluation form must not be sent directly to the National Warranty Department.
If the approved Warranty Labor Rate is verified to be greater than the Dealer’s actual customer pay retail repair labor rate during a review, HMA reserves the right to adjust it.
dealer_actions: Obtain and complete a Warranty Labor Rate Request/Competitive Labor Rate Evaluation Form, with assistance from DPSM/Region and/or Business Management Representative.
Submit the completed form to the HMA Regional Office at least 30 days prior to the dealership operation date.
Enter the planned Retail Labor Rate (projected posted retail customer labor rate) on the Labor Rate Evaluation Form.
Access the Warranty Labor Rate Request form on www.HyundaiDealer.com under the Service Tab / Dealer Resources / Documents Library / Warranty.
Allow at least 30 days for approval/disapproval notification from the Regional office after the packet is received at the National Warranty Department.
Understand that the new dealer’s labor rate (if approved) will be made effective when the dealer becomes activated by Hyundai National’s Dealer Development.

Be aware that the DPSM/Regional Office may review at least 25 of the most current sequentially numbered Hyundai customer pay retail repair orders within 60 days after the Dealer’s operation date to validate the approved Warranty Labor Rate.

title: Warranty Labor Rate Change Request Procedure
raw_content: The procedures stated below must be followed for an existing Dealer requesting a change in their Warranty Labor Rate under this Subsection. Labor rate increase requests under this Subsection will be approved up to a maximum of 5% less than the average RO labor dollars per hour calculated in the 25 RO survey. On a case-by-case basis, the actual labor rate approved by HMA may vary dependent on local market conditions and the total year-over- year increase being requested. Please note, Warranty Labor Rate requests submitted under this Subsection do not constitute labor rate requests under applicable state law procedures. Nothing in this policy prohibits a dealer from requesting to have its rate set under applicable state law procedures. The procedures stated below must be followed for an existing Dealer requesting a change in their Warranty Labor Rate under this Subsection: • Dealer provides and completes a Warranty Labor Rate Request/Competitive Labor Rate Evaluation Form*, Warranty Labor Rate Survey, a Warranty Labor Rate Checklist, and 25 most current sequentially numbered qualified Hyundai customer pay Retail Repair Orders (ROs). To be a qualified RO, the ROs must meet guidelines outlined in Section 5.10.6. to its regional office for review. *Competitor labor rates from dealers under the same ownership group as the requesting Hyundai dealer is not allowed. The above three forms can be found on www.HyundaiDealer.com under the Service Tab / Dealer Resources / Documents Library / Warranty. Please be sure to highlight all qualifying repair lines (total labor hours and labor dollars). If the hours are not on the RO, please submit supporting documentation (such as a timesheet) with hours listed on it. Reimbursement Policies 120 HMA Dealer will provide to DPSM the Warranty Labor Rate Request Form, Warranty Labor Rate Survey, and 25 of its most current sequentially numbered qualified Hyundai customer pay RO s for review. The DPSM will validate and review the highlighted labor hour(s) and labor dollar(s) on each RO against the survey form. The DPSM should notify its regional office once it receives the dealer’s submission. Once DPSM validates the dealer’s submission, DPSM will forward to its regional office for review. If DPSM finds error(s) in dealer’s submission, it may return submission to dealer for correction. Please note that the submission can be returned to the dealer for the following reasons: • If ROs are not listed in sequential order on the Warranty Labor Rate Survey Form • If the supplied ROs are not in sequential order when provided for review • If the Warranty Labor Rate Request/Warranty Labor Rate Survey are not typed • If the ROs do not meet the guidelines outlined in Section 5.10.6 or include non- qualified ROs • If the ROs do not substantiate the dealer’s requested rate • Please do not include maintenance or special pricing repairs (see section 5.10.6 for further details on exclusions) and hours should be typed, not handwritten on the RO. • Once the submission is complete and reviewed by the DPSM, it will be forwarded to its regional office (Regional Warranty Manager) for review. If the regional office finds error(s) in dealer’s submission, it may return submission to DPSM for dealer to correct. • Once the regional office has reviewed the Dealer’s submission, the regional office will forward approved forms (Warranty Labor Rate Request Form, Warranty Labor Rate Survey, ROs, along with Check list) along with its proposed recommendation to the National Warranty Department for final approval and processing. Once the National Warranty Department reaches its decision, it will notify the Regional Office . Note: As set forth above, request for Warranty Labor Rates submitted under this Subsection do not constitute requests under state law and are not governed by state law. Nothing in this Subsection prohibits a Dealer to submit a request to increase its Warranty Labor Rate under applicable state law. Requests for Warranty Labor Rates submitted under state statute shall be governed by the applicable state law.
coverage_details: Procedures for existing dealers to request a change in Warranty Labor Rate under this Subsection.
Labor rate increases approved up to a maximum of 5% less than the average RO labor dollars per hour from a 25 RO survey.
Approved labor rate may vary based on local market conditions and year-over-year increase.
This policy does not restrict dealers from requesting rate adjustments under applicable state law procedures.
exclusions: Competitor labor rates from dealers within the same ownership group are not allowed in the survey.
Warranty Labor Rate requests under this Subsection are not state law requests and are not governed by state law.
Submissions will be returned if Repair Orders (ROs) are not listed or provided in sequential order.
Submissions will be returned if the Warranty Labor Rate Request/Survey forms are not typed.
Submissions will be returned if ROs do not meet Section 5.10.6 guidelines or include non-qualified ROs.
Submissions will be returned if ROs do not substantiate the requested rate.
Maintenance or special pricing repairs must be excluded from ROs.
Handwritten hours on ROs are not accepted; hours must be typed.
dealer_actions: Complete and submit a Warranty Labor Rate Request/Competitive Labor Rate Evaluation Form.
Complete and submit a Warranty Labor Rate Survey.
Complete and submit a Warranty Labor Rate Checklist.
Provide 25 most current, sequentially numbered, qualified Hyundai customer pay Retail Repair Orders (ROs).
Ensure ROs meet guidelines outlined in Section 5.10.6.
Submit all required forms and ROs to the regional office for review.
Highlight all qualifying repair lines (total labor hours and labor dollars) on submitted ROs.
Provide supporting documentation (e.g., timesheet) with hours if not explicitly listed on the RO.
Ensure Warranty Labor Rate Request/Warranty Labor Rate Survey are typed.
Ensure ROs do not include maintenance or special pricing repairs.
Ensure hours on ROs are typed, not handwritten.

title: Warranty Labor Rate Request Document Location
raw_content: Available on www.HyundaiDealer.com under the Service tab / Dealer Resources / Documents Library / Warranty / Warranty Labor Rate Request
dealer_actions: Access the 'Warranty Labor Rate Request' document on www.HyundaiDealer.com via the Service tab, then Dealer Resources, then Documents Library, then Warranty.

title: Warranty Labor Rate Approval Requirements
raw_content: Approval of the requested Warranty Labor Rate is based on the following requirements and verification: Reimbursement Policies 121 • The customer pay retail labor rate that is established and implemented by the dealership must be reasonable and consistent with the local trade practices in the market area in which the dealership performs service business. The rate must be consistent with competition and generally accepted sound business practices. • The repair orders listed on the Warranty Labor Rate Evaluation Sheet must support the region’s recommended rate and not above the dealer’s posted retail rate for the customer. • The repair orders listed on the Warranty Labor Rate Evaluation Sheet must be in sequential order and the RO open date (not the RO closed date) is to be used in the RO open date column. • The customer pay retail labor rate charged to the retail customer must: 1. Be displayed and visible to the customer in both the service customer write- up/reception area and the service customer cashier area. 2. The lettering used to display the customer retail labor rate must be at least 2 inches high. 3. The rate must be displayed at all times during service operation and business hours and should state one or more of the following based upon the method or rate charged: • If the actual technician clock time to perform the repair is the method of labor charge, the words displayed should state: Retail Customer Labor Rate charges are based on a labor rate of $ _______ per hour. • If a published flat rate manual is used as the method of labor charge for the type of repairs, the words displayed should state: Retail customer labor rate charges are based on a labor rate of $ _______ per mechanical hour. Retail customer labor rate charges are based on a labor rate of $ _______ per body repair hour. Note: When the customer retail labor rate charge is based upon a published flat rate manual, the display of the posted rate must clearly state that “the dealership uses the labor time published in the (name of manual) labor time manual. The time published in the (name of manual) lists the average time required to perform the repair and therefore, since the time listed is an average time, the listed time may be more or less than the actual time spent to perform the repair” (consult with local laws for other requirements). • If variable or discounted rates are charged, the words displayed should state for example: Retail customer labor rate charges for Senior Citizens are based on a labor rate of $ ________ per hour. Note: It is advisable to state who qualifies or the criteria for the variable or discounted rate. Reimbursement Policies 122 • If a skill level, specialized, staggered or shop-track labor system is used as the method of labor charge, the words displayed should state: Retail customer labor rate charges are based on a labor rate of: $ ______ per hour for technician skill level A $ ______ per hour for technician skill level B $ ______ per hour for technician skill level C Note: Types of repairs must be correlated with the appropriate technician skill level. This information must be made readily available to the customer if requested by the customer. • If a team system is used, the displayed labor rate must state the fact that a team system is used by the dealership. • The retail customer labor rate must be pre-printed or written on all copies of the retail customer pay repair order. • The labor time charged to each retail customer must be stated in number of hours in tenths on the accounting copy of the retail customer pay repair order. Do not handwrite the hours on the Repair Order. • The labor charges for all retail customer repairs must be based on a labor time and rate that is consistent with the dealership’s practice. • The dealership must maintain a reasonable percentage of warranty service labor sales to total customer service and warranty service labor sales as determined by HMA. • If the regional office’s review of the 25 sequentially numbered Hyundai retail customer pay repair orders reveals any discrepancies or any of the above requirements are not adhered to, the difference between the new verified rate and the current warranty labor rate will be charged back to the dealer for all labor hours approved under the unsubstantiated warranty labor rate. The new verified warranty labor rate will become the Dealer’s approved warranty labor rate.
coverage_details: The customer pay retail labor rate established by the dealership must be reasonable and consistent with local trade practices and competition.
Repair orders listed on the Warranty Labor Rate Evaluation Sheet must support the region’s recommended rate and not exceed the dealer’s posted retail rate for the customer.
The customer pay retail labor rate charged to the retail customer must be displayed and visible in both the service customer write-up/reception area and the service customer cashier area.
The lettering used to display the customer retail labor rate must be at least 2 inches high.
The rate must be displayed at all times during service operation and business hours.
Displayed words must state the retail customer labor rate charges are based on a labor rate of $ _______ per hour, if actual technician clock time is used.

Displayed words must state the retail customer labor rate charges are based on a labor rate of $ _______ per mechanical hour or per body repair hour, if a published flat rate manual is used (with clarification about the manual name and average time).

Displayed words must state examples for variable or discounted rates, such as 'Retail customer labor rate charges for Senior Citizens are based on a labor rate of $ ________ per hour', with advisable criteria.
Displayed words must state retail customer labor rate charges per hour for technician skill levels (A, B, C) if a skill level system is used, with repair types correlated to skill levels.
If a team system is used, the displayed labor rate must state this fact.
The retail customer labor rate must be pre-printed or written on all copies of the retail customer pay repair order.
The labor time charged to each retail customer must be stated in number of hours in tenths on the accounting copy of the retail customer pay repair order.
The labor charges for all retail customer repairs must be based on a labor time and rate consistent with the dealership’s practice.
The dealership must maintain a reasonable percentage of warranty service labor sales to total customer service and warranty service labor sales as determined by HMA.
exclusions: Repair orders listed on the Warranty Labor Rate Evaluation Sheet are not in sequential order or do not use the RO open date.
The customer pay retail labor rate display is not visible, not at least 2 inches high, or not displayed at all times during service operation and business hours.

The displayed labor rate wording does not accurately reflect the method of labor charge or omits required details (e.g., manual name, average time disclaimer, team system usage, skill level correlation).
Handwritten labor hours on the Repair Order instead of pre-printed or written.
Any discrepancies found during the regional office’s review of the 25 sequentially numbered Hyundai retail customer pay repair orders.
Non-adherence to any of the specified warranty labor rate requirements.
Any difference between the new verified rate and the current warranty labor rate will be charged back to the dealer for all labor hours approved under the unsubstantiated warranty labor rate.
dealer_actions: Establish and implement a customer pay retail labor rate that is reasonable and consistent with local trade practices and competition.
Ensure the repair orders listed on the Warranty Labor Rate Evaluation Sheet support the region’s recommended rate and do not exceed the dealer’s posted retail rate.
Ensure repair orders listed on the Warranty Labor Rate Evaluation Sheet are in sequential order and use the RO open date.
Display the customer pay retail labor rate clearly and visibly in both the service customer write-up/reception area and the service customer cashier area.
Ensure the lettering used to display the customer retail labor rate is at least 2 inches high.
Display the retail labor rate at all times during service operation and business hours.
Use specific wording for displaying the labor rate based on the method of charge (e.g., actual clock time, published flat rate manual, variable/discounted rates, skill level system, team system).
If using a published flat rate manual, the display must clearly state the manual name and that the time listed is an average time.
If variable or discounted rates are charged, state who qualifies or the criteria for the rate.

If a skill level, specialized, staggered or shop-track labor system is used, correlate types of repairs with the appropriate technician skill level and make this information readily available to the customer if requested.
If a team system is used, the displayed labor rate must state the fact that a team system is used by the dealership.
Pre-print or write the retail customer labor rate on all copies of the retail customer pay repair order.
State the labor time charged to each retail customer in number of hours in tenths on the accounting copy of the retail customer pay repair order, without handwriting.
Ensure labor charges for all retail customer repairs are based on a labor time and rate consistent with the dealership’s practice.
Maintain a reasonable percentage of warranty service labor sales to total customer service and warranty service labor sales as determined by HMA.

title: Warranty Labor Rate Request and Retail Customer Pay Repair Order Verification
raw_content: Upon submission of a Warranty Labor Rate Request form to the Regional Office under Subsections 5.10.2 or 5.10.3, the DPSM/Regional office may perform, evaluate and verify at least 25 sequentially numbered Hyundai retail customer pay repair orders in order to verify that the labor rate actually charged to retail customers agrees with the dealer’s retail customer labor rate posted. Hyundai Motor America will only accept properly documented retail customer paid repair orders, provided they follow the procedure described below. All repair order submissions must meet the guidelines described above or will be subject to denial. The 25 sequentially numbered Hyundai repair orders (ROs) should consist of the most recent retail customer pay repair orders and be in sequential order. ALL customer repair line(s) on each Reimbursement Policies 123 RO must display the labor hour(s) and labor dollar(s) on the RO or provide qualifying back up (such as a timesheet). Repair Order (RO) Requirements: The repair order provided must contain the following information at a minimum: I. Dealer Logo/Name and Address II. Repair Order must indicate “Accounting, Service File Copy, or Customer Receipt Copy” on the actual RO *IF a dealer uses an electronic repair order system, the dealer must provide the “Accounting, Service File Copy, or Customer Receipt Copy” in addition to the initial repair order and final invoice copy) III. The Totals Section should display the following: Total Labor, Total Parts, Total Sales Tax, Total Charges and Discounts, Miscellaneous, or Less Insurance IV. The RO must include the following information: a. RO number b. VIN c. RO open and close dates (close date is the date the vehicle leaves dealership- document if customer doesn’t pick up vehicle when first informed that it is ready) d. Vehicle mileage (in and out) e. Owner/ Customer’s name, address, and phone number f. Description of customer’s complaint, cause and correction g. Customer’s signature for initial repair order write up h. Customer’s signature for receipt of repair completed vehicle (pre-invoice, customer, accounting, final invoice) i. Replacement part number j. Part(s) dollar amount (cost and part amount charged to the retail customer) k. Part quantity l. Labor dollar amount (if applicable) m. Labor hours (if applicable) n. Technician comments o. Display and indicate any discounts offered to the retail customer p. Summary of the “Totals Section” (parts, labor, tax, and etc. / A description of all payments received. Retail Customer Pay Repair Exclusions: The type of repairs listed below will not be part of the evaluation. · Discounts (i.e. repairs with discounts) Reimbursement Policies 124 · All factory recommended maintenance services such as 7.5K, 15K, 22.5K, 30K, engine oil, air/oil filters belts (timing, serpentine, V-Belt, drive), spark plugs, tune-ups, fluid flushes & refills, etc. · Internal, extended warranty, extended service contract, insurance company, or similar paid repairs. · Any repairs that are not fully customer paid · Dealership Goodwill repairs, including repairs where the Dealer absorbs a portion of the repair costs. · Repairs due to customer abuse · Brake pads (unless part of a larger repair such as replacement of rotors) · Air Conditioner (AC) Refrigerant Evacuation and Recharge · Wheel balance and alignment · Tire service such as replacement and rotation. · Vehicle Reconditioning · Wiper blades · Any repairs with Non-Hyundai make vehicles · State safety and smog inspections · All service specials * · Any menu related repairs · Variables rates applied to specific fleets. * Note: Service specials are generally defined as advertised sales, which may be in effect for a short period or are ongoing. Certain ongoing discounted repair items (e.g. menu repairs) are not considered service specials. Note: The list above is not all-inclusive and is subject to change. For Customer Pay (CP) repair orders, be sure to highlight all qualifying repair lines (i.e., repairs that do not fall under the list of the above retail customer repair exclusions) . For example, if there is a CP repair order that includes the following repairs and times, highlight each qualifying line and SUM THE TOTAL HOURS AND LABOR DOLLARS. The sums for the repair order will be entered on one line of the Warranty Labor Rate Survey. RO#12345A Line 1 Replace alternator 1.0 hours $150.00 Line 2 Replace compressor 1.5 hours $225.00 Line 3 Replace TPMS 0.5 hours $75.00 Line 4 Change oil and filter 0.4 hours $60.00 Total Qualifying Repair Lines 3.0 hours $450.00 Reimbursement Policies 125
coverage_details: Properly documented retail customer paid repair orders for labor rate verification.
Repair orders must consist of the most recent retail customer pay repair orders, sequentially numbered (at least 25).
All customer repair line(s) on each RO must display labor hour(s) and labor dollar(s) or provide qualifying backup (e.g., timesheet).
Only qualifying repair lines (those not listed in exclusions) should be highlighted and their total hours and labor dollars summed for the Warranty Labor Rate Survey.
exclusions: Discounts (i.e., repairs with discounts)
All factory recommended maintenance services (e.g., 7.5K, 15K, 22.5K, 30K services, engine oil, air/oil filters, belts, spark plugs, tune-ups, fluid flushes & refills)
Internal, extended warranty, extended service contract, insurance company, or similar paid repairs
Any repairs that are not fully customer paid
Dealership Goodwill repairs, including repairs where the Dealer absorbs a portion of the repair costs
Repairs due to customer abuse
Brake pads (unless part of a larger repair such as replacement of rotors)
Air Conditioner (AC) Refrigerant Evacuation and Recharge
Wheel balance and alignment
Tire service such as replacement and rotation
Vehicle Reconditioning
Wiper blades
Any repairs with Non-Hyundai make vehicles
State safety and smog inspections
All service specials (advertised sales, short-period or ongoing)
Any menu related repairs
Variable rates applied to specific fleets
dealer_actions: Submit a Warranty Labor Rate Request form to the Regional Office under Subsections 5.10.2 or 5.10.3.
Provide at least 25 sequentially numbered Hyundai retail customer pay repair orders for verification.
Ensure all repair orders follow the described procedure and meet guidelines to avoid denial.
For electronic repair order systems, provide the “Accounting, Service File Copy, or Customer Receipt Copy” in addition to the initial repair order and final invoice copy.

Ensure each repair order contains at a minimum: Dealer Logo/Name and Address; indication of “Accounting, Service File Copy, or Customer Receipt Copy”; Totals Section displaying Total Labor, Total Parts, Total Sales Tax, Total Charges and Discounts, Miscellaneous, or Less Insurance.

Ensure each repair order includes: RO number, VIN, RO open and close dates (document if customer doesn’t pick up vehicle when first informed it is ready), Vehicle mileage (in and out), Owner/Customer’s name, address, and phone number, Description of customer’s complaint, cause and correction, Customer’s signature for initial repair order write up, Customer’s signature for receipt of repair completed vehicle, Replacement part number, Part(s) dollar amount (cost and part amount charged to the retail customer), Part quantity, Labor dollar amount (if applicable), Labor hours (if applicable), Technician comments, Display and indicate any discounts offered to the retail customer, Summary of the “Totals Section” and a description of all payments received.
Highlight all qualifying repair lines on CP repair orders (i.e., repairs not listed in the exclusions).
Sum the total hours and labor dollars for all qualifying repair lines on each repair order.
Enter the sums for each repair order on one line of the Warranty Labor Rate Survey.

title: Warranty Claim Adjustments
raw_content: In instances where an approved/paid warranty claim has been underpaid, overpaid, or contained incorrect or incomplete information such as part number(s), part quantities, labor operation code(s), labor quantities, sublet, towing freight, mileage, etc. Dealer can self-correct the claim two separate ways, depending on if the Total Cost will be impacted. For any approved/paid claims needing an adjustment that will change the claim amount, Dealer may chargeback the original claim and create a new corrected claim with a new claim number. Do not adjust the original claim, or your resubmission will be denied. For any approved/paid claims needing a non-financial adjustment (i.e. mileage discrepancy, etc.), Dealer may chargeback, adjust, and resubmit the claim (same claim number). The following claims will need to be charged back by the Warranty Call Center 1-877-446-2922: • Any claim(s) that are over the immediate Friday since the payment date. • Any claim(s) that have been paid incorrectly, and your DCS Debit screen is not working. Reimbursement Policies 126
coverage_details: Approved/paid warranty claims that were underpaid, overpaid, or contained incorrect/incomplete information (e.g., part number, part quantity, labor operation code, labor quantity, sublet, towing freight, mileage)
Self-correction of claims for financial adjustments (chargeback original, create new claim)
Self-correction of claims for non-financial adjustments (chargeback, adjust, resubmit original claim)
exclusions: Adjusting the original claim if the total cost will be impacted (resubmission will be denied)
Claims over the immediate Friday since the payment date (must be handled by Warranty Call Center)
Claims paid incorrectly when the DCS Debit screen is not working (must be handled by Warranty Call Center)
dealer_actions: For adjustments changing the claim amount: Chargeback the original claim
For adjustments changing the claim amount: Create a new corrected claim with a new claim number
For non-financial adjustments (e.g., mileage discrepancy): Chargeback, adjust, and resubmit the claim (using the same claim number)
For claims over the immediate Friday since the payment date: Contact the Warranty Call Center at 1-877-446-2922
For claims paid incorrectly when the DCS Debit screen is not working: Contact the Warranty Call Center at 1-877-446-2922

title: Foreign Vehicle Warranty Claims
raw_content: Claims for foreign vehicles being serviced and operated in the United States should be submitted using the Foreign Vehicle Warranty Claim screen located in Hyundai WebDCS under the Warranty tab. Go to WEBDCS > Warranty Tab > Information > Foreign Vehicle Information screen to obtain the production and in-service dates of the foreign VIN. To verify warranty coverage or for technical support on a foreign vehicle, please be sure to contact the country of origin prior to performing any repairs. The contact information for the countries of origin can be found on the Foreign Vehicle Warranty Coverage Guide and Contact Information. Once repairs are completed, submit the claim along with attachments required for digital documentation, repair order, and technician copy in WebDCS Foreign Vehicle Warranty Claim screen. Submit the applicable SRC or 3 rd party rental in the Rental Claim screen. For any questions relating to foreign vehicle claims, please submit inquiries by using the Warranty Support Portal in WebDCS. Reimbursement Policies 127
coverage_details: Claims for foreign vehicles being serviced and operated in the United States
dealer_actions: Submit claims using the Foreign Vehicle Warranty Claim screen in Hyundai WebDCS under the Warranty tab.
Go to WEBDCS > Warranty Tab > Information > Foreign Vehicle Information screen to obtain production and in-service dates of the foreign VIN.
Contact the country of origin prior to performing any repairs to verify warranty coverage or for technical support.
Find contact information for countries of origin on the Foreign Vehicle Warranty Coverage Guide and Contact Information.
Once repairs are completed, submit the claim along with required attachments (digital documentation, repair order, and technician copy) in the WebDCS Foreign Vehicle Warranty Claim screen.
Submit the applicable SRC or 3rd party rental in the Rental Claim screen.
For questions relating to foreign vehicle claims, submit inquiries using the Warranty Support Portal in WebDCS.

title: Dealership Operational Procedures and Customer Issue Resolution
raw_content: Repeat visits for the same issue : Must contact Hyundai Techline (800) 325-6604 for assistance when having trouble in diagnosing, identifying, or repairing a customer's complaint before beginning the Second, Third, Fourth, or greater repair attempt for the same problem. Document the contact date, time, and case number on the repair order. Days out of service : If a vehicle is down for over 10 days for parts backorder or technical issues, Dealer must contact the DPSM. If the parts necessary for the repair are on back order and do not affect either the (1) drivability of the vehicle or (2) the safety of the vehicle, Dealer shall return the vehicle to the customer and close out the repair order while the parts are on order. Any parts needed to repair an inoperative Hyundai vehicle must be ordered as an “Emergency Order” and upgraded to “XVOR” if the part is on back order. Customer Satisfaction : Notify the DPSM and Regional Consumer Affairs Manager whenever experiencing an issue with customer satisfaction due to warrantable repairs or days out of service. Refer the customer to Hyundai Consumer Affairs line 1-800-633-5151 and the BBB Auto Line 1-800-955-5100. Reimbursement Policies 128
dealer_actions: Contact Hyundai Techline (800) 325-6604 for assistance when having trouble in diagnosing, identifying, or repairing a customer's complaint before beginning the Second, Third, Fourth, or greater repair attempt for the same problem.
Document the contact date, time, and case number on the repair order for repeat visits.
If a vehicle is down for over 10 days for parts backorder or technical issues, contact the DPSM.
If necessary parts for repair are on back order and do not affect vehicle drivability or safety, return the vehicle to the customer and close the repair order while parts are on order.
Order any parts needed to repair an inoperative Hyundai vehicle as an “Emergency Order” and upgrade to “XVOR” if the part is on back order.
Notify the DPSM and Regional Consumer Affairs Manager whenever experiencing an issue with customer satisfaction due to warrantable repairs or days out of service.
Refer the customer to Hyundai Consumer Affairs line 1-800-633-5151 and the BBB Auto Line 1-800-955-5100 for customer satisfaction issues.

title: Sublet Repair Requirements
raw_content: Sublet repairs are an important part of dealer operations but carry additional responsibilities to maintain compliance with safety regulations and customer satisfaction expectations. This policy outlines the requirements for sublet repairs. These requirements are designed to protect the integrity of repairs and ensure that all work performed meets OE specifications. Below are common sublet repairs and sublet repair requirements. Paint/Anti-Perforation To ensure high-quality repairs, compliance with environmental and safety regulations and to maintain consistent industry standards, dealers should not engage or authorize mobile paint operations for any paint repair, refinishing work or body panel repairs. All paint and body related repairs must be performed at a fixed, licensed facility that meets local, state and federal regulations. Repairs must be completed in a controlled environment to ensure proper curing, finish quality, and adherence to OE specifications. All invoices must be typed and printed. Handwritten invoices shall not be allowed for repair transactions. This requirement ensures legibility, accuracy and documentation compliance. Sublet vendors must use an electronic invoicing system or a computer-generated format. Invoices must include: • Vendor name, address and contact information • Detailed description of services performed • Itemized costs for each services performed • Date of service Glass Dealers must ensure that all sublet glass installations are performed by qualified vendors in compliance with OE specifications and safety standards. If a windshield or other glass component is found damaged or compromised due to improper installation by a sublet glass vendor, the sublet vendor shall be fully responsible for repairing any damage caused by the improper installation, providing and installing a replacement windshield or glass component at no additional cost, and responsible for any related labor and materials necessary to restore the vehicle to OE standards. Cylinder Head Inspection All invoices must include: • Vendor name, address and contact information • Detailed description of services performed • Itemized costs for each services performed Reimbursement Policies 129 • Date of service Failure to comply with Sublet Repair Requirements may result in denial of claim approval or claim being subject to chargeback. Dealer are responsible for ensuring that all sublet operations adhere to the requirements outlined. Record Retention & Administration Requirements 130 Record Retention & Administration Requirements
coverage_details: Sublet repairs are permitted to maintain compliance with safety regulations and OE specifications.
Paint and body related repairs must be performed at a fixed, licensed facility meeting local, state, and federal regulations.
Repairs must be completed in a controlled environment to ensure proper curing, finish quality, and adherence to OE specifications.
Sublet glass installations must be performed by qualified vendors in compliance with OE specifications and safety standards.

If a windshield or other glass component is damaged due to improper installation by a sublet glass vendor, the vendor is responsible for repairing damage, providing and installing a replacement, and covering related labor and materials to restore the vehicle to OE standards.
exclusions: Mobile paint operations are not allowed for any paint repair, refinishing work, or body panel repairs.
Handwritten invoices are not allowed for repair transactions.
dealer_actions: Ensure all sublet work performed meets OE specifications.
Do not engage or authorize mobile paint operations.
Ensure all paint and body related repairs are performed at a fixed, licensed facility.
Ensure repairs are completed in a controlled environment.
Ensure all invoices for sublet repairs are typed and printed.
Ensure sublet vendors use an electronic invoicing system or a computer-generated format.
Ensure invoices include: Vendor name, address and contact information.
Ensure invoices include: Detailed description of services performed.
Ensure invoices include: Itemized costs for each service performed.
Ensure invoices include: Date of service.
Ensure all sublet glass installations are performed by qualified vendors.
Ensure sublet glass installations comply with OE specifications and safety standards.
In cases of improper sublet glass installation, ensure the sublet vendor takes full responsibility for all repairs, replacements, and associated costs.
Understand that failure to comply with Sublet Repair Requirements may result in denial of claim approval or claim being subject to chargeback.
Responsible for ensuring that all sublet operations adhere to the requirements outlined.

title: Record Retention & Administration Requirements
raw_content: The information supplied on the repair order, warranty claim form, and other supporting documents is intended to substantiate the Dealer’s claim for payment. In addition, this information assists the Manufacturer in determining which parts are failing, why they are failing, and provides information for evaluation of the reliability of the product. It is the Dealer’s responsibility to ensure that every claim submitted contains complete, accurate information, that the claim is fully and correctly executed, and that the supporting documentation, including the repair order, is legible and understandable. This policy also applies to other claim types submitted to HMA for payment; including Campaign, PDI, Goodwill, and Retail Part. It is also the Dealer’s responsibility to submit the claim within the required time interval. All warranty claims must be submitted to HMA within 30 days of the repair order close date unless state law provides otherwise. The repair order close date is the date when the vehicle repairs have been completed and should similarly coincide with the date the customer picks up their vehicle. All warranty claims returned to a Dealer as incomplete or incorrect must also be resubmitted to HMA within 30 days from the date the claim was returned to Dealer. Failure to fully and accurately complete and submit the claim will be sufficient grounds for rejection or chargeback of the claim. The record retention and submission requirements stated in the following articles apply to the submission of all types of claims submitted to HMA for payment, including warranty, Campaign, PDI, Goodwill, and Retail Part. The requirements apply to both traditional manual repair order systems and electronic repair order systems unless specifically noted otherwise. Record Retention & Administration Requirements 131
coverage_details: Warranty claims
Campaign claims
PDI claims
Goodwill claims
Retail Part claims
Claims submitted via traditional manual repair order systems
Claims submitted via electronic repair order systems
exclusions: Claims that are not fully and accurately completed and submitted are subject to rejection or chargeback
Claims submitted outside the required time interval are subject to rejection or chargeback
dealer_actions: Ensure every claim submitted contains complete, accurate information
Ensure every claim is fully and correctly executed
Ensure supporting documentation, including the repair order, is legible and understandable
Submit claims within the required time interval
Submit all warranty claims to HMA within 30 days of the repair order close date, unless state law provides otherwise
Resubmit any incomplete or incorrect warranty claims returned by HMA within 30 days from the date the claim was returned to the Dealer
Supply information on the repair order, warranty claim form, and other supporting documents to substantiate claims for payment

title: Record Retention & Administration Requirements
raw_content: The repair order is a document that is generated in response to a customer complaint or request. Occasionally, a request indicates only the need for an explanation of a certain feature or the proper operation of a particular component or accessory. In these cases it may not always be necessary to generate a repair order. A repair order must be written or computer generated when: • An inspection of the vehicle is required. • A repair, replacement, or adjustment is to be performed (including sublet or outside repairs). • A Pre-Delivery Inspection service is to be performed. • A customer statement needs to be documented for future reference. • A campaign (recall) or owner notification program repair or adjustment is to be performed. • Only one diagnostic labor operation (RQ0) is allowed for each repair order (RO). A properly written repair order will: • Describe work in complaint, cause, and correction (3C) format. • Accurately record the customer’s complaint as stated by the customer. • Communicate to the technician either the specific symptoms that the vehicle is exhibiting or a customer complaint for further diagnosis. • Document technician verification of the complaint and indicate the cause of the complaint. • Document the corrective action used to resolve the complaint. • Complete the Technician Comments section on every repair order. • Substantiate the warranty repair in compliance with HMA’s warranty stipulations. Because every warranty claim has its origin in a repair order, a complete and legible repair order will ensure: • Faster warranty payment • Fewer returned claims • Reduced administrative workload The Dealership’s customer service file and the Hyundai WebDCS Warranty Vehicle Information screen must always be checked during the repair order write-up process. A printout of the Warranty Vehicle Information screen must be attached to the service file copy of the RO. This includes dealerships which have an electronic service file as well. This ensures that prior repairs to the same or similar component are recognized. Repeat repairs due to poor diagnosis or improper repair are not covered under warranty. It is the responsibility of the dealership to stand behind its workmanship. Shop comebacks are not acceptable under warranty. Record Retention & Administration Requirements 132 Checking and attaching a copy of the Hyundai WebDCS Warranty Vehicle Information screen will ensure that the Dealer is aware of any outstanding campaigns on the vehicle as well as any applicable HPP or CPO status on the vehicle. Hyundai Motor America does not require the utilization of a specific repair order form or that the format be organized in a specifically defined manner. HMA does however stipulate that the repair order form utilized be clear and legible, contain specific details of information, and be administered as follows: 1. The repair order form utilized for warranty claim submission must contain at least the four following copies: • Dealer Accounting File Copy (Initial repair order and final invoice copies for electronic repair orders) • Service File Copy or Parts Scrapping Copy • Shop Hard Copy (Technician Input) • Customer Receipt Copy (Initial repair order and final invoice copies for electronic repair orders) In certain states or local jurisdictions, additional requirements may be stipulated. Dealers should contact their legal counsel for clarification on this issue. Carbonless or transferable areas on the repair order form ensure the transfer of appropriate information to certain copies only. It is appropriate that repair order copies contain only pertinent information. The Dealer must retain the repair order copies in the appropriate area. Accounting copy files must be kept in numerical sequence in the business office. Customer service file copies and technician input shop hard copies must be retained in the service department organized by vehicle identification number (VIN) in numerical sequence. If an electronic repair order system is used, copies of the initial customer write-up repair order must be retained with the accounting files along with a copy of the final invoice. Electronic facsimile paper copies of the final repair order must also be retained in the customer service file in the service department. The Dealer must provide each owner for whom warranty or customer satisfaction service is performed with a completed copy of the repair order stating all repairs and services performed. Certain states or local jurisdictions stipulate that a customer be provided an estimate copy at the time of original write-up. Dealers should contact their legal counsel for clarification on this issue. Note: In dealerships which utilize an electronic repair order system, the Customer must be provided a copy of both the original initial write-up repair order and the final invoice. Likewise, Customer service file copies of both the original initial write-up repair order and the final Record Retention & Administration Requirements 133 invoice, including all parts replaced, must be retained in the service department, organized by vehicle identification number (VIN) in numerical sequence. 2. Repair orders issued to perform a Pre-Delivery Inspection must include the following minimum information: • Service Manager’s clear and legible signature • Dealer Logo and Address • Repair Order Number • Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) • Vehicle mileage before and after the required test drive • Technician time recording - See section 6.3 for requirements • Repair Order Date • Purchaser’s name, address and telephone number – If the vehicle has not been sold by the date of performance of the Pre-Delivery Inspection, use the dealer’s assigned vehicle stock number in place of purchaser’s information. 3. Repair orders issued to perform warranty, Campaign, or Goodwill repairs must include the following minimum information: • Repair Order Number • Dealer Logo and Address • Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) • Vehicle Mileage - In and Out – HMA’s policy is to recalibrate the new speedometer with the vehicle mileage at the time of failure replacement. In cases where dealer is unable to locate a vendor to recalibrate the new speedometer, then the repaired/replaced odometer should be adjusted to read 0 miles and a Speedometer/Odometer Repair/Replacement Sticker should be affixed to the driver side door frame indicating that such a repair was performed. These speedometer stickers are available from HMA‘s Non-Parts Warehouse, part #00924-00000. If a vehicle comes into your dealership for service which has a Speedometer/Odometer Repair/Replacement Sticker on the door frame, please add the mileages of the odometer and sticker to obtain the true mileage of the vehicle. State and local laws and regulations for this repair should also be reviewed to determine if there are additional obligations which might apply. • Repair Order Open Date and Close Date (close date is the date the vehicle leaves the dealership-document if customer doesn’t pick up vehicle when first informed that it is ready) • Vehicle Delivery Date or Date of First Use • Owner/Customer’s name, address and telephone number • Complaint - Description of the customer’s complaint as stated by the customer (the repair order should state words such as “customer states” followed by customer’s complaint) Record Retention & Administration Requirements 134 • Cause - Description of cause of complaint • Correction - Description of the actions taken by the technician to correct the complaint • Customer’s signature for initial repair order write-up • Customer’s signature for receipt of repair completed vehicle • Claim Number(s) • Prior approval number authorized by the Prior Approval Center (if applicable) • Nature Code and Explanation • Cause Code and Explanation • Labor Dollar Amount (if applicable) • Labor Operation Code(s), Quantities and Hours (if applicable) • Part(s) Dollar Amount (if applicable) • Part(s) Quantity (if applicable) • Causal Part Number • Replacement Part Numbers (if applicable) • Sublet Amount and Sublet Code(s) (if applicable) • Sublet Final Invoice Number and Name (if applicable) • Towing Amount (if applicable) • Towing Invoice Number and Name (if applicable) • Freight Amount (if applicable) • Freight Carrier (if applicable) • Freight Shipping Date (if applicable) • For Replacement Service Parts: the date of previous, original installation; previous, original mileage, and previous, original repair order or counter sales ticket number (if applicable) • Technician Comments 4. The repair order’s technician input/shop hard copy must be separated from the repair order’s remaining soft copies prior to the work being performed. The technician is to be given the technician input/ shop hard copy of the repair order as the technician’s work copy at the time of repair performance. The remaining repair order soft copies are to be sent to the Parts Department or elsewhere as needed. The complete repair order, including the technician input/shop hard copy and all the remaining soft copies are to be sent to the appropriate area for close out of the repair order after completion of the repair. 5. The repair order must contain all parts replaced as per the repair. The parts listed must be legible and contain: • Complete Replacement Part Number(s) • Replacement Part Quantity(s) issued • Dealer Net cost of replacement part(s) on the date the repair is performed. (The extended total Dealer net costs must be stated for replacement part quantities greater than 1) Record Retention & Administration Requirements 135 If an automated parts invoice or a separate parts invoice is used, the automated or separate parts invoice must contain cross-reference to the repair order and be attached to the technician input/shop hard copy that is retained in the customer/VIN service file. The repair order must also contain cross-reference to the automated or separate parts invoice. 6. The customer must sign and approve all repairs for complaints stated on the repair order. The customer must sign the repair order prior to the work being performed and upon receipt of the vehicle after completion of the repairs. Dealerships which utilize an electronic repair order system must ensure that a customer signature is obtained on both the original initial write-up repair order copy as well as the final invoice copy. Digital signatures, or other electronic acknowledgements (E.g., signatures on a tablet, approval via text, email, or other app), are acceptable means of capturing customer authorizations. Electronic acknowledgements should be dated and time stamped, printed, attached, and retained as part of the work order. If the customer uses a night drop envelope, the night drop envelope must contain the customer’s signature and should contain the date of drop off. The signed night drop envelope must be retained in the service files attached to the technician input/shop hard copy of the repair order. In the event that the Dealer cannot obtain a customer signature on a night drop envelope of a repair order, the Service Manager must sign and date the repair order. The Service Manager must sign the repair order for vehicles repaired that are in stock and for the repair orders issued to perform the Pre-Delivery Inspection. Digital signatures (including the ones captured via tablet) are acceptable means of capturing the Service Manager’s signature. Upon completion of the repairs and receipt of the vehicle, the customer must receive the customer receipt copy of the completed repair order which must contain a description of the warranty repairs performed and the parts replaced on the vehicle. 7. The technician input/shop hard copy or electronic hard copy of the repair order must contain the technician’s comments and description of the repair the technician performed. The description written by the technician must include the cause of the customer’s complaint and include notes describing the diagnostic process. The technician must also document the corrective action used to resolve the complaint. In addition, technical measurements or readings that are required in order to determine the serviceability or performance of a part or assembly, as stated in the applicable shop manuals, must be stated on the technician input shop hard copy/electronic hard copy of the repair order. Repairs should follow shop manuals procedures including replacing one-time use parts. One- time use parts designated in order to perform the repair need to be charged out on the warranty repair order in order complete the repair properly. If a warranty repair requires one-time use parts to properly repair a customer vehicle and the warranty claim is submitted without the one- time use parts, the warranty claim may be subject to denial or chargeback. Record Retention & Administration Requirements 136 Ensure that all repairs are carried out in accordance with the latest published procedures detailed in shop manuals or other service related publications such as Technical Service Bulletins and campaigns. Refer to the most recent model and model year specific repair manual Technical Service Bulletin for diagnostic and available repair details. Before and after readings/measurements must be recorded and documented. Parts requiring measurements and readings include, but are not limited to: • Air conditioning Evac & Recharge refrigerant • Automatic transmission oil pressure test/Diagnosis Worksheet • ATM Tester • Battery tester readings/Battery code and printout • Brake discs/rotors • Brake shoes • Idle speed/Throttle body • Cylinder compression • Cylinder leak down • Emission test readings • Engine Diagnosis Worksheet • GDS test printouts/readings • Gear box preload • Headlamp aiming • Ignition timing • Oil consumption test • Test drive results • Valve clearances • Vehicle Tracking Data Sheet • Wheel alignment, including Tire Balance Data Sheet • Wheel balance Note: Go to www.HyundaiDealer.com under the Service tab/Dealer Resources/Documents Library/ Warranty for the latest Test and Measurement Documentation Chart Dealerships which utilize an electronic repair order must adhere to the same requirements, and technicians must input their comments into a computer terminal. In all instances where technician’s comments are updated, a hard copy of the repair order with the technician’s comments must be generated and retained in the customer/VIN service file. This requirement also applies to repairs orders when information is entered using a digital service lane technology application. 8. The individual technician performing the repair must be identified by name or number on the face of the technician input shop hard copy of the repair order next to each labor operation the Record Retention & Administration Requirements 137 technician performed. Technician identification documented for a repair must be true and accurate. This requirement also applies to dealerships which utilize a team system. Identification by team only is not adequate. A code or name must also be utilized with an electronic repair order system. 9. All information added to the technician input shop hard copy of the repair order after the initial repair order write-up with the customer, must be transferred and added to the remaining repair order soft copies, including the customer receipt copy. Subsequent generations of a given repair order in an electronic repair order system must be printed and retained in the customer/VIN service file such that the evolution of a repair order can be demonstrated. 10. Warranty repairs made without a customer complaint are add-on repairs, and are only allowed under these circumstances: • For completion of applicable campaigns or recalls. • For repairs to items that would seriously impact the operational safety of the vehicle (for example SRS light, ABS light, Check Engine Light, or profuse fluid leaks). Add-on Warranty repairs must adhere to the following administration requirements: – The technician must provide a complete description of the needed work on the hard copy of the repair order. The description must include the cause, including notes describing the diagnosis. The technician must also document the corrective action. – The Service Manager must verify the needed repair and sign next to the added repair on the hard copy of the repair order. This authorization must be clearly documented next to the technician’s description of the problem prior to the work being performed. A Service Manager’s signature at the top or bottom of the invoice is not acceptable. If the customer on the repair order was notified of the add-on repair by phone, it is acceptable to document on the repair order hard copy the date, time, contact method and the name of the customer who authorized the add-on repair. The customer who authorized the add-on repair should be the same customer on the repair order. The Service Manager’s signature must be next to the add-on repair line. – The additional repair information must be transferred and added to all remaining copies of the repair order. – The customer’s signature must appear next to the additional repair that has been added to the repair order. The signature must be next to the added repair description. A customer’s signature at the top or bottom of the invoice is not acceptable. – If an electronic repair order system is used, the add-on repair must be indicated with some sort of identifier such as an “*”. Record Retention & Administration Requirements 138 If a warranty repair is generated from a customer pay repair order, whereby the dealer uses separate repair orders for customer pay and warranty, the repair is considered an add-on repair. These repairs are subject to the same requirements as repairs made without a customer complaint. The customer pay repair order must have these items: • The Service Manager’s signature next to the added repair(s) on the hard copy of the repair order. • The warranty repair description. • A cross-reference to the warranty repair order. Complaints added by the customer after the time of initial write-up are customer add-on repairs. The date and time the additional request was made must be documented on the repair order. The following authorization requirements apply: • The Service Manager must sign next to the added customer complaint before the repair work is performed. The signature must be next to the added repair description. A Service Manager’s signature at the top or bottom of the invoice is not acceptable. If the customer on the repair order was notified of the add-on repair by phone, it is acceptable to document on the repair order hard copy the date, time, contact method and the name of the customer who authorized the add-on repair. The customer who authorized the add-on repair should be the same customer on the repair order. The Service Manager’s signature must be next to the add-on repair line. 11. In certain cases, a repair cannot be completed due to replacement parts not being available. In these cases, do not submit the warranty claim for the diagnosis or repair until the part is installed and the repair completed. Drivable vehicles are to be returned to the customer while waiting for the part to arrive. When the vehicle is returned to the customer, fill in the “date/mileage in” and “date/mileage out” fields accurately on the repair order. On the repair order, be sure to add a statement that the vehicle is drivable and was returned while waiting for the parts to arrive. When the part arrives to the dealer: • Open a new repair order using the date and mileage of when the vehicle was originally presented for diagnosis or repair. • On the new repair order, refer to the original repair order number when the part was originally ordered and repeat the original customer complaint statement. • In the customer concern section of the new repair order, input the repair completion date and mileage. • In the customer concern section of the new repair order, include the following information: o The date and mileage when the vehicle was originally presented for diagnosis or repair. o The date the parts were ordered o The parts status at the time originally ordered (e.g. backordered, etc.). o The date the parts arrived at the dealer. Record Retention & Administration Requirements 139 o The date and mileage of when the repairs were completed. Record Retention & Administration Requirements 140
coverage_details: A repair order must be written or computer generated when an inspection of the vehicle is required.
A repair order must be written or computer generated when a repair, replacement, or adjustment is to be performed (including sublet or outside repairs).
A repair order must be written or computer generated when a Pre-Delivery Inspection service is to be performed.
A repair order must be written or computer generated when a customer statement needs to be documented for future reference.
A repair order must be written or computer generated when a campaign (recall) or owner notification program repair or adjustment is to be performed.
Only one diagnostic labor operation (RQ0) is allowed for each repair order (RO).
A properly written repair order will describe work in complaint, cause, and correction (3C) format.
A properly written repair order will accurately record the customer’s complaint as stated by the customer.
A properly written repair order will communicate to the technician either the specific symptoms that the vehicle is exhibiting or a customer complaint for further diagnosis.
A properly written repair order will document technician verification of the complaint and indicate the cause of the complaint.
A properly written repair order will document the corrective action used to resolve the complaint.
A properly written repair order will complete the Technician Comments section on every repair order.
A properly written repair order will substantiate the warranty repair in compliance with HMA’s warranty stipulations.
Add-on warranty repairs made without a customer complaint are allowed for completion of applicable campaigns or recalls.

Add-on warranty repairs made without a customer complaint are allowed for repairs to items that would seriously impact the operational safety of the vehicle (e.g., SRS light, ABS light, Check Engine Light, or profuse fluid leaks).
exclusions: Repeat repairs due to poor diagnosis or improper repair are not covered under warranty.
