title: Record Retention & Administration Requirements
raw_content: HMA highly encourages Dealers to establish, maintain and operate an effective system of internal controls to meet HMA reporting and record keeping requirements. These internal controls are critical components of a Dealer’s parts & service management and can help Dealers achieve their goals and long-term profitability. Warranty audits not only cover the warranty aspect of the Dealer’s parts & service operation, but also provide an opportunity to assess the effectiveness of internal controls and evaluate any deficiencies and/ or discrepancies in a Dealer’s Warranty Policy and Procedures compliance. At the completion of the audit, all noncompliant audit findings are documented in the audit report and may result in a chargeback to the Dealer. In addition, noncompliant audit findings resulting from irregularities and/or discrepancies are included in the audit report as formal requirements and recommendations designed to assist the Dealer in operating a more efficient, profitable, and properly controlled parts & service operation. Record Retention & Administration Requirements 155
coverage_details: Warranty audits cover the warranty aspect of the Dealer’s parts & service operation.
Audits assess the effectiveness of internal controls.
Audits evaluate deficiencies and discrepancies in a Dealer’s Warranty Policy and Procedures compliance.
exclusions: Noncompliant audit findings that may result in a chargeback to the Dealer.
dealer_actions: Establish, maintain, and operate an effective system of internal controls to meet HMA reporting and record keeping requirements.
Comply with HMA Warranty Policy and Procedures.
Address formal requirements and recommendations included in audit reports resulting from irregularities and/or discrepancies.

title: Warranty Claim Register Administration and Reconciliation
raw_content: A Warranty Claim Register provides the Dealer with a summary of the status of the warranty accounts receivable from HMA. A properly maintained warranty claim register enables the Dealer to reconcile the Warranty Settlement Statement. Reconciliation of warranty claims to the Warranty Settlement Statement will indicate when credits for warranty adjustments are being received and whether or not there are any amount differences between amounts claimed and the amounts credited. The Warranty Claim Register should be administered as follows: • Post each warranty claim submitted to HMA to the Warranty Claim Register daily. The information posted should include at a minimum: – Repair Order Number – Claim Number assigned by Dealer – Warranty claim amount – Warranty claim initial submission date to HMA – Warranty claim return date, if applicable – Warranty claim return reason(s), if applicable – Warranty claim resubmission date(s), if applicable – Warranty claim payment date – Warranty claim payment amount – Warranty claim different appeal date(s), if applicable – Warranty parts request date, if applicable – Warranty parts return date, if applicable – Warranty date to scrap – Warranty date scrap initial – Warranty claim chargeback date, if applicable Posting to the Warranty Claim Register should be initiated when a warranty claim is submitted to HMA. Upon notification of claim status, such as payment or return, as per the Warranty Settlement Statement or by the DCS daily answerback (Claim Status Report), the claim’s status should be posted to the Warranty Claim Register. • Post the following data from the Warranty Settlement Statement to the Warranty Claim Register upon receipt of the Warranty Settlement Statement: – Payment/Return date – Claim credit amount • Note any discrepancies between the requested input data per the claim submitted over WebDCS and the Warranty Settlement Statement and add remarks, if necessary, for the difference in the Warranty Claim Register. Examples of discrepancies include: Record Retention & Administration Requirements 156 – Errors or differences in the amount credited. – Claims returned for correction should have the date of return posted and the date of the corrected resubmission posted. – If a returned claim is not to be resubmitted, note it in the remarks section. The Warranty Claim Register and the Warranty Settlement Statement must be retained in the dealership accounting office files. If the Warranty Claim Register is automated, a copy must be printed monthly and retained. The Warranty Settlement Statement should be filed chronologically by statement date and month sequence. Hyundai Warranty Claim Registers are available free through the Hyundai Non-Parts Warehouse. The part number is NP449-MV010. An order quantity of one (1) provides ten (10) register sheets. Record Retention & Administration Requirements 157
coverage_details: Tracking the status of warranty accounts receivable from HMA
Reconciliation of warranty claims against Warranty Settlement Statements
Monitoring for receipt of credits for warranty adjustments
Identifying differences between amounts claimed and amounts credited
dealer_actions: Post each warranty claim submitted to HMA to the Warranty Claim Register daily

Include the following information for each claim posted: Repair Order Number, Claim Number assigned by Dealer, Warranty claim amount, Initial submission date to HMA, Return date (if applicable), Return reason(s) (if applicable), Resubmission date(s) (if applicable), Payment date, Payment amount, Different appeal date(s) (if applicable), Parts request date (if applicable), Parts return date (if applicable), Date to scrap, Date scrap initial, Chargeback date (if applicable)
Initiate posting to the Warranty Claim Register when a warranty claim is submitted to HMA
Post claim status (e.g., payment or return) to the Warranty Claim Register upon notification from the Warranty Settlement Statement or DCS daily answerback (Claim Status Report)
Upon receipt of the Warranty Settlement Statement, post Payment/Return date to the Warranty Claim Register
Upon receipt of the Warranty Settlement Statement, post Claim credit amount to the Warranty Claim Register
Note any discrepancies between the requested input data per the claim submitted over WebDCS and the Warranty Settlement Statement in the Warranty Claim Register
Add remarks for differences in the Warranty Claim Register if necessary (e.g., errors or differences in the amount credited)
For claims returned for correction, post the date of return and the date of the corrected resubmission
If a returned claim is not to be resubmitted, note it in the remarks section of the Warranty Claim Register
Retain the Warranty Claim Register and the Warranty Settlement Statement in the dealership accounting office files
If the Warranty Claim Register is automated, print a copy monthly and retain it
File the Warranty Settlement Statement chronologically by statement date and month sequence

title: Warranty and PDI Settlement Statement Processing Guide
raw_content: HMA issues a weekly Warranty Settlement Statement and a PDI Settlement Statement twice a month. These statements detail all the actions taken on each individual warranty, PDI, and transportation claim received and processed since the last Warranty Statement. This information must be reconciled with the Warranty Claim Register. The Warranty Statement lists the claims in Repair Order Number sequence and groups the claims in several sections. Note: You may reprint your Warranty Settlement Statement via Hyundaidealer.com under the Service tab / WebDCS / DCS Reports. The ‘Report ID’ for the Settlement Statement is HMA- 4301. To view recent Settlement Statements, click on ‘Search All Reports’ and enter a date range. The format for any dates listed on this report are YYMMDD (Year, Year, Month, Month, Day, Day), example: 23 01 31. The sections are as follows: Claims Paid This Statement This section lists warranty claims that have been paid since the last warranty statement was issued. These claims are sorted by repair order. • The “Init Submit” field lists the date you initially submitted the warranty claim. • The “Other Approved” field is the sum of SUBLET AMOUNT and TOWING AMOUNT. Section 2: Claims Paid This Statement–Reprocessed/Credited The claims in this section were originally accepted for payment and then either appealed, charged back via DCS or phone, resubmitted and paid within the same statement period. • The “Other Amount” field is the sum of SUBLET AMOUNT and TOWING AMOUNT. • “Trans Type” is defined as follows: ORIG – Original claim total approved for payment. DEBIT – Chargeback of original claim total approved for payment. PAY – Current claim amount approved for payment. Section 3: Claims Paid Prior Statement–Reprocessed/Credited The claims in this section were originally paid on a previous statement, then either appealed, charged back via DCS or phone charged back, resubmitted and paid. • The “Other Amount” field is the sum of SUBLET AMOUNT and TOWING AMOUNT. • TRANS TYPE relates to the TOTAL AMOUNT. The transaction types are as follows: ORIG – This amount is a reference for you and is NOT factored in the total amount on the summary page. The ORIG amount was paid to you on a previous Record Retention & Administration Requirements 158 statement for this claim. It will NOT be charged back from your account–an adjustment is made to it on the current statement. ADJ – This amount is NOT a chargeback – it is used to determine the “Net Adjusted” payment. CRDT – This amount is NOT a credit – it is used to determine the “Net Adjusted” payment. The CRDT amount is the amount paid to you or the corrected, current claim. • NET ADJUSTED is the net difference amount you will be paid from HMA for your corrected claim. The adjusted payment is calculated by subtracting the ADJ from the CRDT amount. This amount will be factored into the Total Amount at the end of the section and the end of the report. • LAST STATEMENT DATE is the date the original claim was paid. • RETURN REASON/S are listed under each claim to explain why the ADJ was made. Section 4: Claims Paid This Statement– Charged Back The claims in this section were originally accepted for payment on this statement, then either charged back at the request of the dealer or by HMA for reason(s) stated under the “Return Reason” field. The “Net Debit” amount for each claim in this section will always offset the original payment amount. • The “Other Amount” field is the sum of SUBLET AMOUNT and TOWING AMOUNT. • TRANS TYPE defines the TOTAL AMOUNT. The transaction types are as follows: ORIG – Total amount originally paid on the claim. DEBIT – Total amount your claim was charged back. • RETURN REASON/S are listed under each claim to explain why the charge back was made. Section 5: Claims Paid Prior Statement– Charged back This section lists claims that were paid to the dealer on a previous statement, then charged back at the request of the dealer or by HMA for the reason(s) stated under the “Return Reason’s” field. • The “Other Amount” field is the sum of SUBLET AMOUNT and TOWING AMOUNT. • TRANS TYPE defines the TOTAL AMOUNT. The transaction types are as follows: ORIG – Total amount originally paid on the claim. DEBIT – Total amount your claim was charged back. • RETURN REASON/S are listed under each claim to explain why the chargeback was made. Section 6: Claims Currently In Suspense Claims are placed in suspense by HMA for a variety of reasons. The claims will only remain in suspense for a maximum of 48 hours, at which time they will either be paid, sent to Prior Approval Center or returned for more information. This section will list claims placed in suspense at the time the Warranty Statement is printed. Record Retention & Administration Requirements 159 Section 7: Claims Currently Pending PA Center Review Claims are placed in pending PA Center review by HMA for a variety of reasons. This section will list claims placed in pending PA Center review at the time the Warranty Settlement Statement is printed. Claims Returned This Statement Claims in this section, were submitted to HMA and returned for correction or more information since the last issuance of the Warranty Statement. The claims should be corrected, re-entered, and resubmitted through DCS for payment. • RETURN REASON/S are listed under each claim to explain why the claim was returned. Claims Previously Returned But Not Resubmitted Claims in this section were submitted to HMA and returned for correction or more information, on a previous Warranty Statement and not resubmitted. This section contains claims returned within a 90 day period prior to the current statement. The claims should be corrected, re- entered, and resubmitted through DCS for payment. • RETURN REASON/S are listed under each claim to explain why the claim was returned. Section 10: Transportation Claims Paid This Statement This section lists transportation claims that have been paid since the last warranty statement. The claims are sorted by repair order. • The “Init Submit” field lists the date you initially submitted the transportation claim. • The “Other Approved” field is the sum of additional expense(s) claimed (see Section 2.2.4). Section 11: Transportation Claims Paid This Statement – Reprocessed/Credited The transportation claims in this section were originally accepted for payment and then either appealed, charged back via DCS, or phone charged back, resubmitted and paid within the same statement period. • The “Other Approved” field is the sum of additional expense(s) claimed (see Section 2.2.4). • “Trans Type” is defined as follows: ORIG – Original claim total approved for payment. DEBIT – Chargeback of original claim total approved for payment. PAY – Current claim amount approved for payment. Section 12: Transportation Claims Paid Prior Statement – Reprocessed/Credited The transportation claims in this section were originally paid on a previous statement, then either appealed, charged back via DCS, or phone charged back, resubmitted and paid. • The “Other Amount” field is the sum of additional expense(s) claimed (see Section 2.2.4). • TRANS TYPE relates to the TOTAL AMOUNT. The transaction types are as follows: Record Retention & Administration Requirements 160 ORIG – This amount is a reference for you and is NOT factored in the total amount on the summary page. The ORIG amount was paid to you on a previous statement for this claim. It will NOT be charged back from your account – an adjustment is made to it on the current statement. ADJ – This amount is NOT a chargeback – it is used to determine the “Net Adjusted” payment. CRDT – This amount is NOT a credit – it is used to determine the “Net Adjusted” payment. The CRDT amount is the amount paid to you on the corrected, current claim. • NET ADJUSTED is the net difference amount you will be paid from HMA for your corrected claim. The adjusted payment is calculated by subtracting the ADJ from the CRDT amount. This amount will be factored into the Total Amount at the end of the section and the end of the report. • LAST STATEMENT DATE is the date the original claim was paid. Section 13 : Transportation Claims Paid This Statement– Charged back The transportation claims in this section were originally accepted for payment on this statement, and have either been charged back at the request of the dealer or by HMA for reason stated under the “Return Reason” field. The “Net Debit” amount for each claim in this section will always be $0.00 as the chargeback amount will always offset the original payment amount. • The “Other Amount” field is the sum of additional expense(s) claimed (see Section 2.2.4). • TRANS TYPE defines the TOTAL AMOUNT. The transaction types are as follows: ORIG – Total amount originally paid on the claim. DEBIT – Total amount your claim was charged back. • RETURN REASON/S are listed under each claim to explain why the chargeback was made. Section 14: Transportation Claims Paid Prior Statement– Charged back This section lists claims that were paid to you on a previous statement, then charged back at the request of the dealer or by HMA for the reason(s) stated under the “Return Reason/s” field. • The “Other Amount” field is the sum of additional expense(s) claimed (see Section 2.2.4). • TRANS TYPE defines the TOTAL AMOUNT. The transaction types are as follows: ORIG – Total amount originally paid on the claim. DEBIT – Total amount your claim was charged back. • RETURN REASON/S are listed under each claim to explain why the chargeback was made. Section 15: Transportation Claims Currently In Suspense Transportation claims are placed in suspense by HMA for a variety of reasons. The claims will only remain in suspense for a maximum of 48 hours, at which time they will either be paid, sent Record Retention & Administration Requirements 161 to Prior Approval Center or returned for more information. This section will list claims placed in suspense at the time the Warranty Statement is printed. Section 16: Transportation Claims Currently Pending PA Center Review Transportation claims are placed in pending PA Center review by HMA for a variety of reasons. This section will list claims placed in pending PA Center review at the time the Warranty Settlement Statement is printed. Section 17: Transportation Claims Returned This Statement Transportation claims in this section were submitted to HMA and returned for correction or more information since the last issuance of the Warranty Statement. The claims should be corrected, re-entered, and resubmitted through DCS for payment. • RETURN REASONS are listed under each claim to explain why the claim was returned. Section 18: Transportation Claims Previously Returned But Not Resubmitted Transportation claims in this section were submitted to HMA and returned for correction or more information on a previous Warranty Statement and not resubmitted. This section contains claims returned within a 90 day period prior to the current statement. The transportation claims should be corrected, re-entered, and resubmitted through DCS for payment. Section 19: Claims With Outstanding Part(s) Not Yet Received This section lists claims with “Outstanding Parts Request Notices”. HMA’s policy is to chargeback warranty claims when a requested replaced part is not received by the Warranty Technical Center (WTC) within 20 days of the “Date Request”. Section 20: Statement Summary The last page of the report is a summary page. The totals shown on the summary page are factored as follows: 1. The total of claims listed in Section 1 of this report. 2. The total of Section 2 + Section 3 of this report. 3. The total of claims in Section 4 + Section 5. 4. The total of claims in Section 6. 5. The total of claims in Section 7. 6. The total of claims in Section 8. 7. The total of claims in Section 9 8. The total of claims in Section 10 + Section 11 + Section 12 + Section 13 + Section 14 + Section 15 + Section 16. 9. The total of claims in Section 17. 10. The total of claims in Section 18. 11. The total of claims in Section 1 + Section 2 + Section 3 + Section 4 + Section 5 + Section 10 + Section 11 + Section 12 + Section 13 + Section 14. Record Retention & Administration Requirements 162 Record Retention & Administration Requirements 163

title: PDI Settlement Statement Details
raw_content: The Settlement Statement indicates all claims paid, credit adjusted, and chargeback adjusted since the last statement. This statement is sorted by claim number and contains the following information for each claim: • Claim number • Repair order number • Vehicle identification number • Total amount charged back • Total amount claimed • Date approved (payment date) The last page of the PDI Settlement Statement is a summary of the statement totals that includes: • Total amount charged back • Total number charged back • Total amount claimed • Total number credited • Net amount approved • Total number processed Parts Retention Requirements 164 Parts Retention Requirements
coverage_details: Settlement Statement indicates all claims paid, credit adjusted, and chargeback adjusted since the last statement
Statement is sorted by claim number
Information for each claim: Claim number, Repair order number, Vehicle identification number, Total amount charged back, Total amount claimed, Date approved (payment date)

Last page of the PDI Settlement Statement is a summary including: Total amount charged back, Total number charged back, Total amount claimed, Total number credited, Net amount approved, Total number processed

title: Parts Retention and Core Return Policy
raw_content: Parts replaced under HMA Warranty coverages, HMA Customer Satisfaction Funds (Goodwill Claims), Campaign/Recall, or Foreign Vehicle must be retained for 20 calendar days from the date of claim payment. In cases where claims are returned for additional information or correction, the Dealer must continue to retain these replaced parts until 20 days have elapsed from the date of claim payment. Exception: All 12V AGM batteries replaced under warranty must be tagged and held for 10 days from the date of claim payment. See TSB# 24-EE-009H for more details . Please note that the TSB listed is subject to change; for the most up-to-date version, please visit www.HyundaiDealer.com under the Service Tab/Service Programs/Hyundai Tech Info/TSB. Note: The claim payment date is indicated as the Acceptance Date on the DCS answerback (Claim Status Report) and on the Warranty Settlement Statement as the “Date Approved”. Core Policy Reman engines and transmission Assemblies will have a core charge that is a separate charge from the dealer net part price. Credit for the core value is issued on the dealership’s monthly parts statement. All core assemblies, except radios and navigation units, are to be returned unless otherwise requested by the National Warranty Technical Center (WTC) as outlined in section 7.2. MOBIS Parts America’s Dedicated Delivery Service carriers (MPA DDS) will arrange for pickup and delivery to the core return center where the core will be inspected, processed, and your credit authorized and electronically transmitted to HMA. Dealers are not responsible for paying freight on these core returns. Cores must be: 1. Fully Assembled 2. With a completed Warranty Parts Tag 3. Completely drained of all fluids (Automatic Transmission, Engine) 4. With a completed Core Return form 5. With a completed Transmission or Engine Diagnosis Worksheet 6. With a copy of the Repair Order (Repair Order must include detailed Condition, Cause and Correction information) or Final Invoice when over-the-counter sale 7. With all plastic/rubber plugs and straps Parts Retention Requirements 165 Go to www.HyundaiDealer.com, click on WebDCS, Parts tab, Core Returns to enter core returns and arrange for core pickup. For proper core and return credit, ALL information entered within the Core Returns portal needs to be accurate and pertaining to the Core being returned. If you have any problems using the website, contact your Hyundai Parts Dealer Support Group at 1-800-545-4515 or MOBIS Parts Portal. Parts Retention Requirements 166

title: Parts Retention Requirements
raw_content: The following procedures are required for all Warranty, Customer Satisfaction (Goodwill), or Campaign/Recall replacement parts retained: • All parts replaced in performance of a Warranty repair, a Customer Satisfaction repair, or Campaign/ Recall are to be given to the Dealer’s Parts Department at such time as the Parts Department issues the replacement part to the technician. • Dealership Parts department personnel must note receipt of the replaced Warranty, Customer Satisfaction, or Campaign/Recall part(s) on the technician’s shop hard copy of the repair order at the time the Dealer’s Parts Department receives the part and issues the replacement part (this function can be performed electronically in a fully computerized system). This notification must be accomplished by a signature or initials on the repair order hard copy by the parts counter person, or by the stamping of such words such as “Parts Received” by use of a stamp on the hard copy of the repair order (digital stamp from DMS is acceptable). • Prior to storage, each part should be externally cleaned and repackaged in the container in which the new replacement part was received. • All parts containing lubricant or fluid must be drained thoroughly and wiped clean. • Cap or plug parts according to instructions in parts bulletin or service bulletin, if applicable. • Dealership Parts Department personnel must then complete a Warranty Parts Tag. The completed tag is to be attached to the replaced part(s). • After the replaced part(s) is tagged, these parts should be temporarily stored in a secured area until it is feasible to transfer them to the Warranty Parts Retention area. This task should be performed at least weekly–more frequently in large volume dealerships. • Dealership Parts Department personnel must place the replaced part(s) in a designated and secured warranty parts storage area (a 10 bin system). Refer to Section 7.4. Note: If the replaced part(s) contain more than one piece, all parts must be kept together within the Warranty Parts storage area in a box, bag (preferably a clear plastic bag), or wired together. • Warranty, Customer Satisfaction, and Campaign/Recall parts must be retained for 20 days from the date of claim payment. Warranty and Customer Satisfaction parts may be requested at any time within this 20 day period by HMA’s Warranty Technical Center, the DPSM, Field Engineer, or other authorized HMA personnel. The DPSM, Field Engineer, or any other authorized HMA staff person may inspect these replaced parts at any time without notification. Therefore, these parts must be available at all times during this 20 day period. Only the DPSM, Field Engineer, authorized Regional personnel, or HMA National Warranty personnel may dispose of these parts prior to the 20 day retention period. If the warranty parts Parts Retention Requirements 167 are not requested or disposed of by HMA personnel within the 20 day retention period, the Dealer is to destroy and scrap the warranty parts after 20 days. Exception: AGM Battery must be retained for 10 days according to TSB# 24-EE-009H . Please note that the TSB listed above is subject to change; for the most up-to-date version, please visit www.HyundaiDealer.com under the Service Tab/Service Programs/Hyundai Tech Info/TSB. • Warranty, Customer Satisfaction, and Campaign/Recall parts must not be installed on any vehicle, sold at retail, sold for remanufacturing/rebuilding or used in any other application. • In the event that inspection of the warranty parts by HMA reveals that the parts are not defective, not defective as stated in the claim, defective due to improper installation, or removal, or the parts are not available for inspection, a chargeback will be issued. Parts Retention Requirements 168
exclusions: Retained parts must not be installed on any vehicle.

A chargeback will be issued if HMA inspection reveals parts are defective due to improper installation or removal.
A chargeback will be issued if parts are not available for HMA inspection.

title: Warranty Parts Return and Retention Policy
raw_content: Notification of Warranty, Customer Satisfaction (Goodwill), or Campaign/Recall parts to be returned to HMA’s Warranty Technical Center is performed by the following method: • As part of HMA’s Quality Analysis and claim validation program, Dealers will receive from a Warranty Part Return Notice from the HMA National Warranty Technical Center (WTC). These requests for parts will have various return locations. Refer to the destination within the Warranty Part Return Portal (WebDCS) for proper Ship-To address. The Warranty Parts Return Notices and corresponding Parts Return Labels are issued on a daily basis. The Parts Return Labels issued are to be affixed to the back side of the applicable Warranty Parts Tags, and the requested parts with the attached Parts Tags are to be returned to the respective WTC location. Please pay special attention to the notice and label to ensure that parts are grouped and packaged correctly. Dealers will also receive an automated email notification of their “WTC Part Return Status”. Note: Dealers must enter their email address when completing the “Ship from” dealer information on your initial use of the Web Based WTC Part Return System (See Section 7.6) All parts listed on the WTC Parts Return Notice must be returned. The Warranty Settlement Statement, which is issued twice a month, also contains an “outstanding part(s) not yet received” section. This section shows requested parts that have not yet been returned to HMA’s WTC. Occasionally, HMA personnel may inspect, retrieve or dispose of a part prior to the end of the 20 day parts retention period. In such cases, if a parts return notice appears for a part that was previously disposed of by HMA personnel, the Dealer must furnish the WTC with the Warranty Parts Receipt, signed by the DPSM, RWM, FSE, or other authorized individual. The Dealer should retain a photo copy of receipt that has been returned to the WTC. • Replaced Parts or HMA processed signed Warranty Parts Receipt must be received by the WTC within 20 days from issuance of the Warranty Parts Return Notice. If not received, chargebacks will be issued on an automatic basis and will occur approximately 20 days after the issuance of the first request for the replaced part(s). • Parts replaced under warranty coverages, HMA customer satisfaction (Goodwill) funds, or Campaign/ Recall must be retained for 20 days from the date of claim payment approval. In cases where claims are returned for additional clarification, the Dealer must continue to retain these parts until 20 days have elapsed from the date of claim payment. If the parts are not requested by HMA within the 20 day retention period, the Dealer is to destroy and scrap the parts. * Note: AGM Battery must be retained for 10 days according to TSB# 24-EE-009H . WTC must also receive battery within 10 days of the initial request for parts. Please note that Parts Retention Requirements 169 the TSB listed above is subject to change; for the most up-to-date version, please visit www.HyundaiDealer.com under the Service Tab/Service Programs/Hyundai Tech Info/TSB. Note: Your Warranty Settlement Statements include a Parts Scrap Report (HMA-4330). The Parts Scrap Report includes all parts that were approved over 20 days prior to Settlement Statement date and that now may be scrapped. Parts Retention Requirements 170

title: Warranty Parts Tag Completion Requirements
raw_content: The Warranty Parts Tag (P/N NP445-00011) must be completed and attached to the replaced Warranty, Customer Satisfaction, or Campaign/Recall part upon receipt of the replaced parts by the Dealership’s Parts Department personnel from the technician. The following information must be entered on the tag: • Dealer Code (required) • Repair Order Number (required) • Claim Number (optional) • Date of Repair (required) • VIN (required) • Mileage (required) • Part Number (required) • Reason Replaced? (required) By filling out Dealer Code, repair order and part number, the Dealer can quickly locate a part requested by the WTC. Parts Retention Requirements 171

Enter Reason Replaced? on the tag (required)

title: Parts Retention Requirements
raw_content: HMA requires that the Dealer utilize a 10-Bin Warranty parts retention system. In the 10-Bin system, each of the 10 bins is numbered from zero to nine and corresponds to the last digit of the repair order number. There are 10 separate bins, each of which is large enough to contain the volume of Warranty, Customer Satisfaction, and Campaign/Recall replaced parts required. It is imperative that a Dealer employee (preferably a Dealer Parts Department employee) be given the responsibility for maintenance and security of the Warranty parts retention area. Twenty (20) days after the Dealer is notified of claim payment, the related parts are to be removed from the Warranty parts retention area, destroyed and scrapped, provided these parts are not requested to be returned by HMA. Determination of which parts are eligible for the over twenty (20) day scrap, can be accomplished by using the Parts Scrap Report (HMA-4330) section of your Warranty Settlement Statements or the acceptance date on the DCS claim answerback (claim status report). All 12V AGM batteries replaced under warranty must be tagged and held for 10 days from the date of claim payment. Please refer to TSB# 24-EE-009H* for 10-day AGM battery retention . Regular security and maintenance of the warranty parts retention area assures that only those parts subject to retention, inspection, and possible return are in the Warranty parts retention area. *Please note: The TSB listed above is subject to change; for the most updated version, please visit www.HyundaiDealer.com under Service tab/Service Programs/Hyundai Tech Info/TSB. Parts Retention Requirements 172
coverage_details: Warranty, Customer Satisfaction, and Campaign/Recall replaced parts are subject to this retention policy.
dealer_actions: Utilize a 10-Bin Warranty parts retention system.
Ensure each of the 10 bins is numbered from zero to nine, corresponding to the last digit of the repair order number.
Ensure each bin is large enough to contain the required volume of Warranty, Customer Satisfaction, and Campaign/Recall replaced parts.
Assign a Dealer employee (preferably from the Parts Department) responsibility for the maintenance and security of the Warranty parts retention area.
Twenty (20) days after notification of claim payment, remove, destroy, and scrap related parts from the retention area, provided HMA has not requested their return.
Determine which parts are eligible for over twenty (20) day scrap using the Parts Scrap Report (HMA-4330) section of Warranty Settlement Statements or the acceptance date on the DCS claim answerback.
Tag and hold all 12V AGM batteries replaced under warranty for 10 days from the date of claim payment.
Refer to TSB# 24-EE-009H* for 10-day AGM battery retention details.
Perform regular security and maintenance of the warranty parts retention area.
For the most updated TSB versions, visit www.HyundaiDealer.com under Service tab/Service Programs/Hyundai Tech Info/TSB.

title: Parts Return Policy for Warranty, Customer Satisfaction, and Campaign/Recall Claims
raw_content: When replaced Warranty, Customer Satisfaction (Goodwill), and Campaign/Recall parts are requested to be returned to HMA’s Warranty Technical Center, the following procedures must be followed: PLEASE RETURN PARTS WITHIN 48 HOURS OF REQUEST DATE. • Each Warranty, Customer Satisfaction, or Campaign/Recall part returned must be tagged with an accurately completed Warranty Parts Tag. • Affix the DCS Warranty Parts Request Return label to the back of the Warranty Parts Tag. • Requested Warranty, Customer Satisfaction, or Campaign/Recall parts must be returned to HMA in the original packaging of the replacement part. Parts, such as air conditioning parts which are susceptible to moisture contamination or damage to thread fittings, must be properly capped. Caps removed from the new replacement part can be used to cap the return part. • Use only one (1) box for returning parts for the same claim number since not all shipments are received on the same day. Note: All parts replaced in performance of a Warranty, Customer Satisfaction, or Campaign/ Recall repair must be returned when one or more parts are requested as per the claim. All parts associated with the repair claimed must be returned in the same box. • Should a part not be available for return (for example, a wheel cover fell off), the dealer is required to write a note on the part request, include a copy of the repair order, and mail them to the requesting location. The WTC will update the record and avoid a chargeback being issued to the dealer. • Parts that are scrapped in error, misplaced, lost, or stolen are not acceptable reasons for a part not being returned to the WTC and may result in the claim being subject to chargeback. • Include a copy of the DCS Warranty Parts Return Notice(s), including a check off in the column provided for each part contained in the box. Note: Retain a copy of each DCS Warranty Parts Return Notice, identifying the parts shipped, along with the FedEx tracking number. • A copy of your dealership’s repair order is required. A complete and legible copy of the repair order front and back that includes the customer complaint, cause comments and technician’s corrective action must be included with the required documentation and part(s). The repair order needs to arrive at the WTC in good condition and legible. Attach the repair order to the Warranty Parts Tag. Parts Retention Requirements 173 In cases that involve a special order part (SOP) a copy of the previous repair order will be required. • Shipments to the WTC must be made using FedEx Ground or FedEx Freight. Refer to Technical Center Shipping Policies. • Package each shipment separately by claim number. Do this by combining all parts related to a specific claim number into one individual package.

title: Engine / Transmission Part Return Requirements
raw_content: Part return requests related to Engine / Transmission: -Warranty Part Return Request (section 7.2) – WTC Part Inspection -Engine / Transmission Reman Program (section 5.6.4) – Core Return Must adhere to: A returned Engine main components must be re-assembled to hand-tight. All fluids must be completely drained prior to shipping. The engine core must be re-assembled. Shipping back with loose parts could possibly cause damage. Damage to the return core due to improper removal process or insufficient packaging may lead to chargeback of the core charge (if applicable) and warranty claim. • If you are returning a Short block, re-assemble all of the parts to match the remanufactured unit • If you are returning a Sub Assembly, re-assemble all of the parts to match the remanufactured unit Include the following completed forms with the returned engine. Failure to adhere to these requirements will result in the chargeback of the core credit (if applicable) and warranty claim. • Engine Diagnosis Worksheet (available in WebDCS) completely filled in with the engine serial number from the removed engine and the Service Manager’s signature • Dealer’s Repair Order must include detailed condition, cause and correction information • Warranty Parts Tag (part number NP445-00011). Sample in Section 7.3 If a warranty claim is submitted and the engine was not returned as requested by Warranty Parts Return Notice or in accordance with the Engine Reman Program, or if the returned engine core does not match the engine replaced per the Engine Diagnostic Worksheet (engine serial number), the warranty claim will be charged back to the dealer. Dealers will be billed the freight pickup & recovery costs for all invalid returned engine cores. Parts Retention Requirements 174

title: Warranty Part Return Policy and Chargeback Reasons
raw_content: Any Warranty, Customer Satisfaction, or Campaign/Recall part returned to the HMA Warranty Technical Center that does not comply with HMA policy and procedures may be subject to chargeback. The following are examples of reasons that could qualify return parts for chargeback: • Parts found not to be defective, or not defective as stated on claim. • Supporting documentation missing. • Parts found to be repairable or a repairable assembly. • Parts damaged due to the use of improper tools, improper repair procedures or outside influence. • Parts damaged in transit to HMA’s Warranty Technical Center due to improper packaging. • Wrong parts received compared to those requested and stated as replaced on the Warranty claim. • Assembly received in disassembled or incomplete condition. • Warranty Part Tag(s) received without the parts attached or other indication of HMA removal/scrapping. • Parts found to be non-Genuine Hyundai parts or non-approved HMA parts or parts claimed as HMA parts which are not HMA parts. • Parts received without a Warranty Parts Tag, Parts Return Notice, or Parts Return Label. • Parts received without a required Repair Order (check the Parts Return Label). • Parts stated as replaced on the claim which are not received. • Parts received beyond 20 days from DCS request to return. Note: Parts received beyond the deadline and electronic media documentation not submitted per TSB instructions may not result in the recredit of the charged back claim. • Parts not received. • Parts replaced due to TSB/Campaign repair procedure not followed. * Note: ALL 12V Batteries must be retained for 10 days after each battery claim approval per TSB# 24-EE-009H . WTC must also receive batteries within 10 days after the initial request for parts. Please note that the TSB listed above is subject to change; for the most up-to-date version, please visit www.HyundaiDealer.com under the Service Tab/Service Programs/Hyundai Tech Info/TSB. HMA reserves the right to make the final decision as to the defectiveness of the part. Rejected returned parts will be held for 30 days from the date of chargeback pending any Dealer’s request for return. To request the return of a rejected part, contact your DPSM or the Regional Warranty Manager. Parts Retention Requirements 176 Rejected parts that are requested by the Dealership for return will be shipped back at dealer’s expense. If you have information that reflects shipment of the part to HMA, please follow the procedure identified in Technical Center Appeals. Parts Retention Requirements 177

title: WTC Chargeback Appeal Process
raw_content: The chargeback appeal process has been automated. Appealing WTC chargebacks can be completed online at www.HyundaiDealer.com. The electronic Debit Appeal forms can be accessed on either the PARTS or SERVICE page. Under the PARTS tab, select ‘Information’ and then click on ‘WTC Claim Debit Appeal’. Under the SERVICE tab click on ‘WTC Claim Debit Appeal’. Appeals must be submitted within 30 days from the date of the WTC chargeback. A detailed Step-By-Step Instruction manual is posted on the Hyundai Dealer Website. To access the manual: 1. Go to www.HyundaiDealer.com 2. Hover over the Resources Tab and click on “Documents Library”. 3. Scroll down to “Warranty” and click on the arrow to expand the index. 4. Click on “WTC Claim Debit Appeal User Guide”. Non-Receipt of Parts If parts are not received at the WTC within 20 days* of the initial request for parts from HMA request, your entire claim will be charged back. If your Dealership records confirm delivery of the part within 20 days, you may wish to appeal such a chargeback. * Note: ALL 12V Batteries must be retained for 10 days after each battery claim approval per TSB# 24-EE-009H . WTC must also receive batteries within 10 days after the initial request for parts. Please note that the TSB listed above is subject to change; for the most up-to-date version, please visit www.HyundaiDealer.com under the Service Tab/Service Programs/Hyundai Tech Info/TSB. Parts Determined to be Damaged or Not Defective (NTF) Claim chargebacks may result from parts received damaged, broken or missing components. Also, parts may be found to be “Not Defective as Claimed” or “No Trouble Found” (NTF) as a result of testing and technical evaluation at the WTC. To contest such a finding, adhere to the following procedures: • Document your appeal on the electronic WTC Dealer Debit Appeal form, including a detailed explanation of the appeal. • Attach any pertinent resource(s), such as a copy of your repair order to indicate customer comments and/or technician comments that relate to your appeal. Section 8 Service Campaign and Recall Procedures 178 Section 8: Service Campaign and Recall Procedures

title: Service Campaign and Recall Procedures
raw_content: When appropriate, Hyundai Motor America (HMA) may conduct Service Campaigns or Recalls to address any safety, emissions or customer satisfaction issues or conditions. Dealers will perform an inspection and/or correction for all Service Campaigns and Recalls conducted by HMA in accordance with the Service Campaign and Recall Notification materials as outlined in this manual. In the event of a Service Campaign or Recall to correct a safety-related condition or noncompliance with a Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standard or an Emissions-related condition, all inspections and/or corrections will be provided on a “No Charge” basis to owners. Because of the importance to customer safety and satisfaction, all affected vehicles are to be inspected and/or corrected at the earliest possible opportunity. HMA will announce each Service Campaign or Recall directly to the Dealer. Service Campaign and Recall Notification materials (Technical Service Bulletins, VIN Listings, Owner Letters, and Dealer DCS Messages) will be made available to all dealers. Vehicles Involved Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) ranges or production date ranges and other specific data regarding body style, options, or accessories, where applicable. A select model year(s) and model(s) of certain Hyundai Vehicles according to the VIN listing to address the particular issue(s) or condition(s) of the Service Campaign or Recall. Parts Part numbers or replacement parts and instructions on shipment and invoicing. Reimbursement information on the applicable labor operation(s), time allowance(s), part number(s), part prices, and other information as required. At the onset of a Service Campaign or Recall, MOBIS Parts America may ship a quantity of parts that HMA estimates will meet the Dealer’s requirements. However, HMA may also elect to rely on the Dealer’s use of normal parts ordering procedures. Service Campaign / Recall Completion The responsibility for completion of a Service Campaign or Recall is that of the selling dealer. However, all authorized Hyundai Motor America Dealers are also responsible for completing Service Campaigns or Recalls for visiting owners/lessees as well. Each Dealer should review its sales records to determine if any vehicles have been transferred to other Dealers. They should also review their stock list for vehicles that may have been sold or traded subsequent to the date of issuance of the Service Campaign or Recall Notification. Section 8 Service Campaign and Recall Procedures 179 Dealers must perform recall procedures on any affected stock vehicle (new and used) before it is delivered to the customer. United States Code Title 49, Subtitle VI, Part A, Chapter 301, Sub- Chapter II, Section 30120 prohibits retail sale of affected vehicles prior to correction. A civil penalty not to exceed $21,000.00 may be imposed for each violation. The maximum penalty for a series of violations is $105,000,000. In the event that a notice is received for a vehicle that is no longer in Dealer stock and the Dealer is unaware of the location of the vehicle, the Dealer must immediately notify HMA accordingly.
coverage_details: Hyundai Motor America (HMA) may conduct Service Campaigns or Recalls to address safety, emissions, or customer satisfaction issues.
Inspections and corrections for safety-related conditions or noncompliance with Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standards will be provided on a "No Charge" basis to owners.
Inspections and corrections for emissions-related conditions will be provided on a "No Charge" basis to owners.
Coverage applies to select model year(s) and model(s) of certain Hyundai Vehicles according to VIN listing.
Reimbursement information includes applicable labor operation(s), time allowance(s), part number(s), part prices, and other required information.
exclusions: None explicitly stated regarding what is not covered for eligible campaigns or recalls.

The selling dealer is responsible for completion of a Service Campaign or Recall.

Follow HMA's instructions regarding parts acquisition, either via initial shipment from MOBIS Parts America or normal parts ordering procedures.

title: Customer and Dealer Responsibilities for Service Campaigns and Recalls
raw_content: Notification letters will be sent by “First Class” mail from HMA to these customers including a letter of explanation and instructions to the customer to have their car inspected and/or corrected. If, during routine servicing, a vehicle is found to be in need of modification, the customer should be asked for permission to perform the correction. Dealers should take extra care in properly completing the RDR form prior to submission. If an owner moves, the Dealer can submit a change of address using the RDR CHANGE option on their DCS system or have the owner complete and mail the Owner Name and Address card enclosed with their Owner’s Handbook. Visiting owners may come to the Dealer in need of work under a Service Campaign or Recall. These owners, like all others, are entitled to prompt and courteous service.

title: WebDCS and Campaign/Recall Procedures
raw_content: WebDCS contains Information screens to include: • Warranty Vehicle Information Screen Lists open Service Campaign(s) or Recall(s) that have not been performed on each vehicle, including vehicles reclassified as non-warrantable. WebDCS will now reflect the date the VIN effectively started being open for the campaign. All outstanding Service Campaign or Recall procedures are to be performed on all vehicles, including vehicles that have been reclassified as non-warrantable. Refer to Section 5 for instructions on how to access the WebDCS System. • Uncompleted Campaign VIN List - Retailed Provides a list of open Service Campaigns or Recalls, as well as a search function by model/model year, or a 3-digit Campaign number (example: TC1). Section 8 Service Campaign and Recall Procedures 180 • Uncompleted Campaign VIN List - Dealer Stock (New and Used) Allows a search by VIN or model/model year, with the ability to export to Microsoft Excel or print as a PDF. Note: Dealers must review a vehicle’s Warranty Vehicle Information Screen on every vehicle that enters its service area to ensure identification of currently open Service Campaigns or Recalls for the vehicle. Dealers must also review their uncompleted Campaign VIN list for dealer stock and complete the open campaign(s)/recall(s) as soon as possible. A printout of the Vehicle’s Warranty Vehicle Information Screen must be attached to each repair order. NOTE: If customer elects to postpone having open campaign/recall performed, open line on repair order with customer’s reason(s) and have customer sign.

Performance of a previously completed Recall again, at no expense to the customer, even if the vehicle is out of warranty, provided a Prior Approval Request for goodwill consideration is submitted and approved.
dealer_actions: Refer to Service Campaign Notification or Recall materials (Technical Service Bulletins) for specific claim preparation requirements.

Submit a Prior Approval Request for goodwill consideration prior to performing work if a previously completed Recall needs to be performed again and the vehicle is out of warranty, to ensure the Recall is performed at no expense to the customer.
Consult section 5.4.1 for Prior Approval Requirements.

Resources 182 Battery Prorate Chart REPLACEMENT BATTERY PRORATION CHART FOR 2016 - 2026 MODEL YEAR VEHICLES ONLY Months After Installation or Retail Purchase (up to) % that Hyundai reimburses Dealer based on Dealer net cost of battery % that Customer is responsible for based on suggested price of battery 01-12 100 0 13 73 27 14 71 29 15 69 31 16 67 33 17 65 35 18 63 37 19 60 40 20 58 42 21 56 44 22 54 46 23 52 48 24 50 50 25 48 52 26 46 54 27 44 56 28 42 58 29 40 60 30 37 63 31 35 65 32 33 67 33 31 69 34 29 71 35 27 73 36 25 75 37 23 77 38 21 79 39 19 81 40 17 83 41 15 85 42 13 87 43 11 89 44 8 92 45 6 94 46 4 96 47 2 98 48 0 100
coverage_details: Prorated reimbursement for replacement batteries on 2016-2026 model year vehicles.
Hyundai reimburses the dealer 100% for batteries installed 1-12 months prior.
Reimbursement percentage decreases progressively from 73% at 13 months to 2% at 47 months after installation or retail purchase.
Limited Warranty coverage for Replacement Parts and Accessories (SVC-1144).
10 Year/100,000 Miles Original Owner Warranty details (refer to SVC-1248 and SVC-1247).
Foreign Vehicle Warranty Coverage as per guide available online.
exclusions: Replacement battery coverage does not apply to vehicles outside the 2016-2026 model year range.
No Hyundai reimbursement for replacement batteries 48 months or older after installation or retail purchase (100% customer responsibility).
Specific exclusions may be detailed in the Warranty Claims Processing Manual, Warranty Coverage Guide, and Warranty Policy & Procedures Manual.
dealer_actions: Access current warranty publications and forms at www.HyundaiDealer.com (Service tab/Dealer Resources/Documents Library/Warranty).
Utilize the 10 Year/100,000 Miles Original Owner Affidavit Sheet (SVC-1248) and Verification Placard (SVC-1247).
Check the Current NPN Chemical List and Current Warranty Settlement Dates online.
Complete and submit the Anti-Perforation Vehicle Inspection Report (NP430-00000) when applicable.
Refer to the DMV - CA Non Disclosure Statement, DWPI Reference Material, Equus Service Advocate QRG 2014, and Foreign Vehicle Warranty Coverage Guide and Contact Info online.
Complete and fax the Fuel Reimbursement Program Dealer Enrollment Fax Form (DD-1073).
Check online for No Trouble Found (NTF) Acceptance Labor Operations OP Codes.
Use the Reman ATM Core Return Checklist and Diagnosis Worksheet (SVC-1358).
Submit the Request For Labor Time Review Form (NP448-21030) online for labor time reviews.
Consult the Test and Measurement Documentation Chart online.
Use appropriate VIN Decoders: CAD-0695 for 2000-2009, SVC-1222 for 2010-2019, and SVC-1222 for 2020-forward.
Refer to the Warranty Claims Processing Manual, Warranty Coverage Guide (NP448-00010), and Warranty Policy & Procedures Manual (SVC-1245).
Utilize the Warranty Labor Establishment Checklist (SVC-1057), Warranty Labor Rate Request (SVC-1371), and Warranty Labor Rate Survey (SVC-1370).
Use the Warranty Parts Tag (NP445-00011).
Submit the WTC Debit Appeal Request Form (NP445-00221) online and refer to the WTC Debit Claim Appeal Dealer User Guide and WTC Web Part Return System - Starter Guide online.
Order certain items from PDC/HMA personnel and contact the DPSM for more information.
Access Reman ATM and Engine Core Return and Diagnosis Forms via Hyundaidealer.com > Service / Documents Library / Service.
Calculate and claim replacement battery reimbursement from Hyundai based on the provided proration chart for eligible model years.
Communicate the customer's responsibility percentage for replacement batteries based on the suggested price, as outlined in the proration chart.
